## [Andy - Analysis, PC080SN tile preload system design]

* SGDK preload strategy identified exactly: YES — genesistan_preload_scene_tiles iterates (u16 arcade_tile, u16 vram_slot) manifest pairs, calls VDP_loadTileData(rastan_pc080sn + arcade_tile*32, vram_slot, 1, DMA) with no format conversion, scene-scoped at scene entry
* SGDK compatibility defined exactly: YES — raw pc080sn.bin bytes are VDP-native; no conversion required; confirmed by Build 91+ launcher tile browser screenshots
* tile ROM embedding design defined: YES — .incbin "../../build/regions/pc080sn.bin" in main_68k.s .rodata section, labeled genesistan_pc080sn_tile_rom; patcher does not touch above 0x070000; no Makefile changes needed
* tile conversion contract defined exactly: YES — no conversion; raw bytes copied directly to VRAM
* preload scope chosen exactly: YES — bulk preload all 1,067 unique tiles at init (before init_staging_state, between vdp_boot_setup and init_staging_state in main_68k); scene-scoped preload rejected as unnecessary complexity
* VRAM upload mechanism defined exactly: YES — function init_arcade_tile_vram; iterates all 16384 LUT entries; for each nonzero vram_slot: vdp_set_vram_write_addr(vram_slot << 5), write 16 words from genesistan_pc080sn_tile_rom + (i << 5)
* minimal first implementation defined exactly: YES — one .incbin directive + one function + one call site in main_68k; no other changes
* rainbow islands alignment: YES — all 5 checks pass; tile upload before nametable reference, no placeholder reliance, clear separation, no coupling, explicit VDP memory target
* divergence points identified: YES — previous diagnosis doc incorrectly stated planar-to-chunky conversion required; AGENTS_LOG Build 91+ evidence and pre-confirmed facts override this; no conversion needed
* single root cause identified: PC080SN tile pixel ROM never embedded in Genesis ROM and no upload function exists; all VRAM slots 20-1342 remain zero-initialized

## [Andy - Analysis, per-mode VRAM working-set profiling plan]

* mode buckets defined exactly: YES -- Title/Attract (scene 0, A0 range 0x5A7DA-0x5B0B2), Gameplay (scene 1, A0 range 0x56A22-0x570C2), End-Round (scene 2, A0 range 0x5822A-0x59614)
* PC080SN profiling target defined exactly: YES -- instrument genesistan_hook_tilemap_plane_a with 3x192-byte WRAM bitmask (genesistan_tile_slot_observed) recording which VRAM slots are written per scene bucket; count set bits per scene to get per-mode unique slot totals
* PC090OJ profiling target defined exactly: YES -- record all observed 16x16 cell codes in 512-byte WRAM bitmask (genesistan_sprite_cell_observed); maintain genesistan_sprite_peak_unique peak per-frame unique cell count
* profiling method defined exactly: YES -- two non-destructive bit-set additions to main_68k.s; one in hook_tilemap_plane_a after LUT lookup; one in sprite tile prepare path; capture via emulator WRAM dump after full attract + gameplay + end-round traversal
* VRAM budget decision rule defined exactly: YES -- PASS if S_peak_slots <= (1535 - 1342) = 193; at 18 unique cells: 72 <= 193 (PASS, margin 121 slots); at 32 cells: 128 <= 193 (PASS, margin 65 slots); FAIL threshold is 49+ unique cells simultaneously
* expected final direction chosen exactly: YES -- Option A: bulk preload all 1,067 PC080SN tiles at boot; condition: sprite tile base must be 1343 not 1024; budget passes at confirmed worst-case (18 cells = 72 slots needed, 193 available)
* Rainbow Islands alignment evaluated: YES -- Rainbow Islands used scene-scoped preload due to VRAM overflow (2,272 tiles vs 1,164 slots); rastan-direct has 1,067 tiles vs 1,535 slots; overflow condition does not exist; bulk preload is valid; scene-scoped not required
* single root concern identified: unmeasured per-mode PC080SN working set; bulk preload may permanently occupy VRAM no single mode uses simultaneously, constraining sprite streaming to 193 slots when larger reserves may be achievable with mode-scoped discipline
* single next correction defined: instrument genesistan_hook_tilemap_plane_a with 3x192-byte per-scene tile-slot bitmask and add peak-unique sprite cell counter in sprite prepare path; run attract + gameplay + end-round to capture data; verify VRAM budget math against actual runtime behavior before committing to final sprite base address
* what-must-not-be-changed-yet defined: YES -- hook_tilemap_plane_a logic (observational instrumentation only), tile_vram_lut, staged_bg_buffer/vdp_commit_bg_strips_if_dirty, _VINT_handler, vdp_commit_tiles_if_dirty, init_staging_state internals, all opcode_replace entries, VRAM_TILE_BASE, A5 init, patcher, Step 6 checkerboard removal must NOT happen yet

## [Andy - Analysis, mode-based PC080SN tile residency system]

* residency buckets defined exactly: YES — three buckets: Title/Attract (scene 0, 841 pairs, max slot 860), Gameplay (scene 1, 829 pairs, max slot 848), End-Round (scene 2, 1067 pairs, max slot 1342); source address ranges fully disjoint; confirmed from binary inspection of build/pc080sn_scene_preload_*.bin
* global preload rejection explained exactly: YES — 779 of 1067 occupied VRAM slots are aliased across scenes (different arcade tile indices per scene for the same slot); one-time global upload leaves wrong tile data per aliased slot for every scene that is not the one that was preloaded; this is a deterministic correctness failure on all scene transitions
* mode-based residency system defined exactly: YES — Option A: full reload on mode change; display disabled; iterate new scene manifest (u16 arcade_tile, u16 vram_slot) pairs; write 16 words raw from genesistan_pc080sn_tile_rom + (arcade_tile << 5) to VDP; re-enable display; no diff, no incremental, no LRU
* mode transition trigger defined exactly: YES — block-write hook compares A0 (PC080SN source address) against genesistan_scene_a0_lo/hi; on range miss: determine new scene_id by comparing A0 against three hardcoded (lo, hi) pairs; call load_scene_tiles(new_scene_id); update scene state
* upload timing / visibility rule defined exactly: YES — display disabled before first tile write; interrupts masked; all writes synchronous CPU (no DMA); display re-enabled after last tile write; brief black period during transition is acceptable
* VRAM partition defined exactly: YES — slot 0: transparent; slots 1-3: synthetic scaffolding tiles; slots 4-19: unused; slots 20-1342: PC080SN current scene tiles (mode-dependent); slots 1343-1535: reserved for PC090OJ sprites; N=1342 (largest manifest max slot)
* minimal first implementation defined exactly: YES — Title scene only; embed pc080sn.bin + three manifest files via .incbin in main_68k.s rodata; add 3 BSS WRAM fields; implement load_scene_tiles function in assembly; call load_scene_tiles(0) from main_68k between vdp_boot_setup and init_staging_state; single file change
* Rainbow Islands residency model identified exactly: YES — genesistan_preload_scene_tiles in apps/rastan/src/main.c: iterates (arcade_tile, vram_slot) manifest; VDP_loadTileData per pair; scene-change detection via genesistan_bulk_preload_check comparing source_addr against stored scene bounds; Title preloaded explicitly at boot before first arcade tick
* reusable parts identified exactly: YES — scene-scoped preload principle; manifest format; source address range detection; display-disable bracketing; per-scene (lo, hi) bounds table; genesistan_current_scene_id/scene_a0_lo/scene_a0_hi WRAM state
* non-reusable parts identified exactly: YES — VDP_loadTileData SGDK API; VDP_waitDMACompletion; genesistan_scene_manifest_for_id C switch; genesistan_scene_id_from_source_addr source-scene map binary scan; all SGDK types and DMA modes
* Rainbow-derived design rules defined exactly: YES — 8 rules: tile data before nametable reference; manifests are authority; full reload per transition; display off during upload; scene state in WRAM; scene detection by source address range; boot loads Title first; scaffolding slots 1-3 and PC080SN slots 20-1342 do not overlap
* single root cause identified: PC080SN tile VRAM LUT is scene-aliased (779 of 1067 occupied slots map to different arcade tile indices in different scenes), making one-time global preload a deterministic wrong-tile rendering failure on all scene transitions
* single next correction defined: implement load_scene_tiles in apps/rastan-direct/src/main_68k.s; embed pc080sn.bin + three manifest files via .incbin; add WRAM scene state; call load_scene_tiles(SCENE_TITLE=0) from main_68k between vdp_boot_setup and init_staging_state
* what-must-not-be-changed-yet defined: YES — checkerboard scaffolding; sprite system; genesistan_pc080sn_tile_vram_lut and .incbin; _VINT_handler structure; genesistan_hook_tilemap_plane_a translation logic; patcher; Makefile; all 34 opcode_replace entries; rom_absolute_call_relocation; A5 init; VRAM_TILE_BASE; WRAM profiling bitmask instrumentation must not be added
* no implementation performed
## [Andy - Analysis, tile reference correctness under mode residency]

* reference chain defined exactly: YES — 8-step chain from arcade descriptor write to VDP pixel render; hook extracts 14-bit tile index, LUT maps to vram_slot, nametable word committed via staged_bg_buffer, VDP renders from vram_slot*32
* LUT semantic meaning defined exactly: YES — globally consistent scene-aware slot assignment: lut[T]=S invariant across all scenes containing T; slot pixel data changes per scene for aliased slots (779 aliased), slot assignment does not
* LUT/manifest consistency validated exactly: YES — programmatic: all 841 title pairs, all 829 gameplay pairs, all 1067 endround pairs match LUT; 2326 nonzero LUT entries all in at least one manifest; 0 inconsistencies; each manifest is a bijection on slots
* single-scene residency correctness proven: YES — correctness condition holds for all three scenes: every tile in scene X's tile set is in scene X's manifest; every manifest entry matches the LUT; each manifest is bijective; 349 cross-scene tiles use same slot in all scenes; 0 tiles with different slots across scenes
* additional remap layer requirement defined exactly: YES — NO additional remap layer required; LUT is globally consistent; no arcade tile index maps to different slots in different scenes; single LUT + per-scene manifests is sufficient
* SGDK failure mode explained exactly: YES — Build 293/294: adda.w #0x0014,%a4 caused BG+FG assembly commit functions to read tile codes from WRAM shadow region (A2+0x14+D7) instead of hardware tile region (A2+0+D7); LUT updated in same wrong direction; both consistently wrong together (wrong-tiles not blank-tiles); fixed in Build 295 by deleting +0x14 displacement and reverting Python tile-address formula
* recurrence risk in current design determined exactly: YES — ZERO recurrence risk; genesistan_hook_tilemap_plane_a reads tile code at offset 0 from strip table pointer (move.w (%a4),%d3) matching the A2+0 correct arcade formula; no WRAM-shadow region ambiguity; +0x14 displacement is not present and has no path to be introduced
* ROM-resident lookup design validated: YES — LUT already .incbin in .rodata (ROM); scene manifests to be .incbin in .rodata (ROM); only WRAM needed: genesistan_current_scene_id (1 byte) + genesistan_scene_a0_lo/hi (8 bytes) = 9 bytes total; no runtime writes to LUT or manifests ever required
* final lookup/residency contract defined exactly: YES — precondition: load_scene_tiles(X) called with display off before first nametable commit of scene X; invariant: lut[T]=S nonzero for every T referenced by X; VRAM[S*32] holds pixel data for T; WRAM: 3 variables gate scene-change detection; ROM: LUT and manifests read-only
* Rainbow Islands reference-correctness model identified exactly: YES — single global LUT with cross-scene slot reuse (greedy coloring) + per-scene manifests + scene-scoped preload at scene entry; NOT per-scene LUTs; same model as rastan implementation; forced by VRAM overflow in RI SGDK port; forced by slot-aliasing correctness in rastan-direct; both arrive at identical architecture
* single root risk identified: scene-transition preload trigger not yet wired to genesistan_hook_tilemap_plane_a; load_scene_tiles(SCENE_TITLE) is called at boot but hook does not yet call load_scene_tiles for Gameplay or End-Round transitions; aliased slots retain Title pixel data on scene change
* single next correction defined: wire load_scene_tiles to genesistan_hook_tilemap_plane_a — add A0 range check before descriptor loop; compare A0 against stored lo/hi bounds; if out of range, determine new scene ID from hardcoded bounds table, call load_scene_tiles(new_scene_id), update WRAM scene state
* what-must-not-be-changed-yet defined: YES — genesistan_pc080sn_tile_vram_lut and .incbin; all three scene manifest binaries; genesistan_hook_tilemap_plane_a translation logic (descriptor loop unchanged, only scene-detection preamble added); checkerboard scaffolding; sprite system; _VINT_handler; patcher; Makefile; 34 opcode_replace entries; rom_absolute_call_relocation; A5 init; VRAM_TILE_BASE; precompute_pc080sn_tile_lut.py
* no implementation performed

## [Andy - Analysis, scene/mode transition trigger spec]

* trigger location defined exactly: YES — after dest-validation block, before line 222 (where A0 is overwritten with descriptor list base); specifically between `andi.w #0x001F, %d1` (line 221) and `lea ARCADE_PC080SN_DESC_BG_LIST_OFFSET(%a5), %a0` (line 222); A0 holds source address up to that point; A5 = 0xFF0000 already live; all registers saved
* A0 range model defined exactly: YES — Scene 0 Title: lo=0x0005A7DA hi=0x0005B0B2; Scene 1 Gameplay: lo=0x00056A22 hi=0x000570C2; Scene 2 End-Round: lo=0x0005822A hi=0x00059614; all inclusive; all disjoint; A0 masked to 24 bits before comparison; ROM-resident 3-entry table (genesistan_scene_a0_ranges, 24 bytes)
* fast-path condition defined exactly: YES — (A0 & 0x00FFFFFF) >= genesistan_scene_a0_lo AND (A0 & 0x00FFFFFF) <= genesistan_scene_a0_hi; two unsigned cmp.l + two branches; ~25 cycles; no reload; fall through to descriptor loop
* scene-change path defined exactly: YES — (1) scan genesistan_scene_a0_ranges 3 entries for matching range; (2) if found, call load_scene_tiles(matched_scene_id) immediately before descriptor loop; (3) load_scene_tiles encapsulates: SR=0x2700, display off, manifest iteration (16 words per tile, synchronous CPU writes), state update (scene_id then lo/hi), display on, SR=0x2000; (4) after return, fall through to descriptor loop; (5) unknown A0 falls through silently with no reload
* unknown-A0 behavior defined exactly: YES — Option A: leave current scene unchanged, continue hook; no state change; descriptor loop proceeds with current scene tiles; rationale: unknown addresses are transient; forced black frame is worse than potentially wrong tiles for one frame
* boot-time interaction defined exactly: YES — load_scene_tiles(0) called from main_68k between vdp_boot_setup and init_staging_state; sets current_scene_id=0, scene_a0_lo=0x5A7DA, scene_a0_hi=0x5B0B2; .bss zero-init alone is NOT safe (lo=0 would pass fast path vacuously for all A0, hi=0 would fail fast path for all A0 > 0); explicit call required; 0xFF sentinel approach not recommended
* display/interrupt contract defined exactly: YES — load_scene_tiles owns the contract; SR=0x2700 before first VDP write; VDP_MODE2_DISPLAY_OFF before tile writes; synchronous CPU move.w to VDP_DATA (no DMA, no wait); state update after last tile write; VDP_MODE2_DISPLAY_ON then SR=0x2000 on exit; hook does not manage display or SR directly
* re-entrancy / repeat-load safety defined exactly: YES — state update (lo/hi/id) happens inside load_scene_tiles BEFORE rts; next hook call takes fast path for new scene; no oscillation possible (disjoint ranges + deterministic arcade state machine); bounds update before return is the safety invariant
* Rainbow Islands trigger model identified exactly: YES — genesistan_bulk_preload_check (main.c line 1634): fast-path scene_id+bounds check, slow-path call to genesistan_preload_scene_tiles; genesistan_preload_scene_tiles (line 1592): manifest iteration via VDP_loadTileData, VDP_waitDMACompletion, state update (id/lo/hi) in that order; AGENTS_LOG amendment (line 24222): assembly range check replaced per-invocation C call on hot path; 50 cycles vs 300+ cycles; fires ~3-4 times per game on actual transitions only
* reusable trigger rules identified exactly: YES — fast-path lo/hi range check; slow-path fires only on change; state update order (id then lo then hi) inside preload before return; manifest format (u16 arcade_tile, u16 vram_slot, 0xFFFF sentinel); disjoint ranges enable O(1) detection; boot-time explicit preload; per-scene ROM-resident bounds table; display-off bracketing
* non-reusable implementation details identified exactly: YES — VDP_loadTileData SGDK API; VDP_waitDMACompletion; genesistan_scene_id_from_source_addr reading pc080sn_source_scene_map.bin; genesistan_scene_bounds_from_map; genesistan_scene_manifest_for_id C switch; C struct pointer arithmetic; text_writer_read_be16 helper; SGDK DMA modes
* final trigger contract defined exactly: YES — inputs, fast-path condition, slow-path sequence, state update order, post-reload continuation, unknown-A0 fallthrough, re-entrancy guarantee, boot interaction all fully specified in docs/design/Andy_scene_mode_transition_trigger_spec.md
* single root risk identified: WRAM state variables genesistan_current_scene_id / genesistan_scene_a0_lo / genesistan_scene_a0_hi are entirely absent from main_68k.s .bss; if omitted, cmp.l instructions reference arbitrary memory producing nondeterministic scene-change behavior on every hook call
* single next correction defined: Cody adds the three WRAM variables to .bss, adds genesistan_scene_a0_ranges to .rodata, adds state-update tail to load_scene_tiles, and inserts scene-detection preamble between lines 221 and 222 of genesistan_hook_tilemap_plane_a — all in apps/rastan-direct/src/main_68k.s only
* what-must-not-be-changed-yet defined: YES — descriptor loop (lines 229-315); LUT and .incbin; three manifest binaries in build/; init_staging_state; synthetic tiles slots 1-3; _VINT_handler; sprite system; patcher; Makefile; 34 opcode_replace entries; rom_absolute_call_relocation; A5 init; VRAM_TILE_BASE; precompute_pc080sn_tile_lut.py; pc080sn_source_scene_map.bin
* no implementation performed

## [Andy - Analysis, title hook failure diagnosis]

* crash root cause identified: YES — arcade PC090OJ sprite RAM init calls longword block-fill (FUNC B) with count large enough to advance A0 from 0xD00000 past 0xD03FFF into unmapped Genesis expansion space; write to 0xDFFFFE triggers BlastEm machine freeze; pre-existing issue, separate from BG hook
* DFFFFE write source identified: YES — sprite RAM block-fill primitive at arcade 0x03AD44 with non-BG A0 target; not related to BG hook
* 0x03AD48 classification complete: YES — BNE.S loop-back branch at byte +4 of FUNC B; NOT a function entry point; patching here would break loop contract
* MAME Lua tap timing root cause identified: YES — install_write_tap fires AFTER instruction completes; PC observed in callback = instruction following the write; MOVE.L at 0x03AD44 (2 bytes) + SUBQ.W at 0x03AD46 (2 bytes) = PC advanced to 0x03AD48 (BNE.S) at callback time; off-by-4 from actual write instruction
* correct hook insertion point identified: YES — arcade PC 0x03AD44 (Genesis ROM 0x03AF44); function entry; 8-byte span (MOVE.L + SUBQ.W + BNE.S + RTS); original bytes confirmed: 20 C0 53 41 66 FA 4E 75
* hook contract compatibility verified: YES — existing genesistan_hook_tilemap_plane_a uses WRAM desc-list protocol; FUNC B uses register convention (D0=tile data, A0=dest, D1=count); fundamentally incompatible; new hook genesistan_hook_tilemap_bg_fill required
* single root cause defined: YES — MAME Lua write tap reports PC 4 bytes past write instruction; caused 0x03AD48 (BNE.S) to be identified as patch target instead of 0x03AD44 (MOVE.L); boundary validation correctly rejected 0x03AF48
* existing 0x055968 patch disposition: KEEP UNCHANGED — correct for gameplay BG strip writes; zero hits in Title is expected
* design doc: docs/design/Andy_title_hook_failure_analysis.md
* no implementation performed

## [Andy - Analysis, BG fill hook no-visible-output diagnosis]

## [Andy - Analysis, BG fill hook post-RTS no-change diagnosis]

* RTS contract correct: YES — 4e75 at position +6 restores call/return discipline; no stack corruption
* hook logic should produce Plane B updates if called with valid BG data: YES — precompute + fill loop + dirty bit + vblank flush all verified correct
* FUNC B BG-window callers inventoried: YES — only two callers pass range check: 0x03AE70 (A0=0xC00100, D0=0x20, D1=1900) and 0x03AF38 (A0=0xC00000, D0=0x20, D1=4096); both use tile code 32 (blank/clear tile, all-zero pixels)
* Title BG tilemap write path identified: YES — block copy function at 0x05A4DC called via BSR.W $0154 from 0x05A388; reads per-cell tile codes from ROM table (0x5A7DA: 28×21 title_alt, dominant tile 0xAD); writes to [0xC00000, 0xC04000) — NOT FUNC B
* hook classification: C — hook called and correctly implemented; FUNC B is not the function that writes Title BG content
* root cause: Title BG tilemap written by block copy function 0x05A4DC (not FUNC B); writes go directly to Genesis VDP data port at 0xC00000; bypass staged_bg_buffer entirely; no Plane B nametable update occurs
* tile code 32 pixel data confirmed: all 0x00 bytes = all palette index 0 = invisible on black backdrop; hook correctly stages 0x0014 (all-black) for all BG clear calls
* tile code 0xAD pixel data confirmed: all 0x00 bytes; preloaded to slot 80 by load_scene_tiles; content correct but never reaches staged_bg_buffer via current hook
* next step: characterize block copy function 0x05A4DC register contract; design opcode_replace hook genesistan_hook_tilemap_bg_blockcopy at that entry point
* design doc: docs/design/Andy_bg_fill_hook_post_rts_no_change_diagnosis.md
* no implementation performed

## [Andy - Analysis, BG block copy hook & warm restart architecture]

* warm restart discovery: YES — arcade delay loop at 0x39F92-0x39F9C counts down D1; after loop exits at 0x39F9E does MOVEA.L (0x0000).W, SP + MOVEA.L (0x0004).W, A0 + JMP (A0); ROM[0x0004]=0x00000202=main_68k; arcade never RTEs; warm-restarts Genesis wrapper every cycle
* consequence of warm restart: YES — init_staging_state runs every cycle; sets bg_row_dirty=0xFFFFFFFF; next VINT flushes full checkerboard staged_bg_buffer over any valid BG content
* block copy function address corrected: YES — prior doc said 0x05A4DC; actual entry is 0x05A4E0 (4E75 at 0x05A4DC is RTS of prior function; BSR.W 0x0154 from 0x05A388: PC_after=0x05A38C + 0x0154 = 0x05A4E0)
* block copy register contract confirmed: YES — A0=source ROM ptr, A1=dest BG RAM base, D0=cols, D1=rows, D2=attr word; two known callers: (A1=0xC00320, A0=0x5A7DA, D0=28, D1=21, D2=1) and (A1=0xC00328, A0=0x5B0B2, D0=28, D1=20, D2=1)
* row stride mapping verified: YES — function does ADDA.L #0x100, A2 per row; matches FUNC B hook's longword row stride (64 longwords × 4 bytes = 256 = 0x100); start_row/col derived from A1 via same formula: longword_off=(byte_off>>2), col=longword_off&0x3F, row=(longword_off>>6)&0x1F
* init_staging_state fix identified: YES — change move.l #0xFFFFFFFF, bg_row_dirty to clr.l bg_row_dirty; prevents warm-restart cycle from re-flushing checkerboard over valid BG VRAM content
* new design doc: docs/design/Andy_bg_blockcopy_hook_warm_restart_analysis.md
* new Cody prompt: docs/design/Cody_bg_blockcopy_hook_implementation.md
* two changes to implement: (1) init_staging_state dirty flag fix, (2) genesistan_hook_tilemap_bg_blockcopy at 0x05A4E0 + opcode_replace entry
* no implementation performed

## [Andy - Design, address lookup tool]

* manifest confirmed: YES (build/rastan-direct/rastan_direct_patch_manifest.json, 46 opcode_replace entries, each with arcade_pc + rom_pc)
* rom_pc field in manifest: YES (example: entry 0 arcade_pc=0x055968, rom_pc=0x055B68)
* unpatched formula: genesis_rom_offset = arcade_pc + 0x000200 (proven from postpatch_startup_rom.py:978 with shift_deltas=[] because specs/rastan_direct_remap.json contains no shift_replacements key and rom_opcode_replace=[])
* gaps identified: Genesis vectors 0x000000-0x0001FF, wrapper/hooks 0x070000-0x080000, inter-region padding 0x060200-0x070000, PC080SN hw 0xC00000-0xC0FFFF + 0xC20000 + 0xC40000, PC090OJ hw 0xD00000-0xD03FFF, TC0040IOC 0x380000-0x38000F, Genesis WRAM 0xFF0000-0xFFFFFF
* validation cases: YES (patched site 0x055B68->0x055968, unpatched 0x03C3FE->0x03C5FE, hw address 0xC09EA0 -> PC080SN/FG_TILEMAP offset 0x1EA0)
* tool interface defined: YES (tools/addr_lookup.py, --addr/--space/--format/--manifest/--spec/--symbols, JSON + human output, guard-rail preconditions on relocation_delta and shift_replacements)
* design doc created: YES (docs/design/Andy_address_lookup_tool_design.md)
* no implementation performed: YES
* spec complete: YES

Technical detail:
- relocation_delta = dest_start(0x000200) - source_start(0x000000) = 0x000200, from manifest whole_maincpu_copy block and postpatch_startup_rom.py:715.
- runtime_genesis_pc == genesis_rom_offset proven via verify_rastan_direct_boot_guard.py:11 (EXPECTED_RESET_VECTOR=0x00000202 == EXPECTED_START_PROLOGUE_OFFSET 0x000202). Genesis cart mapping is 1:1 identity in [0x000000, 0x3FFFFF] with no bank switching configured.
- specs/rastan_direct_remap.json opcode_replace_count=46 (line 383) matches manifest rom_pc count=46 -> 100% patched-site coverage.
- Tool pre-condition guards fire if either shift_replacements appears in spec or rom_opcode_replace becomes non-empty; protects the +0x200 shortcut from silent drift.
- Tool scope: PC-space resolution only. Hardware-memory writer identification remains the job of Cody_pc080sn_writer_audit.md.

Technical detail:
- Segment kinds: arcade_copy (identity_offset + arcade_start/end), patched_site (origin=opcode_replace|shift_replacement, original/replacement_bytes, shift_delta), preserved_vectors, genesis_only (tag in {wrapper,padding,shim_jump,rom_patch,generated_stub,palette_table,workram_anchor}).
- Finalization pass collapses tentative whole_maincpu_copy segment plus emitted patched/genesis_only segments into a non-overlapping gap-free list through interval carving.
- Patched_site invariant len(original_bytes)/2 == arcade_size and len(replacement_bytes)/2 == genesis_size; opcode_replace path has shift_delta=0 (enforced at postpatch line 973-977); shift_replacement path allows non-zero shift_delta (interior lookup returns UNKNOWN_INTERIOR in that case).
- rom_opcode_replace and shim_jumps carve genesis_only holes in arcade_copy, removing arcade identity for those bytes.

## [Andy - Analysis, VBlank commit audit and corrected spec]

* AGENTS_LOG read: YES
* prior design doc read: YES (docs/design/Andy_rastan_direct_display_tightening_against_rainbow.md, relevant sections §3, §4, §16 re-read)
* Cody STOP report read: YES (docs/design/Cody_bg_fg_dirty_flag_implementation.md, all 108 lines)
* main_68k.s read in full: YES (1201 lines; commit functions, VBlank handler, hooks, bss section all inspected)
* commit function inventory complete: YES (5 functions: vdp_commit_tiles_if_dirty@667, vdp_commit_bg_strips_if_dirty@684, vdp_commit_fg_strips_if_dirty@721, vdp_commit_palette@758, vdp_commit_scroll@768)
* call chains from VBlank mapped: YES (all 5 chains single-hop from _VINT_handler@81)
* mismatch resolved: YES (functions renamed to *_strips_if_dirty; bg_row_dirty/fg_row_dirty are 32-bit bitmap guards at finer granularity than §16 proposed)
* vblank handler audited: YES (lines 81-109; display OFF at 86-88, display ON at 100-102, scroll after display ON at 104)
* current budget assessed: YES (steady ~552 cyc, typical-gameplay ~7400 cyc, worst-case-after-cwindow-clear ~57000 cyc)
* corrected spec produced: YES (retirement of §16; zero implementation action required)
* no implementation performed: YES
* design doc created: YES (docs/design/Andy_vblank_commit_audit_corrected_spec.md)
* spec complete: YES

Technical detail:
- Cody's STOP was correct: vdp_commit_bg / vdp_commit_fg symbols do not exist in current source.
- Current source uses strip-level guards: vdp_commit_bg_strips_if_dirty opens with `move.l bg_row_dirty, %d6 / beq.s .Lbg_done` (lines 685-686); vdp_commit_fg_strips_if_dirty opens with `move.l fg_row_dirty, %d6 / beq.s .Lfg_done` (lines 722-723).
- bg_row_dirty and fg_row_dirty are 32-bit bitmaps (one bit per strip row), set via `bset` by hooks at lines 343, 515, 624, and written wholesale by genesistan_hook_cwindow_clear at lines 661-662 (0xFFFFFFFF).
- init_staging_state clears both bitmaps at lines 949-950 (clr.l).
- Adding coarse .b bg_dirty/fg_dirty flags on top of the existing 32-bit bitmap guards would be PRIME DIRECTIVE scaffolding: duplicates an existing guard at lower granularity, adds dirty-source consistency burden to every hook site, changes steady-state cost by zero cycles (both paths early-exit ~24 cycles), and does not fix the worst-case overrun.
- Overrun summary: steady-state NOT PRESENT (~552 cycles); typical gameplay AT BUDGET (~7,400 cycles with 4 strips/plane); worst case PRESENT (~57,000 cycles when all 64 strip bits set in one frame, e.g. frame after genesistan_hook_cwindow_clear).
- Worst-case overrun is a strip-count / one-shot clear event problem, not a flag-granularity problem. Coarse byte flags cannot fix it because they would be set whenever bitmap is non-zero.
- Retirement action: §16 of docs/design/Andy_rastan_direct_display_tightening_against_rainbow.md is OBSOLETE. Cody should not attempt to implement §16 against current source. No ROM/manifest/address_map changes required.
- Follow-up scope out of this task: cwindow_clear strip staging, DMA migration, per-VBlank commit count limiting. Each requires a separate prompt.

## [Andy - Analysis, text writer 3c4d2 hook spec]

* AGENTS_LOG read: YES
* prior STOP doc read: YES (docs/design/Andy_text_writer_3c3fe_hook_spec.md — confirms 0x03C3FE is not the crash source and identifies 0x03C4D2)
* hook strategy doc read: YES (docs/design/Andy_final_pc080sn_hook_strategy.md — multi-site hook pattern and scaffolding definition)
* address_map.json consulted: YES (build/rastan-direct/address_map.json; arcade_copy segment [0x03B0A4, 0x03EF28) → [0x03B2A4, 0x03F128), identity_offset 512)
* arcade disasm read: YES (build/maincpu.disasm.txt, lines 75918-75959 and 76269-76290 for function, inner sub, and dispatcher callers)
* genesis disasm read: CONFIRMED via address_map.json lookup rather than reading genesis_postpatch.disasm.txt directly (segment arithmetic from artifact used per rule 13)
* build 0032 trace read: YES (states/traces/rastan_direct_video_test_build_0032_mame_30s_20260415_010931/genesis_exec_summary.txt — fg_cwindow_live count=8, first_pc=03C52A last_pc=03C518, first_addr=C09EA0 last_addr=C09EA6, last_data=0x0037, final MAME PC 0x000010)
* build 0032 screenshots reviewed: NO (screenshots/build_32/ does not exist; spec does not depend on visual evidence; noted in §11 of design doc, not a STOP per global rules which do not enumerate screenshot absence)
* address space verification complete: YES (arcade_pc 0x03C4D2 → genesis_rom_offset 0x03C6D2; arcade_pc 0x03C516 → 0x03C716; callers 0x03C924/0x03C92C → 0x03CB24/0x03CB2C; 0xC09EA0 classified HW_ADDRESS/PC080SN/FG_TILEMAP offset 0x1EA0)
* 0x03C4D2 disassembled: YES (entry 0x03C4D2, end 0x03C514 inclusive, body length 0x44 bytes, two sub-calls to 0x03C516 at 0x03C502 and 0x03C50E, fast-fill path at 0x03C4E8-0x03C4F6, slow path at 0x03C4F8-0x03C50E, cleanup A1=A2;RTS at 0x03C512-0x03C514, A1 passed in by caller)
* 0x03C516 disassembled: YES (6 words per iteration, writes D0 to A1@(2) at 0x03C530 and D7 to A1@(6) at 0x03C544, stride +8 via addq.l #8,A1 at 0x03C548, D4 iterations, terminator check cmpi.b #0x50,D3 + cmpi.w #1,D4 early rts at 0x03C526, calls helper_5b512_rts at 0x03C53E which is a no-op)
* row/col arithmetic proven: YES (cell_offset = first_write - 0xC08000; cell_index = cell_offset >> 2; arcade_col = cell_index & 0x3F; arcade_row = (cell_index >> 6) & 0x3F — applied to 0xC09EA0 yields row=30 col=40, 20-cell run fits within row bounds)
* input registers defined: YES (A0 descriptor ptr, A1 hw dest, A4 script state, D3 opcode byte preserved; others clobbered)
* output target defined: YES (staged_fg_buffer + fg_row_dirty; translation via genesistan_pc080sn_tile_vram_lut and genesistan_pc080sn_attr_lut; no C-window writes)
* write count confirmed: YES — fast-fill 10 tile writes (single cell per iter), slow path 20 cell writes (10 script bytes × 2 halves)
* caller sites identified: 2 (arcade_pc 0x03C924 beqw, arcade_pc 0x03C92C beqw — both branch targets, not BSR/JSR; only ONE opcode_replace entry required at 0x03C4D2 itself since both branches still resolve to that address)
* visual evidence reviewed: PARTIAL (trace summary reviewed; screenshot directory absent)
* hook spec complete: YES (genesistan_hook_text_writer_3c4d2; entry/exit register contract; fast/slow path algorithm with terminator check semantics; exact opcode_replace JSON entry template including 60 bytes of NOP padding; opcode_replace_count bump 46->47)
* no implementation performed: YES
* design doc created: YES (docs/design/Andy_text_writer_3c4d2_hook_spec.md)
* spec complete: YES

Technical detail:
- Function at arcade_pc 0x03C4D2 is dispatched via conditional branch from text-script opcode dispatcher at arcade_pc ~0x03C902 (reads opcode byte from A0, masks top nibble to #0xF0, compares to 0x50/0x60 at 0x03C920-0x03C928 with beqw 0x3c4d2 at both match sites). Both opcodes route to the same handler — one opcode_replace entry intercepts both.
- Fast-fill path triggers when A4@(11).b == 0x20 (blank line clear, 10 blank tiles at stride 8, writes only A1@(2)).
- Slow path: A0 dereferenced at 0x03C4D8 then advanced by D0*5 for prefix skip; two calls to 0x03C516 with D4=5 and D2 alternating 0/0xFFF0; subq.l #5,A0 rewind between calls so both halves read same 5 script bytes; net post-call A0 advance = +5.
- Each character = 2 adjacent tilemap cells (glyph left-half + right-half) with different attribute (D2=0 vs D2=0xFFF0). Cody spec collapses the 2-call loop into a single 5-iteration loop emitting both cells per script byte.
- Terminator check (0x03C51A-0x03C526): when D3 (dispatch opcode) == 0x50 AND D4 == 1 (final iteration of second call), inner sub early-RTS skipping the last iteration's writes. A0 post-increment still occurred.
- Inner subroutine 0x03C516 has no external callers per arcade disasm grep — safely dead after patch (but not patched; NOPs inside the 68-byte replacement already cover the handler body, and 0x03C516-0x03C54E remains unexecuted).
- Replacement bytes template (68 bytes): 4EB9 {sym:genesistan_hook_text_writer_3c4d2} (6) + 4E75 RTS (2) + 30×4E71 NOP padding (60) = 68 bytes.
- opcode_replace_count must bump from 46 to 47 in specs/rastan_direct_remap.json.
- Hook reads INPUTS FROM REGISTERS (A0/A1/A4/D3), not WRAM — distinct from existing WRAM-fed tilemap hooks. Still scaffolding-clean: no scene-conditional branches, no state-machine awareness, pure stateless translator like the strategy doc requires.
- Helper at 0x0005B512 called from 0x03C53E is the confirmed-empty helper_5b512_rts (RTS-only), no behavior required in hook.
- No existing source-file edits outside the new hook function; no changes to VBlank, scroll, staging init, or other hooks.
- Address space verification did NOT use any +0x200 arithmetic; all cross-space lookups went through the address_map.json arcade_copy segment record (one segment covers all five relevant arcade PCs with identity_offset=512).

## [Andy — Analysis, Build 33 Diagnostic]

Build number analyzed: 0033 (rastan-direct)

Evidence sources examined:
* E1 MAME trace: YES (states/traces/rastan_direct_video_test_build_0033_mame_30s_20260415_125313/genesis_exec_summary.txt — 1798 frames; fg_cwindow_live count=8 first_addr=C09EA0 last_addr=C09EA6; helper_5b512_rts count=0)
* E2 Exodus screenshots: YES — 537 frames present at states/screenshots/build_33/; 7 frames sampled: frame_0001, frame_0090, frame_0180, frame_0270, frame_0360, frame_0450, frame_0537; all panels inspected (VDP Image Window, Plane A/B, CRAM, VRAM Pattern, Registers, console log)
* E3 BlastEm crash: YES (user-provided screenshot — fatal error "machine freeze due to write to address C09EA0")
* E4 Patch entry integrity: YES (specs/rastan_direct_remap.json:351-356 + apps/rastan-direct/out/symbol.txt:42 + ROM byte-read at genesis_rom_offset 0x03C6D2)

STOP DOCUMENT RULE triggered: NO (patch entry at 0x03C4D2 is valid; JSR 0x0007054E + RTS + 30xNOP present in ROM; matches spec replacement_bytes template)

Root cause: (4) Alternate code path writes to 0xC09EA0.
Details:
- Dispatcher at arcade_pc ~0x03C902 routes 9 script-opcode top-nibble values to 8 distinct handlers.
- Only arcade_pc 0x03C4D2 (serving opcodes 0x50 and 0x60) is hooked in Build 33.
- 7 sibling handlers remain unhooked: 0x03C830 (0x10), 0x03C7A4 (0x20), 0x03C6DC (0x30), 0x03C75C (0x90), 0x03C550 (0xA0), 0x03C636 (0xB0), 0x03C586 (0xC0).
- Sibling handlers use the SAME write pattern (movew D0,A1@(2) / movew D7,A1@(6) / addq.l #8,A1). ROM byte verification at address_map-derived genesis_rom_offsets confirms raw 33400002 / 33470006 opcodes intact at handler 0x20 (genesis 0x03CA16/0x03CA24) and handler 0x10's inner sub 0x03C85E (genesis 0x03CA80/0x03CA8E).
- Build 33 attract-mode script data contains top-nibble opcodes other than 0x50/0x60, dispatching to one of the seven unhooked handlers, which writes directly to HW_ADDRESS/PC080SN/FG_TILEMAP and causes the BlastEm fatal freeze at 0xC09EA0.

Hook firing status: PATCH CORRECTLY INSTALLED and LIKELY EXECUTING for opcodes 0x50/0x60.
- ROM bytes at genesis_rom_offset 0x03C6D2 begin 4EB90007054E (JSR to hook) followed by 4E75 RTS and 30 × 4E71 NOPs (68 bytes total).
- symbol.txt:42 resolves genesistan_hook_text_writer_3c4d2 to 0x0007054E.
- helper_5b512_rts count=0 in trace confirms original 0x03C4D2 body + inner sub 0x03C516 are dead (as intended by the patch).
- Hook source at main_68k.s:636-788 writes only into staged_fg_buffer + fg_row_dirty, no C-window writes.

Anomalies:
- fg_cwindow_live first_pc=03C52A last_pc=03C518 in trace summary do NOT correspond to live write instructions at the same genesis_rom_offset (relocated inner-sub writes are at genesis 0x03C730 / 0x03C744). Combined with helper_5b512_rts count=0, this implies either (a) the trace filter normalizes that particular PC field to arcade-pc space, or (b) the summary reuses stale field state from a prior run. Does NOT change root cause — BlastEm evidence independently confirms present-tense C-window write crash.
- Exodus frames 0001 (pre-game dialog), 0090-0270 (synthetic bring-up patterns), 0360-0537 (BLACK main image + repeated Error Trigger log entries). Black-screen window 0270→0360 coincides with trap-vector transition to runtime_genesis_pc 0x000010 observed in prior MAME exit summaries.
- Build 33 MAME trace summary fields (counts, addrs, PCs for fg_cwindow_live) are byte-identical to Build 32 — flagged as open question 4.

Open questions (4):
1. Trace PC-field interpretation for fg_cwindow_live (arcade-pc vs genesis-pc normalization).
2. Which specific script-opcode top-nibble fires the 0xC09EA0 write in Build 33 attract mode.
3. Exodus Register panel values (legibility constraint at thumbnail resolution).
4. MAME Build 33 trace summary identical to Build 32 — possibly cached/stale state in the filter fields; does not invalidate BlastEm evidence.

## [Andy — Analysis, Dispatcher Map Analysis]

files changed: docs/design/Andy_dispatcher_map_analysis.md (new)
build produced: NO
ROM path: N/A
root cause confirmed: YES (already confirmed in prior Build 33 diagnostic; this doc adds per-handler coverage specification)
fix implemented: NO
no unrelated changes: YES

Dispatcher entry arcade_pc confirmed: 0x03C902 -> genesis_rom_offset 0x03CB02 (from address_map.json arcade_copy segment [0x03B0A4, 0x03EF28) → [0x03B2A4, 0x03F128), identity_offset 512)

Dispatcher entry register contract:
- A0: script pointer; (A0) byte is the opcode (read without post-increment).
- A1: FG C-window destination; all handlers write via A1@(2)/A1@(6) (+8 stride) except dispatcher fall-through which uses A1@+ (+2 stride).
- A4: script state block; fields A4@(11).b (char count / mode), A4@(22).w (attr base), A4@(24).w (per-scene offset), A4@(26).w (tile base), plus handler-specific A4@(1).b, A4@(3).b, A4@(27).b, A4@(30).w, A4@(56).b.
- A5: arcade workram base (0xFF0000); handlers 0x03C636 and dispatcher default read A5@(280).b and A5@(318).w for game-state conditionals.
- D3: clobbered by dispatcher for match work; inner subs read D3 for terminator-check semantics.

Complete routing table (10 sibling branches, 8 distinct unhooked handlers + 1 hooked + 1 fall-through path):
- 0x10 -> arcade_pc 0x03C830 (genesis 0x03CA30) [UNHOOKED]
- 0x20 -> arcade_pc 0x03C7A4 (genesis 0x03C9A4) [UNHOOKED]
- 0x30 -> arcade_pc 0x03C6DC (genesis 0x03C8DC) [UNHOOKED]
- 0x50 -> arcade_pc 0x03C4D2 (genesis 0x03C6D2) [HOOKED - genesistan_hook_text_writer_3c4d2]
- 0x60 -> arcade_pc 0x03C4D2 (genesis 0x03C6D2) [HOOKED - same hook, shared handler]
- 0x90 -> arcade_pc 0x03C75C (genesis 0x03C95C) [UNHOOKED]
- 0xA0 -> arcade_pc 0x03C550 (genesis 0x03C750) [UNHOOKED]
- 0xB0 -> arcade_pc 0x03C636 (genesis 0x03C836) [UNHOOKED]
- 0xC0 -> arcade_pc 0x03C586 (genesis 0x03C786) [UNHOOKED]
- default (no top-nibble match) -> fall-through at arcade_pc 0x03C950 (genesis 0x03CB50) [UNHOOKED; DIFFERENT write shape: A1@+ stride 2, not A1@(2)/A1@(6) stride 8]

Firing handlers in Build 0033 attract mode: NOT IDENTIFIABLE from the current trace. genesis_exec_summary.txt fg_cwindow_live watch shows 8 writes at 0xC09EA0..0xC09EA6 (frames 170-384) but helper_5b512_rts count=0 rules out the hooked handler's dead body from firing, and the trace log does not have per-handler granularity. Additional per-handler instrumentation required. Noted as open question.

Write contract confirmed: PARTIAL.
- Confirmed: all 9 top-nibble handlers use A1@(2)/A1@(6)/addq.l #8,A1 stride-8 family.
- Confirmed: dispatcher fall-through default path uses a DIFFERENT shape (movew %dn, %a1@+ stride-2, plus a fast-fill escape at 0x03C9F8 that matches the +8 family).
- Varied per handler: prefix-skip multipliers (#3, #5, #6, #7, #9, lslw #2), iteration counts (5..~10 cells), attribute-offset alternation sequences, inner-subroutine bodies (each handler has its own), A1 post-loop advances (0x10, 0x18, 0x20, 0x30, 0x50).
- Game-state conditionals read by handlers 0x03C636, 0x03C830, and the default path (A5@(280), A5@(318), A4@(56), A4@(3), A4@(27)) — these make dispatcher-level centralization prohibitive.

Interception level: PER-HANDLER.
Evidence:
- Condition (a) "stable register contract at dispatcher entry" HOLDS (§1).
- Condition (b) "all handlers write via the same logical path" FAILS — stride-8 family vs stride-2 fall-through family, with per-handler semantic divergence within the stride-8 family.
- Condition (c) "single hook without handler-specific logic" FAILS — a dispatcher-level hook would need an internal 10-case dispatch replicating each handler's prefix multiplier, iteration plan, attr-offset sequence, and A1 post-advance. Dispatcher-inside-translator is equivalent work to 8 per-handler translators.
- Condition (d) "no non-FG-tilemap work to preserve" FAILS — game-state conditionals and default-path A0-advance from (A0)+ byte reads must be preserved.

Required per-handler hook list (8 additional hooks, to complement the existing 0x03C4D2 hook):
1. arcade_pc 0x03C830 (genesis 0x03CA30)  — opcode 0x10
2. arcade_pc 0x03C7A4 (genesis 0x03C9A4)  — opcode 0x20
3. arcade_pc 0x03C6DC (genesis 0x03C8DC)  — opcode 0x30
4. arcade_pc 0x03C75C (genesis 0x03C95C)  — opcode 0x90
5. arcade_pc 0x03C550 (genesis 0x03C750)  — opcode 0xA0
6. arcade_pc 0x03C636 (genesis 0x03C836)  — opcode 0xB0
7. arcade_pc 0x03C586 (genesis 0x03C786)  — opcode 0xC0
8. arcade_pc 0x03C950 (genesis 0x03CB50)  — dispatcher fall-through default path; sizing requires sub-sub caller audit (open question 3).

STOP conditions triggered: NONE. All dispatcher branches resolved via address_map.json.

Open questions:
1. Which specific sibling fires in Build 0033 attract mode (trace lacks per-handler granularity).
2. fg_cwindow_live first_pc/last_pc vs helper_5b512_rts count=0 discrepancy (PC-normalization in genesistrace.lua unresolved).
3. Patch-span sizing for default-path hook at 0x03C950 depends on whether sub-subs 0x03C9E8, 0x03C9F6, 0x03CA00, 0x03CA12, 0x03CA26 have callers outside the default path (grep audit is the next mechanical step).

Default path arcade_pc 0x03C950: OUT OF SCOPE — confirmed excluded from this spec, per prompt.

Cross-handler consistency:
- IDENTICAL across all 7 handlers: first two instructions (movea.l A0@(2),A0 + move.b A4@(11),D0+ext.w), write pair offsets A1@(2)/A1@(6) within 8-byte iteration record, +8 stride, attribute base A4@(22) masked to 0x1FF, tile base A4@(26), blank-tile sentinel #0x0180 at A1@(2).
- DIFFER per handler: prefix multiplier (x1 lit, x3, x4 lsl, x5, x6, x7, x9), terminator sentinel (none, 0xFF, zero-byte, or D3==0x50&&D4==1), game-state reads (A4@(1), A4@(56), A5@(280), A5@(318)), A0 rewind schedule, cells-per-call (6..26), post-loop A1 advance (0, 0x10, 0x20, 0x30, 0x48, 0x50, 0x60, 0x68, 0x78), inner-sub bodies (each handler has a private inner sub; 0x03C742 and 0x03C7D2 are shared across multiple handlers and become dead once all 7 patched), tile-source scheme (literal, script-byte+bases, D1-biased, synthesized-from-D3, special 0x0A0D).
- UNIQUE: 0x03C830 sub-sub 0x03C89A writes at A1@(4) (attribute word of SAME cell whose tile is at A1@(2)); this is the only A1@(4) write in the entire dispatcher set.

Shared helper structure viable: YES at write-step granularity only (factor out LUT-translate-and-store + bset row fg_row_dirty into a private helper internal to main_68k.s). NO as a single-handler substitute — each handler's parameter set is unique enough that a dispatcher-level hook is still not feasible (reconfirms prior analysis in docs/design/Andy_dispatcher_map_analysis.md).

Contract differences from 0x03C4D2 (per-handler): documented explicitly per handler in §H1-H7 and summarized in H8 per-handler-difference table.

Implementation readiness: READY FOR CODY.
- 7 new hook functions in apps/rastan-direct/src/main_68k.s (one per handler).
- 7 new opcode_replace entries in specs/rastan_direct_remap.json.
- 7 new required_symbols in specs/rastan_direct_remap.json.
- opcode_replace_count: 47 -> 54. Patcher invariant in tools/translation/postpatch_startup_rom.py needs corresponding bump (total_genesis_bytes_covered recomputed by build, count bump from 47 to 54).

STOP conditions triggered: NONE.
- Every handler entry point confirmed in build/maincpu.disasm.txt.
- Every genesis_rom_offset resolved via address_map.json arcade_copy segment [0x03B0A4, 0x03EF28) → [0x03B2A4, 0x03F128), identity_offset=512.
- No handler body overlaps another handler.
- No cross-space arithmetic.
- No stride-8 handler requires the default path 0x03C950 for correctness.

Open questions:

2. Default path 0x03C950 — out of scope; post-Build-0034 trace will confirm if any fg_cwindow_live writes remain and thus whether the default-path hook is needed.

## [Andy — Analysis, Rainbow Islands comparative translation]

* Rastan handler family confirmed: YES
* Rainbow Islands arcade equivalent found: PARTIAL — RI arcade has NO multi-handler script-opcode dispatcher (verified by single `andib #-16,%d0` site at arcade_pc (RI) 0x002406 which is a sound-RAM writer, not a tilemap dispatcher). RI arcade DOES have 8 stride-8 tilemap writers (federation-of-helpers pattern), but none is a structural counterpart to a Rastan dispatcher handler — only `arcade_pc (RI) 0x01F264` matches Rastan's exact `A1@(2)+A1@(6)+8` write shape.
* Rainbow Islands Genesis implementation identified: YES — confirmed it is a REIMPLEMENTATION (not a translation). RI Genesis cart writes via standard Genesis VDP control/data port pattern (movel #0xC0000000 to A4@ for DMA setup at genesis_rom_offset (RI) 0x00026E; movew 0xC00004,D0 for status read at 0x000384). NO `addal #8,A1` / `andib #-16,D3` / `A1@(2)+A1@(6)` writers anywhere in RI Genesis code regions.
* Structural model determined: PER-HANDLER HOOKS.
* Evidence completeness: PARTIAL — direct comparative evidence (RI Genesis as a translation precedent) is unavailable because RI Genesis is a rewrite, not a translation. Strongest available evidence (RI arcade federation-of-helpers + per-handler-unique semantics in Rastan + prior dispatcher-level rejection) all converge on per-handler hooks. No hedging in §6 final determination.
* no unrelated changes: YES
* design doc created: YES (docs/design/Andy_rainbow_islands_comparative_translation.md)

Build context: Build 0033, rastan-direct.

Key technical findings:
- RI arcade has NO Rastan-style multi-handler script-opcode dispatcher. The single `andib #-16,%d0` instruction in RI arcade at arcade_pc (RI) 0x002406 lives inside a sound-RAM write routine (writes to A5@(16382)), not a tilemap dispatcher (build/rainbow_islands_arcade.disasm.txt:2657).
- RI arcade uses a federation of 8 specialized stride-8 tilemap writers (grep `addal #8,%a1` returns 8 sites: 0x004BF0, 0x00CFDE, 0x00D02C, 0x00D050, 0x00D066, 0x014092, 0x01F234, 0x01F264). 14 of these read A4@(6) as the destination pointer (consistent with Rastan's A1 entry contract analog). Each writer is a small, single-purpose helper called from many call sites — NOT routed through a centralizing dispatcher.
- Cell-record convention varies in RI arcade: most writers use A1@/A1@(4) (4-byte cell), one writer (0x01F264) uses A1@(2)/A1@(6) (8-byte cell, exact Rastan match). Sentinel value is 0xFF consistently in RI; Rastan handlers use four different sentinel schemes.
- RI Genesis code regions contain NO PC080SN-style writers. The arcade architecture was discarded entirely; rendering uses standard Genesis VDP control/data port pattern (movew control word to 0xC00004, DMA setup via movel #0xC0000000, etc.).
- Per-handler hook conclusion: corroborates the prior dispatcher-level rejection in docs/design/Andy_dispatcher_map_analysis.md §6. The 4 alternative structures from the prompt (per-handler / grouped / dispatcher-level / shared-helper+wrappers) are evaluated; per-handler is the only choice consistent with the evidence. Shared helper + per-handler wrappers is treated as an implementation detail within per-handler (the existing 0x03C4D2 hook already has a `_store_cell` helper at apps/rastan-direct/src/main_68k.s:768-778), not a competing structural model.
- Implication: Cody implements the 7 per-handler hooks per docs/design/Andy_stride8_sibling_hook_spec.md. No grouping, no dispatcher-level reroute, no shared-dispatcher hook. opcode_replace_count: 47 -> 54. Dispatcher default path 0x03C950 remains out of scope (Build 0034 trace will determine necessity).

STOP conditions triggered: NONE.

### MAME Exit Summary (2026-04-15 18:40:00)
- Final PC: 0x000010
- Stack Pointer (SP): 0x00DEA634
- Unique Unmapped Memory Addresses: none

## [Andy — Analysis, Build 0034 Exodus Rendering Failure]

* files changed: docs/design/Andy_build_34_exodus_rendering_failure.md (new)
* build produced: NO
* ROM path: N/A
* root cause confirmed: YES
* fix implemented: NO
* no unrelated changes: YES

Build 0034 baseline confirmed: YES (opcode_replace_count=54, all 8 hook symbols present in required_symbols, ROM hash 38fb4d4d2df3e789be18a52b06d47af09ad8c6aec880e3bd6ad87802c87bd205 differs from Build 33's b0445a..., dispatcher default 0x03C950 NOT hooked per prior spec scope).

Frames sampled (Build 34 Exodus, states/screenshots/build_34/): frame_0001, frame_0090, frame_0180, frame_0270, frame_0360, frame_0435 (last available; total 435 frames in directory). All Exodus rendering panels prioritized: VDP Image Window, Plane Viewer (Layer A and Layer B separately), VRAM Pattern Viewer, VDP Palette. Console log panel observed for trap evidence.

Tilemap-to-pattern correlation answered: YES — pattern tiles ARE correctly referenced by Layer A / Layer B plane maps (frames 0090-0270 show Plane Viewer thumbnails matching VRAM Pattern Viewer content matching Image Window). Therefore problem is data PRODUCTION (no real arcade text/tile content reaches staged buffers), NOT data USAGE (mapping is intact).

Trace correlated: YES (states/traces/rastan_direct_video_test_build_0034_mame_30s_20260415_183954/genesis_exec_summary.txt).
- vdp_ports_live count=26336, first_pc=070100 — wrapper VBlank healthy across all 1798 frames.
- fg_cwindow_live count=8 first_addr=C09EA0 last_addr=C09EA6 — BYTE-IDENTICAL to Build 33 trace summary. Two interpretations: (a) trace fields stale/cached (Build 33 open question 4 reinforced), or (b) 8 writes still happen in Build 34 from a code path unaffected by the new sibling hooks. BlastEm crash persistence independently confirms a writer remains active.
- helper_5b512_rts@* count=0 — confirms the original 0x03C4D2 / 0x03C6DC inner subs are dead (as intended by their now-installed hooks).
- New stride-8 hook firing UNVERIFIABLE from current trace (no per-hook live-write watch in genesistrace.lua).

Arcade comparison used: YES (states/reference/rastan_arcade_60s/). Sampled frame_0001 (white power-on), frame_0090 (RASTAN title screen with text + sword art), frame_0270 (chronological narrative + BEST 5 high-score table), frame_0450 (item list AXE/HAMMER/FIRE SWORD/SHIELD with icons + descriptions). All represent dense PC080SN FG text rendering. Build 34 shows none of this content — only bring-up scaffolding patterns.

Primary rendering failure category: UNHOOKED ACTIVE WRITER PATH.
- Dispatcher fall-through default path at arcade_pc 0x03C950 (genesis_rom_offset 0x03CB50) remains unhooked. Default body uses move.w D0,A1@+ (stride 2) writes at arcade_pc 0x03C982, 0x03C990, 0x03C99E plus a fast-fill escape movew #0x0180,A1@(2) at arcade_pc 0x03C9F8. Top-nibble values 0x00, 0x40, 0x70, 0x80, 0xD0, 0xE0, 0xF0 fall through to this default — none of the 8 installed handlers covers these.
- BlastEm crash persistence across Builds 32, 33, 34 (patched-site count 46→47→54) confirms an unhooked writer remains active. Default path is the only writer in the dispatcher not addressed in any build.
- Exodus shows BLACK + Error Trigger console entries from frame ~0270-0360 onward → CPU traps to runtime_genesis_pc 0x000010 (Genesis vector trap stub) before arcade scene loader / text-script dispatcher can deliver real arcade content.

Why other categories are NOT primary:
- Wrong tilemap mapping: refuted §3.8 — tiles in Pattern Viewer are correctly referenced by plane maps.
- Wrong tile/attribute composition: refuted — bring-up tiles ARE rendered with correct colors; no hooked content visible because execution dies before content production.
- Wrong VDP register/scroll/plane state: refuted by vdp_ports_live count=26336 (wrapper writes VDP registers every frame).
- Wrong commit timing/ordering: refuted — bring-up patterns DO display correctly through frame 0270; commit pipeline healthy until trap.

Secondary issue: Whether the 7 new stride-8 hooks fire correctly when their script-opcode top-nibble matches has not been verified due to lack of per-hook live-write watch in genesistrace.lua. Diagnosable only after default-path hook eliminates the trap.

Evidence completeness: FULL.
STOP triggered: NO.

Next-step recommendation (analysis only — not implemented):
1. Spec the hook for arcade_pc 0x03C950 default path; resolve sub-sub caller audit (open question 3 of dispatcher map analysis).
2. Build 35 should produce both new ROM and new trace; re-examine Exodus frames for arcade text appearance in staged_fg_buffer.
3. Add per-hook live-write watchpoints to tools/mame/genesistrace.lua to attribute writes to specific handlers.

## [Andy — Analysis, Default Path 0x03C950 Sub-Caller Audit and Hook Spec]

files changed: docs/design/Andy_default_path_3c950_hook_spec.md (new)
build produced: NO
ROM path: N/A
root cause confirmed: YES (continuation of Build 34 diagnostic)
fix implemented: NO
no unrelated changes: YES

Entry point confirmed: arcade_pc 0x03C950 -> genesis_rom_offset 0x03CB50 (address_map.json arcade_copy segment, identity_offset=512). Bytes at that genesis offset are unpatched original arcade content (no opcode_replace overlap).

All 5 sub-subs audited via grep of build/maincpu.disasm.txt:
- 0x03C9E8: INTERNAL ONLY — callers 0x03C97E (bsrw), 0x03C9BE (bsrw)
- 0x03C9F6: INTERNAL ONLY — callers 0x03C966 (bnew), 0x03C9AC (bnew)
- 0x03CA00: INTERNAL ONLY — callers 0x03C960 (bsrw), 0x03C9A6 (bsrw)
- 0x03CA12: INTERNAL ONLY — callers 0x03C992 (bsrw), 0x03C9CE (bsrw)
- 0x03CA26: INTERNAL ONLY — caller 0x03C958 (bnew, from default-path entry block itself)

External callers found: NO for all 5 sub-subs.

Out-of-scope helper noted: 0x03C8F6 is also INTERNAL-only (single caller 0x03C98C inside default path), but lives BEFORE the 0x03C950 contiguous range. NOT included in patch span; becomes dead code after patch (its only caller is overwritten). Hook must replicate the helper's behavior internally: cmpib #0x70,D3; if match, addw A4@(24),D1.

Write instructions targeting HW_ADDRESS/PC080SN/FG_TILEMAP exhaustively identified (9 distinct writes labelled WRITE A..I):
- WRITE A: 0x03C982 movew %d0, %a1@+ (primary cell-N attr)
- WRITE B: 0x03C990 movew %d1, %a1@+ (primary cell-N tile)
- WRITE C: 0x03CA20 movew %d0, %a1@+ inside sub 0x03CA12 (primary cell-(N+1) attr)
- WRITE D: 0x03C99E movew %d7, %a1@+ (primary cell-(N+1) tile)
- WRITE E: 0x03C9C2 movew %d0, %a1@+ (alt cell-N attr)
- WRITE F: 0x03C9CC movew %d1, %a1@+ (alt cell-N tile)
- WRITE G: 0x03CA20 movew %d0, %a1@+ inside sub 0x03CA12 second invocation (alt cell-(N+1) attr)
- WRITE H: 0x03C9E0 movew %d7, %a1@+ (alt cell-(N+1) tile)
- WRITE I: 0x03C9F6 movew #0x0180, %a1@(2) (fast-fill escape, blank tile single cell)

Sub-subs 0x03C9E8, 0x03CA00, 0x03CA26 perform NO writes (control/state only).

Safe patch span determined: arcade_pc 0x03C950 .. arcade_pc 0x03CA37 inclusive = 0xE8 bytes (232). Genesis range: 0x03CB50 .. 0x03CC37 inclusive. Binary verified unpatched. No overlap with existing patched_site segments. Single opcode_replace entry sufficient.

Safe for single opcode_replace: YES.

Hook contract complete (genesistan_hook_text_writer_3c950):
- Input regs: A0 (script ptr, post-inc consumed inside), A1 (FG hw dest), A4 (script state — fields A4@(3), A4@(22), A4@(24), A4@(26), A4@(30), A4@(39)), D0 (cleared at entry), D2 (loop count, caller-supplied), D3 (top nibble from 0x03CA00 sentinel sub), D5 (sentinel marker), D6 (bit 0 selects entry path via 0x03CA26), D7 (selects main vs alt main loop, also used inside as path/sign flag).
- Output: staged_fg_buffer + fg_row_dirty per cell (row/col formula: cell_index=(A1-0xC08000)/4, col=cell_index&0x3F, row=(cell_index>>6)&0x3F — same as 0x03C4D2 §5.1).
- 4 writes per non-sentinel iteration (full cell record: tile+attr both cells); 1 write per sentinel iteration (blank tile cell N tile slot only); 8-byte A1 advance in either case.
- Fast-fill escape semantics differ from stride-8 family: writes only cell-N tile (not cell-N attr), skips cell N+1.
- Hook reads word at absolute address 0x000010 (Genesis preserved-vectors region) via subw 0x10,D7 in alt path WRITE H — must preserve verbatim (effectively subtracts 0 on Genesis since 0x000010 holds high word of reset vector).
- Helper 0x03C8F6 D3==0x70 logic must be inlined into hook (D1 += A4@(24) when D3==0x70).

Callers: dispatcher fall-through only (implicit fall-through from 0x03C94C beqw to 0x03C586 — no instruction explicitly targets 0x03C950). NO direct bsr/jsr to 0x03C950 anywhere in arcade ROM. Single opcode_replace at 0x03C950 sufficient.

Differences from stride-8 family (implementation-affecting):
- Stride-2 A1@+ (post-inc) vs stride-8 indexed A1@(2)/A1@(6).
- 4 writes per iter (full cell record) vs 2 writes per iter (tile-only).
- Loop count from caller D2 vs hardcoded per-handler.
- Path selection via D6 bit 0 + D7 + D3 + sentinel sub (more complex than stride-8 family's per-handler conditionals).
- Reads from absolute address 0x000010 (unique among all dispatcher handlers).
- Conditional secondary dispatch via 0x03CA26 (no stride-8 equivalent).

Implementation readiness: READY FOR CODY.

Cody work items per §6:
1. Add hook genesistan_hook_text_writer_3c950 to apps/rastan-direct/src/main_68k.s (reuse existing _store_cell + _translate_attr helpers).
2. Add to .global and to required_symbols.
3. Add opcode_replace entry per §4.7 template (232 bytes).
4. Bump opcode_replace_count: 54 -> 55.
5. Bump patcher invariant in tools/translation/postpatch_startup_rom.py: 54 -> 55 (total_genesis_bytes_covered unchanged for equal-length replacement).
6. Build 0035, 30s MAME trace, Exodus capture; verify fg_cwindow_live drops to zero and BlastEm no longer crashes at 0xC09EA0.

STOP conditions triggered: NONE.

Open questions:
1. Attribute-extraction pattern for D0/D7 should pass through existing _translate_attr helper at main_68k.s:739-766 (consumes generic 16-bit input). Cody implementation detail.
2. Trace-summary fg_cwindow_live field staleness across Builds 32/33/34 unresolved; per-hook live-write watch in genesistrace.lua is a tooling improvement, not required for Build 0035 correctness validation (BlastEm crash absence is the primary success signal).

root cause confirmed: YES — CROSS-SPACE CONFUSION RESOLVED. Trace writer PCs are genesis_rom_offset values, NOT arcade_pc values. genesis_rom_offset 0x03C516 maps to arcade_pc 0x03C316 (via address_map.json), NOT to arcade_pc 0x03C516. The live writer is the NUMBER-RENDERER FUNCTION at arcade_pc 0x03C2E2, completely separate from the text-script dispatcher family.
fix implemented: NO
no unrelated changes: YES

Critical finding: the trace writer PCs (genesis_rom_offset 0x03C516/0x03C518/0x03C52A/0x03C52C) were misidentified as arcade_pc 0x03C516 (text-script inner subroutine). Address_map.json segment lookup proves: genesis 0x03C516 → arcade 0x03C316. Arcade_pc 0x03C302..0x03C32C is a digit-rendering loop inside the number-renderer function at arcade_pc 0x03C2E2 — confirmed from build/maincpu.disasm.txt:75750-75801. Write instructions at arcade_pc 0x03C312 (movew D7,A1@+ attr), 0x03C314 (movew D1,A1@+ tile), 0x03C326, 0x03C328 (same shape, high-nibble path) produce the FG tilemap writes. The MAME watchpoint reports genesis_rom_offset PCs a few instructions AFTER the actual write ops (pipeline timing artifact, confirmed by Cody's anomaly note).

All direct callers of arcade_pc 0x03C2E2 found and classified (7 total):
1. arcade_pc 0x03A546 → genesis 0x03A746, arcade_copy, LIVE
2. arcade_pc 0x03A96E → genesis 0x03AB6E, arcade_copy, LIVE
3. arcade_pc 0x03AC60 → genesis 0x03AE60, patched_site [0x03AC54,0x03AC66), DEAD (overwritten with bras+NOPs by existing PC080SN-readback bypass patch; the bsrw at offset 0x0C in the patch span is now NOP)
4. arcade_pc 0x03B0AC → genesis 0x03B2AC, arcade_copy, LIVE
5. arcade_pc 0x03B426 → genesis 0x03B626, arcade_copy, LIVE
6. arcade_pc 0x03B42C → genesis 0x03B62C, arcade_copy, LIVE
7. arcade_pc 0x03B714 → genesis 0x03B914, arcade_copy, LIVE

External callers: 6 LIVE + 1 DEAD = 7 total.
All LIVE callers are bsrw 0x3C2E2 — single hook at 0x03C2E2 intercepts all.

A4=0 at runtime: EXPLAINED — number-renderer does not use A4. A4=0 is a benign artifact of the caller context (attract-mode state machine does not establish a text-script A4 pointer for this function).

A0=0x0003C59A at trace time: EXPLAINED — genesis_rom_offset 0x03C59A → arcade_pc 0x03C39A = table_base(0x03C37C) + 0x1E. Function computed D0_input=3 × muluw #10 = 30 = 0x1E, placing A0 at display-entry index 3.

D3=0x00FF: D3 not used by this function; value retained from prior attract-mode sentinel processing.

helper_5b512_rts count=0 reconciled: the text-script inner sub at arcade_pc 0x03C516 (genesis 0x03C716) IS dead (confirmed). It was never the live writer. The trace PCs at genesis 0x03C516 pointed to arcade 0x03C316 (number-renderer), not the inner sub.

Selected fix path: OPTION C (reframed) — hook the number-renderer function at arcade_pc 0x03C2E2.
- Why A rejected: 0x03C2E2 is unrelated to 0x03C4D2 (text-script handler); extending that patch span cannot reach the number-renderer.
- Why B rejected: arcade_pc 0x03C516 is confirmed dead; hooking dead code does not fix the live crash.
- Why C selected: arcade_pc 0x03C2E2 is the single entry point reached by all 6 live callers. One hook intercepts all live FG-write traffic from this function. Structurally sound per Rainbow Islands strategy (separate intent class: number/score display).

Implementation readiness: READY FOR CODY (pending follow-up hook spec doc for genesistan_hook_number_renderer_3c2e2).
- Hook name: genesistan_hook_number_renderer_3c2e2
- Patch span: arcade_pc 0x03C2E2..0x03C37B = 0x9A bytes (154). ROM table at 0x03C37C left intact (readable by hook).
- genesis_rom_offset of patch start: 0x03C4E2
- Input: D0 = display-entry index (0-based)
- A1 loaded from table entry at +2 (FG tilemap hw address)
- Write shapes: stride-4 A1@+ (attr+tile per digit), A1@(2) indexed (leading-zero suppression), A1@+ sequence ("ALL" special)
- opcode_replace_count: 55 → 56
- 6 live callers, 1 dead (patched_site overlap)

Open questions:
1. Full hook spec for genesistan_hook_number_renderer_3c2e2 needed (follow-up Andy prompt with standard H1-H7 template).
2. Whether ROM table at 0x03C37C entry index 3 contains 0x00C09EA0 as the FG dest address (should be verified by hex dump).
3. After Build 36 (with this hook), check trace for remaining fg_cwindow_live writes from other non-dispatcher, non-number-renderer FG writers.

## [Andy — Analysis, Number Renderer 0x03C2E2 Hook Spec]

* files changed: docs/design/Andy_number_renderer_3c2e2_hook_spec.md (new)
* build produced: NO
* ROM path: N/A
* root cause confirmed: YES (continuation — number renderer at arcade_pc 0x03C2E2 confirmed as the live FG tilemap writer producing the 0xC09EA0 crash)
* fix implemented: NO
* no unrelated changes: YES

Entry point and patch span confirmed:
- arcade_pc 0x03C2E2 → genesis_rom_offset 0x03C4E2 (address_map.json arcade_copy segment, identity_offset=512).
- Patch span: arcade_pc 0x03C2E2..0x03C37B = 0x9A bytes (154). ROM table at 0x03C37C EXCLUDED from span.
- Genesis bytes at 0x03C4E2 match arcade bytes at 0x03C2E2 (first 20 bytes verified: 3E3C0000C0FC000A41FA0090D0C0301022680002). Unpatched.
- No overlap with any patched_site segment in address_map.json.

ROM table entries documented — all FG:
- 13 valid entries (indices 0-12). Entry size: 10 bytes. All A1 destinations are HW_ADDRESS/PC080SN/FG_TILEMAP (range 0x00C08D24 to 0x00C09EA0). NO non-FG entries. STOP NOT triggered.
- Entry 3 confirmed: count=2, A1=0x00C09EA0 (the crash address), A2=0x0010C013 (BCD credits counter in arcade workram). Row=30, Col=40 — matches Build 35 trace evidence.
- A2 data-pointer translation required: all A2 values are arcade-absolute workram addresses (0x0010Cxxx). Hook must translate to genesis WRAM: genesis_a2 = A5 + (table_a2_value & 0xFFFF), where A5 = 0xFF0000 (genesis WRAM base). This is data-pointer relocation, not code-address cross-space arithmetic.

All three write forms specced:
1. Digit loop (arcade_pc 0x03C302-0x03C32C): D0 iterations, alternating high/low nibble extraction from A2 BCD bytes (A2 scans backward via subql #1 on odd digits). Tile = 0x30 | nibble. Write order: attr (D7=0) then tile (D1) via A1@+. A1 advances +4 bytes per digit (2 words × 2 bytes). Total cells for count=6: 12 cells, 24 bytes A1 advance.
2. Leading-zero suppression (arcade_pc 0x03C32E-0x03C350): active only when count==6. Scans forward from entry A1, replaces tile 0x30 with tile 0x20 (space) via movew #0x20, A1@(2) indexed write. Stops at first non-0x30 tile. A1 advance +4 per scanned cell. Modifies cells already written by digit loop.
3. "ALL" handler (arcade_pc 0x03C352-0x03C37A): entered when count==0xFFFF. If A2@ low nibble == 7: backs up A1 by 8, writes 'A','L','L' (tiles 0x41, 0x4C, 0x4C) with attrs. If nibble != 7: re-enters digit loop with D0=1 (renders 1 digit). "ALL" write uses mixed A1@+ (5 post-inc writes) and A1@ (1 non-post-inc).

A1 advancement per write form confirmed:
- Digit loop: +4 bytes/digit via 2× A1@+ post-inc.
- Suppression: +4 bytes/scanned digit via addql #4, A1.
- "ALL": -8 backup then +10 forward (5× A1@+) = net +2 from pre-backup position; final write has no post-inc.

Tile translation determination: LUT REQUIRED.
- Evidence: tile codes generated by this function (0x30+nibble, 0x20, 0x41, 0x4C) are arcade PC080SN tile indices. All other FG hooks in this project translate arcade tile codes through genesistan_pc080sn_tile_vram_lut. The number renderer uses the same tile index space as the text-script handlers (same PC080SN FG plane, same hardware). Therefore LUT translation is mandatory.

Attribute word analysis: D7=0 (set at function entry, never modified). Passes through genesistan_pc080sn_attr_lut[0] → default Genesis palette/priority/flip word for palette line 0.

fg_row_dirty strategy: per-write bset (safe approach). In practice, each table entry's digits span a single row (counts ≤ 6, starting at fixed col, no row wrap). One bset per call would suffice but per-write is correct for the "ALL" backup case.

Implementation readiness: READY FOR CODY.
- Hook name: genesistan_hook_number_renderer_3c2e2
- Table access: must use absolute lea (not PC-relative; hook lives in 0x70000+ wrapper, table at genesis_rom_offset 0x03C57C is out of PC-relative range)
- A2 translation: genesis_a2 = A5 + (table_a2_value & 0xFFFF)
- opcode_replace_count: 55 → 56
- 6 live callers via bsrw 0x3C2E2; single opcode_replace sufficient
- Build 36: verify fg_cwindow_live drops to 0 and BlastEm no longer crashes at 0xC09EA0

Open questions:
1. Table access: Cody uses absolute addressing (movea.l #0x0003C57C, A0 or equivalent). Implementation detail, no design ambiguity.
2. After Build 36: if fg_cwindow_live count > 0, additional FG writers exist beyond the dispatcher family and number renderer. New writer audit against Cody_pc080sn_writer_audit.md needed.

### MAME Exit Summary (2026-04-16 12:12:13)
- Final PC: 0x000202
- Stack Pointer (SP): 0x00DEA640
- Unique Unmapped Memory Addresses: none

## [Andy — Analysis, Build 36 Exodus Rendering]

* files changed: docs/design/Andy_build_36_exodus_rendering_analysis.md (new)
* build produced: NO
* ROM path: N/A
* root cause confirmed: YES
* fix implemented: NO
* no unrelated changes: YES

Build 36 baseline confirmed: YES.
- fg_cwindow_live count=0 (FG C-window crash eliminated).
- opcode_replace_count=56 (number-renderer hook at 0x03C2E2 installed).
- Execution runs full 1798 MAME frames without trapping to runtime_genesis_pc 0x000010 — NO BLACK screen, NO Error Trigger spam.
- Build 36 Exodus output still INCORRECT — shows red/blue bars, then green hatched fill, then dark green steady state. NO arcade text, title, score, or item graphics at any frame.

Arcade comparison used: YES (states/reference/rastan_arcade_60s/).
- Arcade frames sampled: 0090 (RASTAN title screen), 0300 (item list AXE/HAMMER/FIRE SWORD/SHIELD), 0400 (item list continuation), 0500 (scrolling text continuation).
- Arcade shows dense colored text + item sprites on dark background. Build 36 shows uniform synthetic patterns.

Build 36 sampled frames: 0001, 0090, 0180, 0300, 0350, 0400, 0500, 0596 (last). All Exodus rendering panels examined: VDP Image Window, Plane Viewer (Layer A + Layer B), VRAM Pattern Viewer, VDP Palette, Console log.

First divergence: TOTAL from frame 0090 onward. No frame shows any recognizable arcade content (no letter glyphs, score digits, title art, item sprites).

Primary rendering failure selected: WRONG TILE GRAPHICS / VRAM CONTENT.
- VRAM Pattern Viewer shows only synthetic bringup tiles (checker/hatch/solid from init_staging_state and placeholder scene-preload data) across all sampled frames.
- NO recognizable arcade font characters, sprite tiles, or title art present in VRAM at any point.
- Plane Viewer confirms nametable entries ARE populated and DO change across frames (bars → hatch → dark fill), proving hooks ARE writing translated tilemap indices to staged_fg_buffer and commits ARE reaching VRAM plane maps.
- But the VRAM tile positions those nametable entries reference contain WRONG GRAPHICS (synthetic patterns instead of arcade glyphs).
- vdp_ports_live count=26468 confirms VDP pipeline healthy through all 1798 frames.

Why other categories are NOT primary:
- Wrong tilemap/nametable mapping: REFUTED — planes reference tiles and content changes across frames. Mapping pipeline works.
- Wrong palette/CRAM: SECONDARY — greyscale-ramp placeholder from Build 113 palette_pre_conversion is present. But even with correct palette, tile shapes would be checker/hatch, not text. Palette produces wrong colors; tiles produce wrong shapes. Wrong shapes is more fundamental.
- Wrong plane/layer composition: REFUTED — both Layer A and B visible in Plane Viewer; display OFF/ON bracket working; plane bases set correctly in vdp_boot_setup.
- Intended content never produced: PARTIALLY REFUTED — hooks DO produce nametable writes (plane content changes prove this). What was never produced is the VRAM TILE GRAPHICS — the scene-preload/tile-loading pipeline has not delivered correct arcade tile pattern data.

Secondary issue: VDP palette (CRAM) is Build-113 greyscale-ramp placeholder. Even after VRAM tile fix, colors will be wrong until arcade palette-write path is hooked.

Evidence completeness: FULL. All evidence sources present and examined.
STOP triggered: NO.

Next-step recommendation (analysis only):
- Audit the tile-loading pipeline: scene-preload binaries (pc080sn_scene_preload_*.bin), tile LUT consistency, and whether the arcade expects additional runtime tile loads beyond what the preloader covers.
- Audit the palette pipeline: whether the arcade's palette-write path needs a real-time interception hook (the current Build-113 placeholder greyscale ramp is not connected to arcade palette intent).

## [Andy — Analysis, Tile Index Alignment Audit]

* files changed: docs/design/Andy_tile_index_alignment_audit.md (new)
* build produced: NO
* ROM path: N/A
* root cause confirmed: YES — PRIOR DIAGNOSIS REVISED. The primary rendering failure is WRONG PALETTE / CRAM USAGE, NOT wrong tile graphics / VRAM content.
* fix implemented: NO
* no unrelated changes: YES

Concrete tile trace completed: YES.
- Traced tile: arcade tile code 0x30 (digit '0')
- LUT lookup: genesistan_pc080sn_tile_vram_lut[0x0030] = 28 (VRAM slot 28, address 0x0380)
- Preload check: title scene manifest (build/pc080sn_scene_preload_title.bin) contains pair (source=0x30, dest=28) — MATCH with LUT
- Tile ROM content at genesistan_pc080sn_tile_rom offset 0x30*32: valid non-zero glyph data (digit '0' shape: 0001110000100110011000110110001101100011001100100001110000000000)
- attr_lut[0] = 0x0000 → palette line 0, no flip/priority
- Combined nametable word: 0x001C (tile index 28, palette 0)

Additional tiles verified (7 total — 0x20, 0x30, 0x31, 0x32, 0x39, 0x41, 0x4C):
- ALL have LUT value == preload manifest dest value (no mismatch for ANY checked tile)
- ALL glyph tiles (except 0x20 space) have valid non-zero pixel data in tile ROM
- Tile 0x0180 (blank fill) maps to slot 0 — not in preload (expected: slot 0 is blank by convention)

nametable_index found: 28 (for digit '0')
vram_content_at_index: REAL ARCADE TILE (digit '0' glyph with pixel values 0,1,2,3,6)
real_tile_index found: 28 — same as LUT output (NO MISMATCH)
mismatch confirmed: NO — tile index alignment is CORRECT

Root cause of rendering failure: WRONG PALETTE / CRAM USAGE.
- Tile 0x30 pixel values {0,1,2,3,6} index into CRAM palette line 0.
- Palette line 0 = Build-113 greyscale-ramp placeholder: entry 0=black, entry 1=0x0222 (dark green), entry 2=0x0444, entry 3=0x0666, etc.
- Result: digit '0' renders as dark green shape on black background — not readable as a digit at Exodus thumbnail resolution.
- A screen full of correctly-shaped but green-tinted glyphs appears as "green hatched pattern" — this was MISIDENTIFIED in prior analysis (Andy_build_36_exodus_rendering_analysis.md §5) as "synthetic bringup tiles."

Prior diagnosis corrected:
- Andy_build_36_exodus_rendering_analysis.md §5 concluded "wrong tile graphics / VRAM content" → REFUTED by concrete trace evidence. Tile graphics ARE in VRAM at correct positions.
- Correct diagnosis: wrong palette / CRAM usage. The greyscale-ramp placeholder in CRAM makes real text/digit glyphs unrecognizable.

Trace correlation: YES.
- vdp_ports_live count=26468 confirms VDP pipeline healthy.
- load_scene_tiles runs at boot during display-OFF, preloading 841 title-scene tiles.
- init_staging_state writes 3 synthetic tiles to VRAM slots 1-3 (no conflict with preload range starting at slot 20).
- Frame transitions (bars→hatch→dark green) correspond to hooks writing new nametable entries into staged_fg_buffer: initially FG is blank (all-zero), then hooks populate it with translated indices, then the "hatched" appearance is actually dense text content in wrong colors.

Root cause selected: NONE of the three prompt options (A=LUT wrong, B=preload mismatch, C=mixed pipeline) — all refuted by evidence. Actual root cause: WRONG PALETTE.
- Option A (LUT wrong): REFUTED — LUT values match preload dest for all 7 checked tiles.
- Option B (preload mismatch): REFUTED — preload pairs generated by same slot-assignment code as LUT.
- Option C (mixed pipeline): REFUTED — single load path, no overlap between synthetic tiles (slots 1-3) and preload range (slots 20+).

Implementation readiness: BLOCKED — further analysis required.
- Fix target is clear: replace Build-113 greyscale-ramp with correct arcade palette translation.
- Two implementation paths: (1) static palette preload (one-shot fix for attract), (2) runtime palette interception (structurally correct per Rainbow Islands model).
- Next Andy prompt should determine which approach and produce the corresponding spec.

Open questions:
1. Which arcade palette entries does the attract mode use? (Requires identifying the arcade palette-write code path)
2. Does the palette_pre_conversion step run in Build 36? (It should — but is staged_palette_words populated from the correct source?)
3. Is there an existing palette-write interception hook in the current hook set? (Check handler_translation_coverage.md)
