- FULL transition to structured text output occurs between frames 0161 and 0274 (row-structured purple content visible at 0274 = hooks actively writing translated tilemap indices from attract-mode text screens).

Arcade structural comparison:
- Frame 0078 vs arcade: NO structural features present (bars ≠ arcade text/title content).
- Frame 0161 vs arcade: NO structural features present (uniform red ≠ arcade text/story screens).
- Frame 0274 vs arcade: PARTIAL structural matching. Row-organized layout with within-row fine repetition MATCHES arcade text screen layout (score table or item description screens show text in horizontal rows). Colors wrong (purple vs white), individual glyphs not legible at thumbnail resolution. Upper ~1/4 black (header row region absent or outside scroll window).

Synthesis:
- Frame 0274's purple row-structured output is observably DIFFERENT from frame 0078's bars and frame 0161's uniform red — proving active rendering IS producing structured tilemap content.
- The row structure in frame 0274 matches the arcade's text-screen layout pattern.
- Colors are wrong: purple/magenta instead of white/colored text on black.
- The VRAM speckle increasing from frame 0078 → 0161 → 0274 confirms tiles being loaded and potentially reloaded.
- Palette panel shows change between frame 0161 and 0274 (purple/magenta bias acquired), indicating arcade palette writes were captured but produced wrong Genesis CRAM values.
- Corroborates tile-index audit: tile indices correct, VRAM tile graphics present, palette wrong.
- Post-transition at ≈MAME frame 540: CPU hits trap at runtime_genesis_pc 0x000010 (from unhooked non-FG hardware write path — possibly PC090OJ sprite RAM at 0xD00000+ or other).

STOP triggered: NO.

## [Andy — Analysis, Tile Mapping Audit]

* files changed: docs/design/Andy_tile_mapping_audit.md (new)
* build produced: NO
* ROM path: N/A
* root cause confirmed: YES — tile mapping is structurally correct; failure is CRAM palette content
* fix implemented: NO
* no unrelated changes: YES

Phase 1 — Selected tiles: 3 samples at (row 19, col 13), (row 21, col 13), (row 30, col 40) — derived from number-renderer ROM table entries 0, 1, 3.

Phase 2 — Nametable word construction:
- STOP condition: hex values in extended screenshot VRAM/nametable editors not legible at available resolution.
- Nametable words proven from SOURCE CODE PATH + BINARY LUT DATA instead:
  - tile_lut[0x0030] = 0x001C (tile 28, pal 0, no flip)
  - attr_lut[0] = 0x0000 (no bits set)
  - Combined D1 = 0x001C | 0x0000 = 0x001C
  - Written to staged_fg_buffer[19*64+13] via .Ltw_store_cell
- Verified for 6 additional tile codes (0x31, 0x41, 0x4C, 0x52, 0x53, 0x54): all produce correct nametable words with tile indices 29, 43, 54, 59, 60, 61 respectively, all palette 0, no flip.

Phase 3 — VRAM content at tile indices:
- Tile index 28 (digit '0'): correct glyph shape verified from ROM binary (oval outline, 6px tall, pixel values 0-3).
- Tile index 43 (letter 'A'): correct glyph shape (crossbar at row 4).
- Tile index 59 (letter 'R'): correct glyph shape.
- Preload manifest pairs match LUT for all 7 checked tiles (source=arcade_tile, dest=genesis_vram_slot; LUT[arcade_tile] == preload_dest for every checked case).

Phase 4 — Known-tile reference test:
- Tile 28 IS referenced by plane map (hook writes 0x001C to staged_fg_buffer at table-entry-0 position).
- VBlank commit transfers row to VRAM Plane A.
- Conclusion: mapping partially correct = YES (index generation correct, referencing correct).

Phase 5 — Failure class: NONE OF A–E.
- A (wrong index): REFUTED — 7 tiles verified LUT/preload match, no bitfield corruption.
- B (wrong VRAM): REFUTED — binary shows correct glyph shapes at correct positions.
- C (attribute mispack): REFUTED — attr_lut[0]=0x0000 for common case; extraction code verified for all 16 entries.
- D (flip): REFUTED — no flip bits set for attr_index=0.
- E (plane base): REFUTED — Plane A at 0xE000 correct.

Actual failure: CRAM palette content. All four palette lines contain Build-113 greyscale ramp (0x0000, 0x0222, 0x0444, 0x0666...) instead of arcade text colors. Pixel values 1-3 render as dark green-grey tones instead of white/bright — producing near-invisible glyphs that appear as colored noise at Exodus thumbnail resolution.

Extended screenshots (10 images) corroborate:
- Same structural layout (horizontal text rows) persists across ALL color changes (purple, teal, blue, red/green, green).
- CRAM panels in different frames show DIFFERENT color entries — palette cycling occurs as arcade code writes different palette states for different attract-mode screens.
- "Banding" in output is text-row structure (normal for text rendering), not an artifact of wrong tile mapping.
- "Repeated patterns" are character cells within rows (normal for text layout), not single-index-repeating bugs.

Phase 6 — Cross-frame consistency:
- Frame 0078: pre-transition, BG checkerboard visible, FG blank. Indices not yet written.
- Frame 0161: early active, scene tiles loaded, FG writes starting. Indices beginning to populate.
- Frame 0274: active rendering, row-structured text content in FG. Indices correct; colors wrong.
- Index consistency: LUT is static ROM data, stable across all frames.
- VRAM stability: tile graphics loaded at boot, no runtime replacement.

STOP condition: PARTIAL (hex values not readable from screenshots; compensated with binary/code analysis).

## [Andy — CORRECTION, Tile Mapping Audit Revision]

* files changed: (inline AGENTS_LOG correction)
* build produced: NO
* ROM path: N/A
* root cause confirmed: YES — REVISED AGAIN. Prior "palette only" diagnosis is WRONG.
* fix implemented: NO
* no unrelated changes: YES

### CRITICAL CORRECTION

The prior tile mapping audit concluded "tile mapping is structurally correct; failure is CRAM palette content." **This conclusion is WRONG.**

Evidence that refutes the palette-only hypothesis:

The text-script handlers (0x03C4D2 family) compute tile codes as:
  `tile_code = raw_script_byte + A4@(26) + A4@(24)`

When A4@(26) is a nonzero offset (e.g., 0x2700 — common for Rastan text rendering), the produced tile codes are in the 0x2720+ range, NOT the 0x0020+ range.

Direct tile-shape comparison for THREE key characters:

**Digit '0' (raw 0x30 vs offset 0x2730):**
- RAW (0x30 → VRAM slot 28): CORRECT '0' glyph — oval outline, 6px tall, recognizable digit
- OFFSET (0x2730 → VRAM slot 829): WRONG — sparse tile with ~4 isolated pixels in right column. NOT a digit.

**Letter 'A' (raw 0x41 vs offset 0x2741):**
- RAW (0x41 → VRAM slot 43): CORRECT 'A' glyph — triangle with crossbar, recognizable letter
- OFFSET (0x2741 → VRAM slot 846): WRONG — nearly all-zero tile with single pixel at bottom-right. NOT a letter.

**Letter 'R' (raw 0x52 vs offset 0x2752):**
- RAW (0x52 → VRAM slot 59): CORRECT 'R' glyph — vertical stem with loop, recognizable letter
- OFFSET (0x2752 → VRAM slot 375): WRONG — ALL pixels filled (solid block, every nibble = max value). NOT a letter.

Systematic check across printable ASCII range (0x20-0x5A):
- RAW tile codes (0x20-0x5A): most have glyph-like shapes (heuristic: 4-50 nonzero pixels, >2 unique byte values)
- OFFSET tile codes (0x2720-0x275A): most do NOT have glyph-like shapes — they contain unrelated arcade graphics (scenery tiles, solid fills, sparse pixels)

### Corrected root cause

**Failure class B — Wrong VRAM tile content at the OFFSET tile code positions.**

The tile ROM (`genesistan_pc080sn_tile_rom`) at indices 0x2720-0x277F does NOT contain the expected text font glyphs. It contains unrelated arcade tile graphics. When the text-script hooks add A4@(26) to shift raw character codes into the 0x2720+ range, the resulting tile_lut lookups return VRAM slots that are loaded with NON-FONT tile data.

The number-renderer path (which uses raw codes 0x30-0x39 WITHOUT adding A4@(26)) IS correct — those raw positions DO contain valid digit glyphs.

### Why the prior palette diagnosis was wrong

The prior analysis verified tile shapes only for RAW tile codes (0x30, 0x41, 0x4C) — which are used by the number renderer. These ARE correct glyphs. But the text-script handlers use OFFSET codes (0x2730, 0x2741, 0x274C), which map to DIFFERENT tile ROM positions with WRONG content. The palette issue is real (greyscale ramp instead of correct colors) but it is SECONDARY — even with correct palette, the offset tiles would show garbage shapes (solid blocks, sparse pixels), not readable text.

### What the screen output actually shows

The visible "hatched/banded" pattern on screen IS wrong tile shapes — not correct glyphs in wrong colors. The solid-block tiles (like 0x2752 → all-0xFF) produce bright solid rectangles; the sparse tiles (like 0x2730) produce near-blank cells; the mix creates the banded/textured appearance.

### Next step required

Determine WHY the tile ROM at indices 0x2720-0x277F does not contain the expected font glyphs:
1. Does the arcade PC080SN tile ROM actually have font data at these indices?
2. Was the tile conversion (`precompute_pc080sn_tile_lut.py` / `preconvert_pc090oj_tiles.py`) incorrect for these indices?
3. Is A4@(26) actually 0x2700 at runtime, or is the base offset different?
4. Does the arcade font live at a different tile index range than 0x2720+?

This is the critical next investigation.

## [Andy — Analysis, Early Title Control Flow + Exception Transition]

* files changed: docs/design/Andy_early_title_control_flow_audit.md (new)
* build produced: NO
* ROM path: N/A
* root cause confirmed: YES
* fix implemented: NO
* no unrelated changes: YES

Pre-exception timeline constructed: YES (IMG_01 → IMG_03).
- IMG_01: PC=0x0007125C (boot init), A1=0xFFFFFFFF, A4=0xFFFFFFFF, E000=0xFFFF (uninitialized VRAM).
- IMG_02: PC=0x00070022 (main loop), A1=0x0003B2FB, A4=0xFFFFFFFF, E000=all zero (Plane A cleared by init).
- IMG_03: PC=0x00070022 (main loop), A1=0x0003C0BC, A4=0xFFFFFFFF, E000=all zero (unchanged).
- Last normal frame: IMG_03.
- First exception frame: IMG_04 (PC=0x00000200).

Plane A state before exception: ZERO.
- IMG_02 and IMG_03: 0xE000 region contains 0x0000 everywhere.
- No title-phase tilemap data populated before the exception.
- A4=0xFFFFFFFF in all pre-exception frames — text-script state system never initialized.

A1 progression vs VRAM: YES — A1 progresses (0x0003B2FB → 0x0003C0BC) while E000 remains zero.
- A1 values are genesis_rom_offset code pointers, NOT HW_ADDRESS/PC080SN/FG_TILEMAP destinations.
- A1 reflects general-purpose register usage during arcade tick, not FG destination state.

Write stage identified: NONE (destination never reached).
- No text-script handlers have fired (A4=0xFFFFFFFF).
- No staged writes to staged_fg_buffer occurred (fg_row_dirty would be 0).
- No VBlank FG strip commits populated 0xE000.
- Arcade state machine is in initialization countdown before text-rendering phase.

Root cause selected: A — Destination never reached.
- The arcade state machine has NOT progressed to its text-rendering phase before the exception.
- A4=0xFFFFFFFF proves text-script state was never initialized.
- The initialization countdown runs for hundreds of frames before text output.
- An exception from an unhooked hardware write kills execution before the transition.

Transition trigger identified: YES.
- IMG_03 → IMG_04: PC jumps 0x00070022 → 0x00000200 (default exception handler = RTE at preserved-vectors segment).
- A1 corrupts: 0x0003C0BC → 0x50205743 (invalid address; contains ASCII-like fragments 'P',' ','W','C').
- E000 goes from all-zero to partially populated with 0x0000/0x0011/0x5020/0x5741 pattern.
- All three changes appear simultaneously at IMG_04 — no intermediate state captured.

Corruption propagation confirmed: YES.
- Nametable at 0xE000 shows repeating pattern: 0x0000, 0x0011, 0x5020, 0x5741.
- Values 0x5020 and 0x5741 are fragments of corrupted A1=0x50205743 (high word = 0x5020; low word ≈ 0x5743 ± 2).
- VDP Port Monitor (IMG_05) confirms active DP Write of 0x0011, 0x5020, 0x5741 — these are being written to VDP during the exception loop.
- Stack overflow: SSP decreases by ~60K-80K per captured frame (0x00FC3C62 → 0x00FB49FC → 0x00F9E69E → 0x00F8AC1A) — infinite exception-RTE loop consuming stack.
- By IMG_07: PC=0x50205759 (executing from corrupted address derived from A1-like value) — irreversible failure.

Next investigation: identify the unhooked hardware write that triggers the initial exception between IMG_03 and IMG_04. Candidates: HW_ADDRESS/PC090OJ/SPRITE_RAM (0xD00000+), HW_ADDRESS/TC0040IOC (0x380000+), or other unmapped arcade hardware addresses. Hooking this write path would allow execution to survive past the initialization countdown and reach the text-rendering phase where the title screen would be displayed.

## [Andy — Analysis, IMG_02 Display Failure Audit]

* files changed: docs/design/Andy_img02_display_audit.md (new)
* build produced: NO
* ROM path: N/A
* root cause confirmed: YES
* fix implemented: NO
* no unrelated changes: YES

Which plane is driving the display at IMG_02: Plane B (0xC000).
- Plane A (0xE000) is ALL ZERO (confirmed: Cody raw sample 0x0000×4 at 0xE00A+) → transparent.
- Visible output is tile-based (alternating red/purple vertical bars with horizontal striping) → NOT a solid background fill.
- Since Plane A is transparent, all visible content must come from Plane B.

State of Plane A (0xE000) at IMG_02: ALL ZERO.
- Raw values: 0x0000 0x0000 0x0000 0x0000 (from Cody_vdp_ground_truth_build36_early.md line 151).

State of Plane B (0xC000) at IMG_02: NOT EXTRACTED by Cody.
- Cody's data covers only 0xE000 region status; no 0xC000 nametable samples were taken.
- Source code analysis: init_staging_state fills staged_bg_buffer with checkerboard (tile indices 1 and 2) but clears bg_row_dirty to 0 (line 2051), so the VBlank BG strip commit SKIPS (no dirty bits set). VRAM Plane B at 0xC000 retains pre-init state (emulator-dependent).

Why title is not visible — evidence from register state:
- A4 = 0xFFFFFFFF (Cody line 28): text-script state pointer NOT INITIALIZED. The arcade attract-mode state machine has not reached the text-rendering phase. No text-script handlers have fired. No staged_fg_buffer writes have occurred. fg_row_dirty = 0. No VBlank FG strip commits have populated Plane A.
- D0 = 0x00000007 (Cody line 26): early frame count (7 frames into main loop). Hundreds of frames of initialization countdown remain before text output.
- PC = 0x00070022 (Cody line 24): CPU in main-loop wait (between frame_counter compare and arcade_tick_logic call). Normal execution, not trapped.

Selected failure classification: D — Startup sequencing delay — title rendering has not yet been triggered.
- This is EXPECTED pre-title behavior, not a rendering failure. The arcade's initialization countdown runs for hundreds of frames before the first text-script handler fires.
- The ACTUAL rendering failure occurs later, when an exception from an unhooked hardware write (between IMG_03 and IMG_04) prevents the state machine from ever reaching the text phase.
- Why not A: Plane A SHOULD NOT be populated yet (A4 proves text state never initialized — correct for this timeline position).
- Why not B: Plane B IS the visible source because Plane A is correctly empty at this point.
- Why not C: Plane B content not extracted; cannot assess its initialization correctness.
- Why not E: sufficient evidence exists (A4=0xFFFFFFFF + D0=7 + Plane A zero = startup delay).

Attached image examined at full resolution BEFORE any file read: YES.

VDP Image orientation corrected: YES.
- Prior: described as "alternating red/purple vertical bands" (based on examining wrong image at low resolution).
- Corrected: 2D CHECKERBOARD GRID of alternating RED and PURPLE/BLUE rectangular blocks. Both horizontal AND vertical alternation. Black region at top.

Plane A Plane Viewer content: NON-ZERO.
- Shows DARK RED/MAROON uniform fill — NOT black/empty/transparent.
- CONTRADICTS prior conclusion "Plane A is all-zero → transparent."
- CONTRADICTS Cody's 4-word spot sample at 0xE00A+ which showed 0x0000×4.
- Likely cause: tile 0 with CRAM palette entry 0 being non-black (dark red), OR non-zero nametable entries outside the spot-sampled region.

Plane B Plane Viewer content: NON-ZERO.
- Shows ALTERNATING RED AND PURPLE HORIZONTAL BANDS — clearly structured striping.
- Consistent with init_staging_state checkerboard (tile indices 1 and 2) being committed to Plane B, OR Plane B retaining boot-state VRAM content.

Prior conclusions contradicted: YES — 3 of 4.
1. "VDP Image shows vertical bars": CONTRADICTED — image shows 2D checkerboard grid.
2. "Plane A is all-zero / transparent": CONTRADICTED — Plane Viewer shows dark red fill in Layer A.
3. "Plane B is the sole display source": CONTRADICTED — both layers contribute; VDP Image is composite of Layer A fill + Layer B banding.
4. "Failure class D (startup delay)": UNVERIFIABLE — register state (A4=0xFFFFFFFF) supports startup timing, but Plane A non-empty contradicts the premise that "Plane A is correctly empty at this point."

Failure classification changed: YES.
- Prior: D (startup sequencing delay).
- Corrected: C (Plane B incorrectly initialized producing wrong output).
- Evidence: Layer B contains structured horizontal banding NOT produced by text-script hooks (A4=0xFFFFFFFF). BG plane has synthetic checkerboard content that should not be visible at this execution point. The displayed output is a WRONG display, not an expected pre-title state.

Cody spot sample reconciliation: the 4-word sample at 0xE00A+ does NOT represent the full 2048-word Plane A nametable. The Plane Viewer's visible non-black content across the full Layer A extent contradicts the assumption that the entire plane is zero.

## [Andy — Analysis, Test Pattern Execution Verification]

* files changed: docs/design/Andy_test_pattern_execution_verification.md (new)
* build produced: NO
* ROM path: N/A
* root cause confirmed: YES
* fix implemented: NO
* no unrelated changes: YES

`init_staging_state` execution confirmed: YES — via caller chain. Boot sequence: main_68k (0x070000) calls load_scene_tiles (confirmed by trace: exec_enter pc=071252 at frame 1, inside load_scene_tiles body), then immediately calls init_staging_state (0x071298) before enabling interrupts (SR=0x2700 → 0x2000). No code path skips it. Interrupts disabled means no preemption.

`tile_init_words` tiles committed to VRAM: YES — tiles 1, 2, 3.
- Tile 1: 16×0x1111 → every pixel = value 1 → palette entry 1 = 0x000E = BRIGHT RED.
- Tile 2: 16×0x2222 → every pixel = value 2 → palette entry 2 = 0x00E0 = BRIGHT GREEN.
- Tile 3: alternating 0x3030/0x0303 → checkerboard pattern.
- Committed via tiles_dirty=1 (set by init line 2050) → first VBlank vdp_commit_tiles_if_dirty writes 48 words to VRAM at VRAM_TILE_BASE=0x00000020 (tile indices 1-3).

`palette_init_words` committed to CRAM: YES.
- palette_dirty=1 (set by init line 2049) → first VBlank vdp_commit_palette writes 64 words from palette_init_words to CRAM.
- Palette line 0: 0x0000(black), 0x000E(RED), 0x00E0(GREEN), 0x0E00(BLUE), 0x00EE(yellow), 0x0E0E(magenta), 0x0EE0(cyan), 0x020C, + 8 more entries.
- CRITICAL: this is NOT a greyscale ramp. It is a DIAGNOSTIC COLOR PALETTE — bright primary/secondary colors. Prior analyses incorrectly described this as "greyscale ramp from Build 113 palette_pre_conversion." The actual init palette has distinct red, green, blue, yellow, magenta, cyan entries.

`bg_row_dirty` state after init: 0 (cleared at line 2051).
- First VBlank: vdp_commit_bg_strips_if_dirty SKIPS (bg_row_dirty=0 → beq exits immediately).
- The checkerboard in staged_bg_buffer (tile 1/tile 2 alternating) is NOT committed on the first VBlank.
- Committed LATER: when genesistan_hook_cwindow_clear fires during arcade tick, it sets bg_row_dirty=0xFFFFFFFF (line 1763). BUT c-window clear also OVERWRITES staged_bg_buffer with tile index 20 (translated space = 0x0014, all-zero pixels). So what gets committed is tile 20 everywhere (transparent → CRAM[0] = black), NOT the checkerboard.

BG strip commit confirmed before IMG_02: UNRESOLVED.
- If c-window clear fires before IMG_02: Plane B receives tile 20 everywhere = black. Contradicts visible red/purple pattern.
- If c-window clear has NOT fired: bg_row_dirty still 0, no BG commit, Plane B retains emulator VRAM power-on state.
- Neither scenario clearly explains the structured red/purple banding at IMG_02.
- BG strip hooks (genesistan_hook_tilemap_plane_a) may also set dirty bits during early arcade ticks, causing partial commits of the still-checkerboard staged_bg_buffer.

Correlation with IMG_02 output: YES (partial).
- Red component: tile 1 = all-pixel-value-1, palette entry 1 = 0x000E = red. Confirmed match.
- Purple component: NOT explained by palette_init_words entry 2 = 0x00E0 = green. Purple implies CRAM has been modified since init. Cody's data confirms "IMG_02: [CRAM] changed" from baseline.
- The arcade code modifies WRAM (trace: wram_ff0000 changes from frame 60+). If staged_palette_words is within WRAM and arcade code writes to it, the next VBlank palette commit (if palette_dirty is set again) would update CRAM with arcade-modified values.

Final classification: A — Synthetic test pattern is actively executing AND committed to VDP.
- Tile 1 (red) and tile 2 (green-or-modified) are the synthetic tiles producing the visible pattern.
- palette_init_words colors determine the visible colors (red confirmed; green/purple explained by CRAM modification after initial commit).
- Plane A = all zero (direct VRAM clear at init lines 2091-2096) = transparent.
- Visible output = Plane B content rendered through synthetic tiles + modified palette.
- The synthetic system IS the source of the wrong display. It must be removed or gated for correct rendering.

CORRECTION TO PRIOR ANALYSES: palette_init_words is NOT a greyscale ramp. It is a diagnostic color palette with bright red (0x000E), green (0x00E0), blue (0x0E00), yellow (0x00EE), magenta (0x0E0E), cyan (0x0EE0). All prior references to "Build-113 greyscale-ramp placeholder" as the palette source were WRONG — that was a separate system (palette_pre_conversion in postpatch_startup_rom.py) that writes to genesistan_palette_rom_table in ROM, NOT to staged_palette_words which is the active CRAM source.

- Final PC: 0x000202
- Stack Pointer (SP): 0x00DEA640
- Unique Unmapped Memory Addresses: none

## [Andy — Design, First-Fault Crash Handler Spec]

* files changed: docs/design/Andy_crash_handler_spec.md (new)
* build produced: NO
* ROM path: N/A
* root cause confirmed: N/A
* fix implemented: NO
* no unrelated changes: YES

Vectors covered: ALL 60 vectors currently routing to _default_handler (vectors 2-29, 31, 32-63). Only vector 30 (_VINT_handler) unchanged. Unique stubs recommended for vectors 2-11 and TRAP 0-15 (28 stubs); grouped stubs for remaining low-value vectors.

CRASH_ACTIVE_FLAG address: 0x00FF6800 (byte at CRASH_RECORD_BASE + 0x00).
CRASH_RECORD_BASE address: 0x00FF6800.
- Conflict check: BSS at 0xFF4000, ends ~0xFF6108. Arcade workram at 0xFF0000-0xFF3FFF. Record at 0xFF6800 has 0x6F8 bytes margin above BSS end. No conflicts.

Total crash record size: 0x6C bytes (108).
- All longword fields confirmed at even addresses.
- Layout includes: lockout flag, exception type, stacked SR/PC, SP at entry, USP, D0-D7, A0-A6, frame counter, fault address (bus/addr only), access type, project-specific state (DEST_BG, DEST_FG, BG_ROW_DIRTY, FG_ROW_DIRTY, PALETTE_DIRTY, TILES_DIRTY).

Rendering approach: EMBEDDED 1BPP FONT (768 bytes, 96 ASCII chars 0x20-0x7F).
- Expanded to 4bpp at render time, written directly to VRAM via VDP_DATA.
- No dependency on existing VRAM tile data, staging buffers, DMA, VBlank, or dirty flags.
- VDP reinit sequence: 14 register writes to establish known-good H40 state with display ON, V-int OFF, auto-inc 2.
- Crash palette: CRAM line 0 entry 0=black (0x0000), entry 1=white (0x0EEE). Written directly.
- Fallback: if VDP access fails → lockout halt → CRAM[0]=black → solid black screen (stopped crashing).

Backtrace: STACK DUMP ONLY — raw 16 longwords from SP. No heuristic frame-pointer scan. 68000 provides no guaranteed frame pointers; translation engine synthetic frames make heuristic scan unreliable.

Lockout flag position in execution order: confirmed as instruction 4 in _crash_common (after SR mask, SP copy, lockout CHECK at instruction 3). Stub writes CRASH_EXCEPTION_TYPE as the very first action (before _crash_common), using direct WRAM byte write to preserve D0 for later register capture.

Per-vector stub pattern (register-preserving variant):
```
_crash_stub_bus_error:
    move.b  #2, 0x00FF6802      ; write vector ID to CRASH_EXCEPTION_TYPE
    bra.w   _crash_common
```

Exception frame decode:
- Standard frame (vec 4+): SR at A0+0 (word), PC at A0+2 (long).
- Bus/address error (vec 2-3): access type at A0+0 (unreliable), fault address at A0+2 (reliable long), instr reg at A0+6 (unreliable), SR at A0+8 (word), PC at A0+10 (long).
- Handler branches on CRASH_EXCEPTION_TYPE to select decode path.

Register save: individual move.l instructions (NOT movem.l to temp area).
- `move.l %d0, CRASH_D0` through `move.l %a6, CRASH_A6` — 15 instructions.
- No temporary stack area needed. Simpler and sufficient since registers are never restored.

Halt sequence:
```
.Lcrash_halt:
    stop    #0x2700
    bra.s   .Lcrash_halt
```

Crash screen layout: 40-column H40 mode, rows 0-21 + row 27. Includes exception name, vector number, fault PC/SR, fault address (bus/addr only), all registers, project-specific state, 16-longword stack dump, and "HALTED — BUILD 0038" footer. All tiles at priority 1 to overlay any existing plane content.

Ready for Cody implementation: YES.

## [Andy — Analysis, Crash Handler Spec Revision]

* files changed: docs/design/Andy_crash_handler_spec.md (updated in-place)
* build produced: NO
* ROM path: N/A
* root cause confirmed: N/A
* fix implemented: NO
* no unrelated changes: YES

Correction 1 applied: YES — stub pattern updated to `moveq #N, %d0` / `bra.w _crash_common`. All stub variants that wrote to WRAM removed. Common handler prologue updated: vector ID written to CRASH_EXCEPTION_TYPE at step 6, AFTER lockout check (steps 3-4) and lockout set (step 5).
D0 sacrifice documented: YES — fault-time D0 is lost; CRASH_D0 stores the vector number. All other registers D1-D7, A1-A6 reflect fault-time state. A0 is also sacrificed (overwritten by SP copy at step 2); CRASH_SP_AT_ENTRY stores the original SP value instead.

Correction 2 applied: YES — SP resolution added as execution order step 13: `lea 0x00FFFF00, %sp`. Inserted between "store project-specific state" (step 12) and "VDP reinit" (step 14).
Safe SP address 0x00FFFF00 confirmed conflict-free:
- CRASH_RECORD_BASE at 0xFF6800-0xFF686B: no overlap.
- BSS at 0xFF4000-0xFF6108: no overlap.
- Arcade workram at 0xFF0000-0xFF3FFF: no overlap.
- Stack grows down from 0xFFFF00 into 0xFFFExx — well within WRAM, above all allocated regions.
- Crash renderer requires minimal stack depth (1-2 bsr levels for hex formatting); 256 bytes sufficient.
Full updated execution order documented: 18 steps from SR mask through halt.

Correction 3 applied: YES — font expansion method specified as inline direct-to-VRAM.
PC080SN glyph reuse excluded from primary path: YES — embedded 1bpp font is the ONLY path.
Exact bit expansion sequence specified in assembly: 8-iteration loop per byte, `btst` per bit, `lsl.l #4` per nibble, `ori.b #1` for set pixels, `move.l %d1, VDP_DATA` per row. No RAM scratch buffer.
VRAM crash font base: 0x8000 (tile index 1024). Between scene-preload cache A (max tile 1023) and cache B (min tile 1280). No conflict confirmed.
VDP command longwords specified: 0x40000002 for VRAM write at 0x8000; 0x60000003 for VRAM write at 0xE000.
Nametable target: Plane A (0xE000) — drawn on top of Plane B by default; priority 1 set on all crash tiles.
Nametable entry formula: `0x8400 + (ascii - 0x20)` = priority 1 + tile 1024+offset.

Correction 4 applied: YES — explicit frame decode branch on D0 added.
Branch instruction: `cmpi.b #3, %d0` / `bhi.s .Lstandard_frame`.
Bus/address error path: reads at A0+0 (access type), A0+2 (fault addr), A0+6 (instr reg), A0+8 (SR), A0+10 (PC). Stores to CRASH_FAULT_ADDRESS, CRASH_ACCESS_TYPE, CRASH_STACKED_SR, CRASH_STACKED_PC.
Standard frame path: reads at A0+0 (SR), A0+2 (PC). CRASH_FAULT_ADDRESS/CRASH_ACCESS_TYPE left as 0.
Branch on CRASH_EXCEPTION_TYPE removed — branch uses D0 directly (still live from stub).

Ready for Cody implementation: YES.

* files changed:

* root cause confirmed: YES
* fix implemented: YES
* no unrelated changes: YES (within this task scope)

Technical detail:
- Phase 0 loop termination validation (required PCs):
  - `0x00000404` / `0x0000040C` map to immediate-extension words in `_crash_common` call/setup sequence, not standalone loop heads.
  - `0x000008AA` / `0x000008B2` / `0x000008B8` map inside `crash_clear_plane_a`; active loop is `.Lclear_loop` (`dbra %d7,.Lclear_loop` at `0x000008BC`).
  - `.Lclear_loop` counter trace (5 iterations):
    - iter1: `%d7=0x045F` -> dbra -> `0x045E`
    - iter2: `%d7=0x045E` -> dbra -> `0x045D`
    - iter3: `%d7=0x045D` -> dbra -> `0x045C`
    - iter4: `%d7=0x045C` -> dbra -> `0x045B`
    - iter5: `%d7=0x045B` -> dbra -> `0x045A`
    - termination condition: exits when `%d7` becomes `0xFFFF` (finite, 1120 writes).
- Additional renderer loop audit (root-cause loop bug):
  - In `crash_render_screen` `.Lstack_col_loop`, `%d6` is the column counter.
  - Pre-fix, `crash_extract_top_nibble` clobbered `%d6` as scratch, so the caller's loop counter was overwritten by nibble data from `%d4` each iteration.
  - This violated deterministic loop progression for `.Lstack_col_loop` (counter integrity bug).
- Fix applied:
  - `crash_extract_top_nibble` scratch register changed `%d6 -> %d3`.
  - This preserves `%d6` as the stack-column loop counter across `crash_put_hex32_inline` calls.
- D0 loop-counter audit:
  - D0 used as loop counter without reinit: NO.
  - D0 remains vector-ID carrier on crash entry and is explicitly reinitialized before local use.
- VDP register/command verification (pre-fix):
  - `R1=0x8134`, `R2=0x8238`, `R4=0x8406`, `R7=0x8700`, `R12=0x8C81`, `R15=0x8F02`.
  - CRAM command: `0xC0000000` (correct).
  - Font VRAM command (`0x8000`): `0x40000002` (correct by formula).
  - Plane A nametable command (`0xE000`): `0x60000003` (correct by formula).
  - Write order: command-before-data order is correct in CRAM, font upload, and nametable paths; no DATA/CTRL mixup found.
- Phase-5 root-cause class selection:
  - Selected: `E` (loop-control failure inside renderer path).
  - Other classes (`A/B/C/D`) not supported by code evidence in current source.
- Build/verification:
  - command: `source tools/setup_env.sh && make -C apps/rastan-direct -B release`
  - result: PASS
  - numbered build produced: `dist/rastan-direct/rastan_direct_video_test_build_0040.bin`
  - automatic trace produced: `states/traces/rastan_direct_video_test_build_0040_mame_30s_20260417_152031`

Anomalies:
- MAME trace PC sampler continues to report addresses inside crash handler immediate words (`0x0000040X`, `0x000008A/BX`) after first fault; this instrumentation behavior predates this fix and was not changed in this task.

## [Andy — Analysis, D-Register Base Pointer Origin Trace]

* files changed: docs/design/Andy_d_register_base_pointer_trace.md (new)
* build produced: NO
* ROM path: N/A
* root cause confirmed: YES
* fix implemented: NO
* no unrelated changes: YES

D-register pattern identified as: NOT a single instruction load — total register corruption from CPU executing ROM header ASCII text as 68000 instructions after a fault cascade (Exodus emulator). The structured D-register values (0x196, 0x1AE, ...) are accumulated side effects of garbage-code execution in the 0x100-0x200 ROM header area.

Base register identified: NO single base register. All registers corrupted. The Exodus crash data shows the POST-CASCADE state, not the initial fault state.

Base register value at time of load: N/A (cascade corruption, not single load)

Origin of bad value — MAME trace provides the CLEAN first-fault data:
- CRASH_FAULT_ADDRESS = 0x50205741 (written to WRAM at 0xFF6854-0xFF6857 at MAME frame 386)
- 0x50205741 = ASCII text fragment ('P',' ','W','A') — a data value used as a pointer, not a valid address
- The trace's "reg_d01bfe" and "reg_350008" symbol watches at ff6854/ff6856 are monitoring the crash record CRASH_FAULT_ADDRESS field, not actual arcade hardware registers — the symbol names are misleading overlaps

Root cause class: E — State machine transition at frame ~386 loading a bad base from an unhooked or incorrectly translated structure.
Evidence:
- MAME frame 386: crash handler writes CRASH_FAULT_ADDRESS = 0x50205741 (visible as reg_d01bfe and reg_350008 symbol changes in trace)
- Frame 386 = arcade attract-mode transition (same window as all prior builds: Build 36 at frame 389)
- fg_cwindow_live count=0 in Build 44 — FG C-window writers all hooked — crash from DIFFERENT unhooked path
- vdp_ports_live last_pc=0x0008AA, last_frame=390 — crash handler's VDP rendering confirmed executing
- After frame 390: only z80_ctrl changes every 30 frames — CPU halted by crash handler's stop #0x2700

Frame 386 correlation: YES — coincides with arcade attract-mode transition. Same timing window as Build 36 frame 389.

DEST_BG/FG origin identified: YES (in Exodus data only) — corrupted by post-fault cascade, not a separate root cause. DEST_BG=0x610 and DEST_FG=0x628 are corrupted WRAM values from garbage-code execution overwriting 0xFF10A0/0xFF10A4. In the MAME run (clean first-fault capture), DEST_BG/FG would retain their init values (0xC00000/0xC08000).

Exodus vs MAME discrepancy explained:
- MAME: catches first fault cleanly at frame 386 (fault addr 0x50205741, crash handler fires immediately)
- Exodus: handles initial fault differently, allowing cascade execution → CPU runs ROM header as code → all registers and WRAM corrupted → secondary address error caught with garbage state (fault addr 0x196, FRAME 0x0736, D0-D7 all low-ROM values)
- Both emulators crash at the SAME arcade transition point (~frame 386); the cascade severity differs

Next investigation: identify the unhooked arcade code path at the attract-mode transition that produces a memory access to 0x50205741. This is a data-as-pointer error — some instruction loads ASCII text from ROM/WRAM and uses it as an address. Trace the instruction that accesses 0x50205741 to its arcade_pc source.

## [Andy — Analysis, Stripe Pattern Root Cause Build 0046]

* files changed: docs/design/Andy_stripe_root_cause_build0046.md (new)
* build produced: NO
* ROM path: N/A
* root cause confirmed: YES
* fix implemented: NO
* no unrelated changes: YES

init_staging_state frequency: B (once per warm restart = once per frame). main_68k re-entered on every warm restart from arcade_pc 0x039F9E.

Warm restart overwrites staging before commit: YES.
- Per-frame ordering: VBlank (bg_row_dirty=0 → skip) → arcade tick (hook writes data + sets dirty) → warm restart (JMP main_68k → init_staging_state clears buffer + dirty) → next VBlank (bg_row_dirty=0 → skip again).
- Net result: BG strip hook output NEVER reaches VRAM because init_staging_state erases it before every VBlank.

Source tile selection repetitive: IRRELEVANT — data cleared before commit regardless.
strip_index execution coverage: IRRELEVANT — same reason.
%d7 constant or varying: IRRELEVANT — same reason.
Commit path fires correctly: NO — bg_row_dirty is always 0 at VBlank time because warm restart clears it.
Commit stride correct: YES — when it does fire (which is never due to dirty=0), stride is correct.
Plane mismatch: NO — Plane B at 0xC000 matches VDP register config.

Root cause selected: A — Warm restart causes init_staging_state to clear staging buffers after valid hook writes, so VBlank always commits cleared data.
Evidence: init_staging_state line 2054 (clr.l bg_row_dirty) + lines 2069-2073 (clear staged_bg_buffer). Called from main_68k line 81, which is re-entered on every warm restart.
Fix direction: split init_staging_state into boot-only and per-restart paths; per-restart must NOT clear staged_bg/fg_buffer or bg/fg_row_dirty.

Additional capture needed: NO.

Entry dispatch design:
```
main_68k:
    move.w  #0x2700, %sr
    tst.b   boot_initialized
    bne.s   .Lwarm_restart
    bsr     vdp_boot_setup
    moveq   #0, %d0
    bsr     load_scene_tiles
    bsr     init_boot_state
    move.b  #1, boot_initialized
    bra.s   .Lenter_main_loop
.Lwarm_restart:
    bsr     init_restart_state
.Lenter_main_loop:
    move.w  #0x2000, %sr
    ; .Lmain_loop unchanged
```

Prerequisite for Cody: verify `apps/rastan-direct/src/boot/boot.s` zero-initializes BSS (so `boot_initialized` starts at 0 on cold boot). If not, add BSS zero-init to boot.s; fallback is explicit `clr.b boot_initialized` as the very first main_68k instruction (before `tst.b`).

Scope kept strictly within `apps/rastan-direct/src/main_68k.s`. No STOP conditions triggered.

## [Andy — Design, Rastan-Direct Runtime Decomposition]

* files changed: docs/design/Andy_rastan_direct_runtime_decomposition.md (new)
* build produced: NO
* ROM path: N/A
* root cause confirmed: YES (current `main_68k` and `_VINT_handler` violate RULES §1, §3, §6, §7)
* fix implemented: NO
* no unrelated changes: YES

* permanent items added:
  - `_bootstrap` (boot.s — cold-boot helper, non-looping, JMPs to arcade_pc 0x3A000 permanently)
  - `_vblank_service` (vdp_comm.s — level-5 vector target; VDP commits then tail-JMP to arcade L5 handler)
  - New modules `vdp_comm.s`, `tilemap_hooks.s`, `scene_load.s`
* temporary items added: NONE
* diagnostic items added: NONE
* bringup-only items added: NONE
* scaffolding inventory documented: YES (none introduced; many existing violations removed)
* removal / revert plan documented: YES (N/A — all added items permanent; removals are RULES-mandated)
* no undocumented scaffolding added: YES

Minimum detail:
- architectural violations identified: 6
    1. `main_68k` / `.Lmain_loop` (Genesis-owned loop) — §1, §2, §4, §9
    2. `_VINT_handler` as top-level level-5 vector — §3, §1
    3. `arcade_tick_logic` (Genesis-side caller of arcade) — §1, §3, §4, §7
    4. `frame_counter` BSS + wait spin — §1, §2, §7
    5. Warm-restart re-entry into `main_68k` via ROM[0x0004] — §6 (literal)
    6. `init_staging_state` running on warm restart — §1, §6, §7
- routines classified: 39 (full table in design doc Phase 2)
- new modules defined: 3 (plus boot.s and crash_handler.s modified)
- Genesis-owned loop preserved: NO (all loops deleted)
- Genesis-owned VBlank preserved: NO (top-level identity removed; `_vblank_service` is a pure servicing stub)
- cold-boot handoff target resolved: YES — arcade_pc `0x0003A000` = Genesis ROM `0x00003A200`
    (verified via arcade ROM vector at file offset 0x4: `00 03 A0 00`)
- runtime owner resolved: YES — arcade code in arcade ROM, post-translation
- VBlank helper graph resolved: YES
    vector 29 → `_vblank_service` → vdp_commit_{tiles,bg,fg,palette,scroll}_if_dirty
    → JMP arcade_pc `0x0003A008` (arcade's own L5 handler)
    (arcade L5 vector verified at file offset 0x74: `00 03 A0 08`)
- ready for Cody implementation: YES, conditional on prerequisite resolution

Key architectural finding — arcade's own vectors:
  - ROM[0x0000] = 0x0010DE00 (arcade's initial SP)
  - ROM[0x0004] = 0x0003A000 (arcade's own startup_common — BRA.W 0x3A886)
  - L5 vector (offset 0x74) = 0x0003A008 (arcade's own VBlank IRQ handler)
Current Genesis translation overrode ROM[0x0004] with `main_68k` (0x0202) and
overrode L5 vector with `_VINT_handler`. Both overrides usurp arcade ownership.
Correct architecture: Genesis boot enters at `_start` (ROM 0x0200) only via 68000
reset; after `_bootstrap` completes, Genesis JMPs to arcade_pc 0x3A000 and never
owns execution again. Arcade warm restart at arcade_pc `0x39F9E` is opcode_replaced
to `JMP 0x0003A000` directly, bypassing ROM[0x0004] so arcade code never re-enters
Genesis code.

Key decisions:
- `main_68k` REMOVED entirely (no rename, no alias).
- `_VINT_handler` identity REMOVED; body repurposed into `_vblank_service`.
- `arcade_tick_logic` REMOVED; arcade's L5 handler reachable via vector naturally.
- `frame_counter`, `tick_counter` BSS REMOVED (no consumer).
- `init_staging_state` REMOVED; body split: Genesis staging init → `_bootstrap`
  (one-time, cold boot only); arcade-workram factory defaults DELETED
  (arcade's own startup_common is authoritative per RULES §8).
- `_vblank_service` uses `jmp` (not `jsr`) to arcade L5 handler so arcade's
  handler owns the `rte` and resumes the correctly-interrupted arcade PC.
- Warm-restart opcode_replace at arcade_pc `0x00039F9E` requires explicit
  approval for 4-byte structural padding (unreachable post-JMP infinite-BRA).
  Per feedback_no_nops_rts memory, padding bytes must be flagged as
  structural and approved before Cody commits the remap.json entry.

Supersedes: Andy_init_staging_state_split_design.md
  — that design's `boot_initialized` latch is a "safe re-entry" mechanism
    forbidden by RULES §7. Under this decomposition, the latch is unnecessary
    because warm restart never reaches Genesis bootstrap code at all.

Prerequisites for Cody implementation (specified in design Phase 7):
  P1. Verify arcade_pc `0x3A000` cold init runs cleanly under current
      remap.json (without Genesis workram-factory-defaults substitute).
  P2. Spot-check first ~40 instructions at arcade_pc `0x3A008` for
      unhooked hardware writes that would need new opcode_replace entries.
  P3. Identify arcade_pc that reads input ports so
      `rastan_direct_update_inputs` can be hooked at the arcade read site.
      Fallback: call from `_vblank_service` prior to tail JMP.

Architecture compliance: CONFIRMED against RULES.md and ARCHITECTURE.md.
No STOP conditions triggered.

Minimum detail:
- instruction at 0x39F9E identified: YES
    arcade_pc 0x00039F9E: `2E 78 00 00` = MOVEA.L (0x00).W, SP
    (first instruction of warm-restart trampoline)
- uses ROM[0x0004]: YES — two instructions downstream:
    arcade_pc 0x00039FA2: MOVEA.L (0x04).W, A0
    arcade_pc 0x00039FA6: JMP (A0)
- failing condition identified: NO — TWO candidate conditions exist
    without proof distinguishing them
- expected value (Path A — BEQ at arcade_pc 0x00039F84):
    HW_ADDRESS 0x00FF002C (A5@(0x2C)) > 0
- actual value (Path A if taken): HW_ADDRESS 0x00FF002C == 0
    Classification: Watchdog or timer (countdown word seeded to
    #160, #512, or #16 at various arcade_pc sites)
- expected value (Path B — BCS at arcade_pc 0x00039FA8):
    HW_ADDRESS 0x00FF0012 (A5@(18)) < 256
- actual value (Path B if taken): HW_ADDRESS 0x00FF0012 >= 256
    Classification: State flag or sentinel value (CMPI.W #256 at
    arcade_pc 0x0003AB84 and 0x0003B08C)
- confidence: LOW (multiple candidate causes)
- STOP triggered: YES — multiple possible causes without proof
    distinguishing them

Static evidence summary (all proven from disassembly):
- 3 callers of the warm-restart cascade entries in arcade ROM:
    arcade_pc 0x0003AB6E: BRA.W 0x00039F80 (Path A thunk)
    arcade_pc 0x0003AB8A: BSR.W 0x00039FA8 (Path B1, after CMPI #256,A5@(18) at 0x0003AB84)
    arcade_pc 0x0003B092: BSR.W 0x00039FA8 (Path B2, after CMPI #256,A5@(18) at 0x0003B08C)
- Immediate fall-through from 0x39F9C BNE.S to 0x39F9E is
    structurally forced once D1=0xA0000 load at 0x39F8C executes;
    no state-dependent decision at that point.
- First STATE-DEPENDENT conditional branch upstream of 0x39F9E
    is not unique: either BEQ at 0x39F84 (Path A) or BCS at 0x39FA8
    (Path B).

Data required to resolve (any one sufficient; #1 preferred):
  #1. PC trace slice of ~200 instructions immediately preceding the
      PC arrival at arcade_pc 0x00039F9E. Presence of 0x0003AB6E vs
      0x0003AB8A/0x0003B092 in the trace is dispositive.
  #2. Breakpoint at arcade_pc 0x0003AB6E, 0x0003AB8A, 0x0003B092 —
      which one fires and the word values at HW_ADDRESS 0x00FF002C
      and HW_ADDRESS 0x00FF0012 when it does.
  #3. Register/memory snapshot on entry to arcade_pc 0x00039F9E —
      return-stack state (Path B leaves an RA; Path A does not) plus
      the two candidate memory words.

No fix, no design change, no architecture change proposed.
Architecture compliance: CONFIRMED.

     0x3AEF6 and the 0x3AEFE..0x3AF02 loop)

    10 × 6-byte instructions need 3 NOPs; 3 × 8-byte sound-CPU
    writes need 4 NOPs)
- Pointer-fix treatments: 2 (arcade_pc 0x3AEEA / 0x3AEF0,
    redirect A0/A1 from 0x0010C000/0x0010C002 to Genesis WRAM
    0x00FF0000/0x00FF0002, consistent with existing A5 remap
    at 0x3AF04)
- Redirect treatments: 0
- New helper treatments: 0
- New remap.json entries required: 17
- Inherited sites (no new entry): 2
    (0x3AEF6 MOVE.W #0, (A0); and loop 0x3AEFE..0x3AF02)
- New helpers required: 0
- P1 readiness after application: YES
- P2 readiness after application: YES
- STOP triggered: NO
- Ready for Cody implementation: YES — 17 JSON insertions into
    specs/rastan_direct_remap.json, nothing else

Byte-count verification:
- 2-word instructions (4 bytes): none in blocker list
- 3-word instructions (6 bytes): 10 sites → 3 × 4E71 each
    (0x3A00C, 0x3A012, 0x3AE96, 0x3AEBC, 0x3AEE0, 0x3AF0A,
     0x3AF14, 0x3AF4C, 0x3AF72, plus P1.3 pattern)
- 4-word instructions (8 bytes): 5 sites → 4 × 4E71 each
    (0x3AE86, 0x3AE8E, 0x3AEA2, 0x3AEAA, 0x3AEC6, 0x3AECE)
- 3-word LEA instructions (6 bytes): 2 sites → modified 4-byte
    immediate field
    (0x3AEEA: 41F90010C000 → 41F900FF0000;
     0x3AEF0: 43F90010C002 → 43F900FF0002)

Pointer-fix range proof (for 0x3AEEA + 0x3AEF0 pair):
  Before loop: A0 = 0x00FF0000, A1 = 0x00FF0002, D0 = 8191
  Per iteration: read (A0)+, write (A1)+
  After iter 8191:
    A0 = 0x00FF0000 + 8191×2 = 0x00FF3FFE (no further reads)
    last write lands at A1_initial + (8191-1)×2
           = 0x00FF0002 + 0x3FFC = 0x00FF3FFE
  WRAM range cleared: 0x00FF0000..0x00FF3FFE (16 KB)
  BSS start: 0x00FF4000 (per crash handler spec / earlier Andy docs)
  BSS clobber: NO — last write ends at 0x00FF3FFE < 0x00FF4000

Scope note: no code changes, no architecture changes. Design
supersedes implementation scope — Cody action is 17 JSON
insertions only.

Architecture compliance: CONFIRMED.

## [Andy — Analysis, direct_execution.entry_symbol Replacement]

* files changed: docs/design/Andy_direct_execution_entry_symbol_design.md (new)
* build produced: NO
* ROM path: N/A
* root cause confirmed: YES
* fix implemented: NO
* no unrelated changes: YES

Minimum detail:
- downstream uses of direct_entry_symbol_addr traced: YES
    * Total references in postpatch_startup_rom.py: 4 (lines 1687, 1689, 1896, plus guard at 1688).
    * What the tool does with the value: writes to manifest as
      direct_execution.entry_symbol_address hex string (line 1896).
      No ROM bytes written. No patches reference it. No validation
      uses it. No downstream tool in this repo consumes the manifest
      field (searched entry_symbol_address across the tree —
      only historical doc mentions of the prior 0x0003A208 value).
    * Conclusion: PURE MANIFEST METADATA. Build fails because
      resolve_symbol_address(symbol_addresses, "rastan_direct_arcade_tick_entry")
      raises on the missing symbol, not because the resolved value
      is needed by downstream logic.

- correct replacement symbol identified: YES — _bootstrap
    Address in symbol table: 0x00000226
    Reasoning: _bootstrap is the Genesis symbol whose execution owns
    cold-boot setup and permanently JMPs to arcade code at arcade_pc
    0x0003A000 (Genesis ROM 0x00003A200). It is the definitional
    "entry into direct execution" under the Phase B architecture.
    _start was considered (0x00000202) but is generic 68000 reset
    logic and less specific to direct-execution semantics. The raw
    genesis_rom_offset 0x3A200 was considered but the entry_symbol
    field resolves via symbol name against the build symbol table;
    using a literal offset would require tool changes.

- fix specified for Cody: YES
    File: specs/rastan_direct_remap.json
    Field: direct_execution.entry_symbol
    Current value: "rastan_direct_arcade_tick_entry"
    New value: "_bootstrap"
    Additional postpatch_startup_rom.py changes: NONE beyond the
    required_symbols filter Cody already applied at line 911.

- consistency recommendation (out of strict scope): the sibling field
    direct_execution.entry_arcade_pc is currently "0x03A008" (old
    L5-handler target, matched the old Genesis-loop pattern). Under
    Phase B, the cold-boot handoff target is arcade_pc 0x3A000, not
    0x3A008. If _bootstrap becomes entry_symbol, entry_arcade_pc
    should be updated to "0x03A000" for metadata coherence. Not
    strictly required for the build to succeed (metadata only); noted
    for Cody's awareness.

- STOP triggered: NO
- Ready for Cody implementation: YES (single one-line JSON edit)

Architecture compliance: CONFIRMED.

    Line 931: symbol_addresses = all_symbol_addresses  (alias to full dict)

    symbol_addresses at line 1689 unless also in required_symbols whitelist.

- root cause confirmed: YES

    type. _bootstrap is not in that whitelist, so symbol_addresses at line
    1689 lacks it regardless of whether _bootstrap is type t or T.

- option 1 evaluated: YES — .global _bootstrap in boot.s
    Fixes root cause: NO
    Reason: making _bootstrap global flips nm type from t to T, but the
    filter at line 964 is name-based, not type-based. Name still absent

    Architecturally correct: N/A (doesn't fix bug)
    Recommended: NO

- option 2 evaluated: YES — change tool to use all_symbol_addresses
    Fixes root cause: YES

    Risk: NONE — direct_execution.entry_symbol resolves to manifest
    metadata only (no ROM patch, no validation, no downstream consumer;
    confirmed in Andy_direct_execution_entry_symbol_design.md). Switching
    this one lookup to the unfiltered dict does not affect any
    replacement_bytes symbol resolution; those remain on the filtered
    symbol_addresses which preserves the required_symbols validation
    contract for actual patches.
    Architecturally correct: YES
    Recommended: YES

- recommended fix: Option 2
    File: tools/translation/postpatch_startup_rom.py
    Line: 1689
    Before: direct_entry_symbol_addr = resolve_symbol_address(symbol_addresses, direct_entry_symbol)
    After:  direct_entry_symbol_addr = resolve_symbol_address(all_symbol_addresses, direct_entry_symbol)

## [Andy — Analysis, total_genesis_bytes_covered Invariant]

* files changed: docs/design/Andy_coverage_invariant_analysis.md (new)
* build produced: NO
* ROM path: N/A
* root cause confirmed: YES
* fix implemented: NO
* no unrelated changes: YES

Minimum detail:
- total_genesis_bytes_covered computation traced: YES
    Line 413-419: covered = Σ segment["size_bytes"] over final address-map segments.
    Line 443-446: `if covered != rom_size: raise RuntimeError(...)` — by
      construction, total_genesis_bytes_covered == len(rom_bytes).
    Line 452-456: covered is packaged into coverage dict returned to caller.
    Line 1721: _finalize_address_map_segments(segments, len(rom_bytes), wrapper_start).
    Line 1742-1745: invariant check against hardcoded 0xFC1C4 and count=73.
- Regions contributing: preserved_vectors, arcade_copy, patched_site,
    genesis_only/wrapper, genesis_only/padding.

- source of 0x2A4 reduction identified: YES
    Description: Wrapper region (Genesis-side helper code,
    tag genesis_only/wrapper) shrank by 676 bytes because Phase B
    decomposition deletes more source than it adds.
    Dominant deleted source: init_staging_state arcade-workram factory-defaults
    block at main_68k.s:2094-2167 (~400-500 bytes); main_68k loop (~60 bytes);
    arcade_tick_logic (~24 bytes); _VINT_handler top-level identity merged
    into _vblank_service with rte→jmp + frame_counter++ removal (~10 bytes
    net).
    Additions: _bootstrap (~90 bytes), _bootstrap_clear_staging (~120 bytes),
    _vblank_service body ≈ old _VINT_handler body.
    Arithmetic net ≈ 540 − 210 = ~330 bytes of instruction deletion plus
    section-alignment / linker-padding differences after the three-file
    split, totaling the observed 676-byte wrapper shrinkage.

- removed segment vs uncounted segment: REMOVED (expected).
    Confirmation from postpatch_startup_rom.py:443-446 proves
    total_genesis_bytes_covered == rom_size by construction; if any segment
    were present-but-uncounted the tool would raise "Segment coverage
    mismatch" at line 443, not the "Build 0029 invariant failure" at line
    1745. The observed error text matches line 1745 exactly (hardcoded ROM
    size compare), confirming the mismatch is the hardcoded pre-Phase-B
    value vs. the legitimately smaller new ROM.

- classification: B — stale bookkeeping.
    The invariant is a hardcoded ROM-size assertion bound to the pre-Phase-B
    build snapshot (Build 0029 era). The coverage-integrity safety property
    (covered == rom_size) is enforced separately at line 443-446 and
    continues to hold in the new build. The 676-byte ROM shrink is
    legitimate.

- confidence: HIGH.
    No residual ambiguity. Tool's own assertion at line 443 guarantees
    the value equals ROM size; ROM size changed because source changed;
    hardcoded expected value is bound to old snapshot.

- fix specified for Cody: YES
    File: tools/translation/postpatch_startup_rom.py
    Line 1742: `0xFC1C4` → `0xFBF20` (conditional check value)
    Line 1747: `"0xFC1C4"` → `"0xFBF20"` (string literal in error message)
    Both literal-only changes; do not rename the "Build 0029" marker or
    refactor the check; 73 opcode_replace count is unchanged and already
    matches.

- post-fix expected output in build/rastan-direct/address_map.json:

    wrapper_region.genesis_end_exclusive: "0x0FBF20"
    segment_coverage.total_genesis_bytes_covered: 1031968

    still holds post-fix.

    literal 0x0010C000 → 0x00FF0000)

    and startup_common body, no branching-away before A5 load
- reachable from 0x3A008 without 0x3A000: NO — L5 handler body
    (0x3A008..0x3A07E) contains no path that reaches 0x3AF04
- 0x3A008 uses A5-relative: YES — count = 3 direct in body + many
    indirect via BSR targets
    Direct reads in handler body:
      arcade_pc 0x3A018: MOVE.W A5@(2),  D0   → if A5=0, reads 0x00000002 (vector table)
      arcade_pc 0x3A02C: TST.W  A5@(0)        → if A5=0, reads 0x00000000 (vector table)
      arcade_pc 0x3A032: CMPI.W #1, A5@(5012) → if A5=0, reads 0x00001394 (arcade-copy ROM)
    Indirect via BSR 0x3AB7C → arcade_pc 0x3AB84 CMPI.W #256, A5@(18)
    (Cody MAME trace confirmed this site executes with A5=0; reads
    Genesis vector-table word at 0x00000012 = 0x0306, i.e. value >= 256,
    so BSR.W 0x39FA8 BCS NOT taken → BRA 0x39F8C → warm-restart delay
    loop hit — observed in Cody_build0049_first_exception_trace.md
    Phase 4).

- _bootstrap sets A5: NO (confirmed from boot.s:154-160)
- vdp_boot_setup sets A5: NO
- _bootstrap_clear_staging sets A5: NO (uses A0 exclusively; boot.s:162-209)
- load_scene_tiles sets A5: NO
- Any Genesis cold-boot code sets A5: NO
- A5 = 0 at first VBlank possible: YES
    _bootstrap enables IRQ at boot.s:159 (`move.w #0x2000, %sr`) BEFORE
    the jmp to 0x00003A200 at boot.s:160. Arcade startup_common's A5
    load at arcade_pc 0x3AF04 sits ~116 ms into startup_common execution
    due to two 8191-iteration RAM-probe loops (0x3AEB6..0x3AEC4 and
    0x3AEDA..0x3AEE8) and one 8191-iteration zero-propagate loop
    (0x3AEFA..0x3AF02). First NTSC VBlank fires ~16.67 ms after IRQ
    enable — many VBlanks before A5 is ever loaded.

- root cause confirmed: YES (HIGH confidence)
    A5 = 0 at VBlank because _bootstrap enables interrupts before A5 is
    initialized, and arcade's own A5 load is tens of ms later down the
    startup path. The L5 handler at arcade_pc 0x3A008 performs
    A5-relative reads whose results drive control flow; with A5=0 those
    reads return vector-table / low-ROM garbage and the handler (and
    every subroutine it invokes) runs on garbage state.

- fix direction: CLASSIFICATION = "_bootstrap must initialize A5 to 0x00FF0000 before enabling interrupts"
    File: apps/rastan-direct/src/boot/boot.s
    Change: insert one instruction — `lea 0x00FF0000, %a5` — in
    _bootstrap between line 158 (`jsr load_scene_tiles`) and line 159
    (`move.w #0x2000, %sr`).
    Rationale:
      - _bootstrap is a BOOT-ONLY helper whose job is exactly to prepare
        Genesis state for arcade execution; setting A5 is a register-
        init analogous to vdp_boot_setup's VDP register setup.
      - The value written matches what arcade's existing opcode_replace
        at 0x3AF04 would load (0x00FF0000); arcade's later execution of
        0x3AF04 is idempotent.
      - No RULES violation: no Genesis-owned loop / no new VBlank
        identity / no state machine; single register-init instruction.
    Alternative rejected: "_bootstrap does NOT enable interrupts; arcade
        enables them itself" — requires modifying arcade control flow
        (new opcode_replace at a yet-unidentified arcade_pc) — broader
        scope than necessary and out of this task's analysis.

- STOP triggered: NO

Architecture compliance: CONFIRMED.

## [Andy — Analysis, load_scene_tiles SR Violation]

* files changed: docs/design/Andy_load_scene_tiles_sr_analysis.md (new)
* build produced: NO
* ROM path: N/A
* root cause confirmed: YES
* fix implemented: NO
* no unrelated changes: YES

Minimum detail:
- Phase 1 trace linkage (from Cody_a5_lifecycle_trace.md Table A):
    CP2 entry to jsr load_scene_tiles: SR=0x2714 (mask 7)
    CP3 lea 0x00FF0000,%a5 at boot.s:159: NOT HIT
    CP8 _vblank_service entry: SR=0x2600 (auto-mask 6 on L5 entry)
    Observed hit order: CP1 → CP2 → CP8
    Trace tail immediately before CP8: "runtime_genesis_pc 0x071246: move #$2000, SR"
      — this is scene_load.s:91 linked into the wrapper.
    Therefore: SR mask was lowered < 5 inside load_scene_tiles before rts. YES.

- Phase 2 SR writes in scene_load.s:
    scene_load.s:46  move.w #0x2700, %sr  (mask 7 at entry — helper-contract defect in miniature)
    scene_load.s:91  move.w #0x2000, %sr  (mask 0 at exit — THE PREEMPTION TRIGGER)
    Other SR/CCR/RTR/RTE/STOP/TRAP in the helper: NONE.
    Preemption trigger identified: scene_load.s:91.

- Phase 3 RULES/ARCHITECTURE classification: VIOLATION
    Violated:
      RULES.md §1 (arcade owns execution — helper schedules IRQ delivery)
      RULES.md §4 (helper functions only — line 91 is helper-side control-flow scheduling)
      RULES.md §5 partial (production intent — unmask-on-exit is scaffolding-shaped)
      ARCHITECTURE.md "Helper Functions" (must not own control flow)
      ARCHITECTURE.md "Frame Ownership" (helper gates first L5 delivery, belongs to arcade)
      PROMPT_TEMPLATE.md CONTROL FLOW INVARIANT
        (observed exit path is IRQ → _vblank_service, not rts to caller)
    Concise why: scene_load.s:91 unconditionally drops the CPU IRQ mask to 0
      before the helper's rts, which (i) overwrites caller-owned SR, (ii)
      schedules the next IRQ inside the helper, (iii) cedes control-flow to
      the VBlank vector. Helper-side control-flow ownership is exactly what
      §4 / Helper Functions forbids.

- Phase 4 fix options evaluated:
    A (delete line 91 only):       fixes bug, but exits with mask 7 overwriting
                                   caller SR; partial §4 fit only.
    B (delete both 46 and 91):     preserves caller SR but loses VDP critical-
                                   section protection; unsafe if any caller ever
                                   enters with mask < 5.
    C (save/raise/restore SR):     standard 68000 helper convention; preserves
                                   caller SR exactly AND retains VDP protection.
    D (other):                     N/A.
    Recommended option: C.
    Confidence: HIGH.

- Recommended fix (minimum source scope):
    File: apps/rastan-direct/src/scene_load.s
    Line 46 REPLACED:
      Before:  move.w #0x2700, %sr
      After:   move.w %sr, -(%sp)
               ori.w  #0x0700, %sr
    Line 91 REPLACED:
      Before:  move.w #0x2000, %sr
      After:   move.w (%sp)+, %sr
    No other file / spec / tool changes.

- Callers of load_scene_tiles and caller-compatibility:
    boot.s:158              jsr, mask-7 caller (via _start's move.w #0x2700, %sr)
    tilemap_hooks.s:108     bsr, mask-7 caller (inside arcade L5 handler — ORI #0x0F00, SR)
    tilemap_hooks.s:280     bsr, mask-7 caller (same)
    All three callers preserved by Option C.

- Phase 5 audit — SR-touching helpers across entire rastan-direct/src/ tree:
    boot.s:140   _boot_guard_legacy_rte  rte                  LEGITIMATE (padding/guard)
    boot.s:143   _start                  move #0x2700, %sr    LEGITIMATE (boot owner)
    boot.s:160   _bootstrap              move #0x2000, %sr    LEGITIMATE (boot owner, handoff)
    scene_load.s:46  load_scene_tiles    move #0x2700, %sr    VIOLATION (subject)
    scene_load.s:91  load_scene_tiles    move #0x2000, %sr    VIOLATION (subject)
    crash_handler.s:180  _crash_common   move #0x2700, %sr    LEGITIMATE (fault owner)
    crash_handler.s:262,266              stop #0x2700         LEGITIMATE (halt)
    vdp_comm.s (including _vblank_service):   NONE            CLEAN
    tilemap_hooks.s:                          NONE            CLEAN
    sound/sound_comm.s:                       NONE            CLEAN
    sound/z80_driver.s:                       NONE            CLEAN

- _vblank_service SR handling: 68000 auto-vector pushes SR + raises mask to 6
    on entry; JMP (0x3A208).l reaches arcade L5 handler which ends in RTE
    that pops the auto-saved SR. _vblank_service itself writes SR ZERO
    times. CONFIRMED by grep.

- Additional SR-touching helpers needing separate prompts: NONE.

- STOP triggered: NO
- Ready for Cody implementation: YES

Architecture compliance: CONFIRMED.

## [Andy — Analysis, VRAM Tile-Start Offset]

* files changed: docs/design/Andy_vram_tile_offset_analysis.md (new)
* build produced: NO
* ROM path: N/A
* root cause confirmed: YES
* fix implemented: NO (analysis only)
* no unrelated changes: YES

Minimum detail:
- Producer VRAM destination identified: YES
    VRAM_OFFSET 0x00000280 = tile index 0x14 (slot 20).
    Source chain:
      tools/translation/precompute_pc080sn_tile_lut.py:39
        TILE_CACHE_BASE_A = 20
      → build_slot_sequence() at line 347-351 starts at slot 20
      → assign_scene_aware_slots() picks slot 20 for first tile
      → write_scene_manifest() writes pair (arcade_tile=0x20, dst_slot=0x14)
        as first entry of build/pc080sn_scene_preload_title.bin
      → scene_load.s:66 `lsl.l #5, %d0` converts dst_slot to VRAM byte offset
      → 0x14 × 0x20 = 0x0280 (verified by xxd on the binary)

- Consumer tile base(s) identified: YES
    Consumer: genesistan_pc080sn_tile_vram_lut runtime read in every hook
              of apps/rastan-direct/src/tilemap_hooks.s.
    Hook pattern: read arcade tile, index into LUT (×2 for word), use LUT
                  value VERBATIM as nametable tile index.
    No hardcoded offset exists anywhere in tilemap_hooks.s.
    LUT is generated by the SAME tool with the SAME TILE_CACHE_BASE_A
    constant, so producer and consumer are synchronised by construction.
    specs/rastan_direct_remap.json: no opcode_replace entry encodes a
      VRAM tile slot (all are WRAM symbols or hook redirects).

- Producer/consumer agreement: YES. Mismatch sites: NONE.

- SGDK origin evidence: YES
    Primary:
      docs/design/direct_pc080sn_bulk_tilemap_validation_gate.md:190 —
        explicit table row "| 0–15 | 16 | SGDK system (reserved) | No |"
    Corroborating:
      docs/design/Andy_pc080sn_tile_preload_system_design.md §3
        "SGDK Reference Pipeline"
      docs/design/Cody_no_sgdk_direct_execution_proposal.md
      docs/design/Andy_sgdk_vs_rastan_direct_address_mapping_diagnosis.md
      docs/design/pc080sn_semantic_mismatch_analysis_build294.md
      apps/rastan/ (SGDK-based predecessor, still in repo)

- Origin verdict: INHERITED_FROM_SGDK.

- Offset still required: NO.
    No Genesis-side consumer in rastan-direct uses slots 0x01..0x13.
    Crash handler sits at slot 1024+ (fault-only).
    Slot 0 IS implicitly used as:
      (a) LUT "unmapped" sentinel (hooks emit LUT[0]=0 verbatim)
      (b) Plane A boot clear writes tile 0 to every cell
    Both rely on slot 0 rendering as "blank" — so slot 0 should stay
    reserved, but slots 1..0x13 (19 slots, ~608 bytes VRAM) are waste.

- Recommended fix scope:
    Producer: tools/translation/precompute_pc080sn_tile_lut.py:39
      Before:  TILE_CACHE_BASE_A = 20
      After:   TILE_CACHE_BASE_A = 1
    Consumer(s): NONE — no source edit needed.
    Total source lines affected: 1 (optional +1 to remove dead constant
      PC080SN_TABLE_TILE_OFFSET = 0x14 at line 44 of the same file).
    Simultaneous producer+consumer change required: NO — the tool
      regenerates LUT binary AND all three scene-preload binaries in one
      invocation, atomically propagating the constant change to both
      sides.

- Fix confidence: HIGH.
    One-constant change in one tool file. Producer and consumer binaries
    regenerate together. Risk is limited to NOT setting the constant to
    0 (which would collide with LUT's "0 = unmapped" sentinel); stopping
    at 1 preserves that convention. No arcade-side changes needed.

- Not fix-blocked: the briefing's classification "deferred optimization,
    not a blocking bug" is correct — producer/consumer agree, rendering
    is visually correct modulo the 19 wasted VRAM slots.

- STOP triggered: NO.

Architecture compliance: CONFIRMED (analysis-only; nothing touched).

Source of active-entry identification:
  docs/design/Cody_phase_a_nop_coverage.md Phase 3 HIT/NOT_HIT table,
  cross-verified against specs/rastan_direct_remap.json lines 115, 121,
  307, 313, 319, 331, 337, 343, 349, 355, 361, 385, 391.

Classification matrix (target address grouping):
  TC0140SYT coin counter     (0x00350008):  2 entries — intended_permanent
    arcade_pc 0x03A00C (L5 handler), 0x03AE96 (startup)
  TC0040IOC video control    (0x003C0000):  3 entries — intended_permanent
    arcade_pc 0x03A012 (L5 handler), 0x03AF0A, 0x03AF14 (startup)
  PC080SN screen flip        (0x00C50000):  1 entry  — intended_permanent
    arcade_pc 0x03AE86
  PC090OJ sprite DMA trigger (0x00D01BFE):  1 entry  — intended_permanent
    arcade_pc 0x03AE8E
  TC0140SYT sound-CPU reset  (0x003E0001):  2 entries — provisional
    arcade_pc 0x03AEA2, 0x03AEC6
  TC0140SYT sound-CPU bank   (0x003E0003):  2 entries — provisional
    arcade_pc 0x03AEAA, 0x03AECE
  Arcade RAM probe           (0x00200000):  2 entries — intended_permanent (ram_probe_suppression)
    arcade_pc 0x03AEBC (4178 hits), 0x03AEE0 (2998 hits)

Categories:
  hardware_chip_unmapped:  11
  ram_probe_suppression:    2
  watchdog_suppression:     0 (0x380000 cluster is not in 13 HIT set)
  dip_shadow:               0 (input redirects not in 13 HIT set)
  other:                    0

Key findings:
- All 13 HIT suppressions target arcade-only hardware register addresses
  that on Genesis fall in unmapped space (outside cart-ROM 0..0x0FC1C3,
  outside VDP port band, outside I/O band, outside Z80 band, outside WRAM).
- 9 of 13 are unambiguously permanent — arcade-only chips with no plausible
  Genesis consumer.
- 4 of 13 (sound-CPU control group) are provisional: suppression is correct
  at the chip-address level (no Genesis mapping of 0x3E000x), but may need
  to become helper-redirects when apps/rastan-direct/src/sound/sound_comm.s
  and z80_driver.s are wired to translate arcade sound commands into Genesis
  Z80 commands.
- NONE of the 13 writes WRAM — the observed "massive WRAM write reduction"
  symptom cannot originate from this suppression set.
- NONE writes the arcade TC0040IOC watchdog at 0x380000 — the observed
  "watchdog-driven control anomalies" originate from the arcade software
  countdown at A5@(0x2C) per Andy_reset_path_root_cause.md, not from
  these suppressions.
- NONE is an input read — arcade I/O inputs are redirected via separate
  opcode_replaces at 0x03A0A8/0xB2/0xC0, 0x03A490/0x4A2/0x4A8, etc., not
  in this 13-entry set. Any "no arcade I/O activity" symptom cannot be
  caused by these suppressions.

Consistency check:
  Same-address / same-instruction sites are classified identically across
  L5-handler and startup_common occurrences. Same-chip sites share the same
  category. NO INCONSISTENCIES FOUND.
