## [Andy — Polling Loop Investigation (0x0003A192 ↔ 0x0003A19C in Canonical Build 0077)]

* files changed:
  - docs/design/Andy_polling_loop_investigation.md (new — descriptive name per OPEN-002 extended policy)
  - AGENTS_LOG.md (this append)
* build produced: NO
* ROM path: N/A
* root cause confirmed: YES — the "polling loop" framing is corrected to "broken watchdog mechanism"; OPEN-001 and OPEN-004 collapse to the same root cause (arcade game-loop post-bootstrap progression is blocked, so the watchdog kicks at 0x0003A5D4..0x0003ADD0 are never reached, so the watchdog expires every ~3.6s and soft-resets to bootstrap)
* fix implemented: NO (separate task after Chad routes next-step choice)
* no unrelated changes: YES

Investigation findings:
- Phase 1 BM-002 byte reading verification: CONFIRMED — bytes at canonical Build 0077 file offset 0x0003A19C are `66 F4` = BNE.S -12 → target 0x0003A192
- Deliverable 1 — full routine disassembled at 0x0003A180..0x0003A1AC: prologue at 0x0003A180 checks workram counter at %a5@(44); if positive, decrements and returns (RTS); if zero, falls through to ~655360-iteration delay loop at 0x0003A18C..0x0003A19C, then reloads SP from 0x0000 and PC from reset vector 0x0004 (= 0x00000202 = _bootstrap), JMPs there
- Deliverable 2 — wait condition: the loop body has NO polling read (MOVE.L $0.W, D0 reads constant initial-SSP from ROM; D0 discarded next iteration). The IMPLICIT routine-entry wait condition is %a5@(44) = 0x00FF002C — a software watchdog timer. Caller-facing semantics: "if 0x00FF002C is positive, kick happens; if zero, system soft-resets after delay."
- Deliverable 3 — writers to 0x00FF002C: 11 KICK sites at runtime PCs 0x0003A5D4 (#0xA0), 0x0003A63E (#0xA0), 0x0003AC88 (#0xD0), 0x0003ACF2 (#0xA0), 0x0003AD22 (#0xA0), 0x0003AD5E (#0x200), 0x0003ADD0 (#0x10), 0x0009A3B0 / 0x0009A3D0 / 0x0009A4B0 / 0x0009A4D0 (deeper ORI.B side effects); 1 FORCE-EXPIRE site at 0x0003AE76 (CLRW — Rastan's intentional reset trigger); 5 decrement sites in watchdog and adjacent routines. All writers are in arcade-translated code (not Genesis-native helpers).
- Deliverable 4 — writer reachability/execution: NONE of the 11 kick sites is in any of the excursion-visited regions (0x000002D4 vector dispatch; 0x0003B0B6/0x0003B0E6 arcade-translated handlers; 0x000705F6/0x0007060C/0x00070614/0x00070624/0x000719D4/0x000719E0/0x00071B60/0x00071CE2 Genesis-native helpers including vdp_commit_sprites at 0x000719B0 and genesistan_hook_tilemap_bg_fill body). The CPU is reaching the polymorphic-dispatch tilemap_bg_fill helper and vdp_commit_sprites via interrupt-driven excursions, but no kick site is on those excursion paths. Arcade game-loop main progression is blocked upstream of all 11 kick sites.
- Deliverable 5 — architectural read: BROKEN WATCHDOG (functionally a "broken polling wait" in spirit — kick code exists, was supposed to run, isn't running). Confidence: HIGH. Evidence: routine structure (unconditional soft-reset at expiry; no exit branch besides expiry); kick site locations (post-bootstrap arcade game-loop range); observed excursion pattern (interrupts firing but not reaching kicks); OPEN-004's ~15 re-entries in 64s timing matches 3.6s/cycle estimate; Andy nametable composition path classification's independent "game-loop blocked" finding. Correctly-idle-wait alternative ruled out (no input-poll, no input-state branch). OPEN-001 + OPEN-004 collapse: same root cause; routing call (formal merger vs separate-with-cross-reference) is Chad's.
- Recommended next diagnostic step (Chad routes; Andy does NOT route): arcade-source cross-reference with MAME rastan.cpp / docs/reference/mame/rastan/ to identify the "primary" kick site the arcade game-loop reaches under normal Rastan operation. Alternative: bookmark cycle (BM-NNN, post-OPEN-013 closure) targeting the suspected primary kick. Alternative: static control-flow trace from _bootstrap exit (0x00003A200) to the first watchdog routine call. Each has different cost/evidence.
- Record corrections noted (this design doc, NOT modifications to BM-002 / BM-003 / CLOSED-010 / CLOSED-011): BM-002 characterization NOT superseded (was correct; investigation adds context about routine's role as OPEN-004 re-entry mechanism); BM-002 trace 7-hits reinterpreted as OPEN-014-style undersampling of saturated loop; BM-003 Outcome A "almost immediate" clarified as constant-loop saturation effect, not normal-flow visit
- New OPEN issue(s): NONE opened (rationale in §7.2: refinement-not-new-fault; record corrections don't warrant separate tracking; next-step routing is Chad's call, not a tracked issue)
- STOP triggered: NO

Floor:
- Phase 1 loop disassembly via direct ROM hex + static-disasm artifact (functionally equivalent to interactive Ghidra for this scope): YES
- Phase 1 BM-002 byte verification independent of inherited characterization: YES (CONFIRMED match)
- Deliverables 1-5 all resolved with cited evidence: YES
- Record corrections documented: YES
- No ROM/source/spec/Makefile/build/tool/evidence modifications: YES
- No bookmark cycles initiated: YES
- No issue closures: YES
- No architectural routing decisions (Andy provides findings; Chad routes): YES
- Ambiguities surfaced explicitly: YES (§5.3 hypothesis flagged; §5.4 recommended-next-step alternatives surfaced for Chad's choice)
- STOP triggered: NO

Open/Closed Issues Impact:
- Open issues touched: OPEN-001 (proximate cause narrowed: arcade game-loop progression blocked upstream of all 11 watchdog kick sites; collapses to same root cause as OPEN-004), OPEN-004 (mechanism identified as watchdog at 0x0003A180 expiring every ~3.6s and soft-resetting to bootstrap; collapses to same root cause as OPEN-001), OPEN-014 (BM-002 trace 7-hits reinterpretation cited as instance of the parked-helper sampling gap pattern), OPEN-002 (continued naming convention compliance — descriptive design doc filename), OPEN-008 (continued convention use)

Discrepancy note (location interpretation for Insertion 2):
The prompt's §A Insertion 2 location specification reads "Between the existing 'OPEN/CLOSED ISSUES IMPACT — REQUIRED' section and the 'OBJECTIVE' section." The current PROMPT_TEMPLATE.md has OBJECTIVE at line 35 (early in the template) and OPEN/CLOSED ISSUES IMPACT at line 77 (after OBJECTIVE) — the reverse of the prompt's assumed ordering. Literal application is impossible. §0.6 of the locked Phase 0 text reads "between Phase 0 and the OBJECTIVE phase unless a STOP fired", making the semantic intent unambiguous: Phase 0 immediately precedes OBJECTIVE. Andy applied the semantic intent (Phase 0 inserted between MANDATORY ARCHITECTURE COMPLIANCE and OBJECTIVE; OPEN/CLOSED ISSUES IMPACT remains at its existing location later in the file). If Tighe/Chad disagree with this resolution, the section can be moved by a follow-up patch.

Contradictions of CONFIRMED/STRONG priors: NONE

Static trace summary:
- Arcade trace from reset `0x3A000` forward verified at: `0x3A000` (reset branch), `0x3AE86` (init start), `0x3AEEA / 0x3AF04` (arcade WRAM `0x10C000` setup), `0x3B07A` (interrupt enable), `0x3B07E … 0x3B096` (main loop: hardware write, pretest, watchdog call, branch back), `0x39FA8 / 0x39FAC` (watchdog test wrapper).
- Arcade kick sites verified read: `0x3A3D4`, `0x3A43E`, `0x3AA88` (counter #208), `0x3AAF2` (counter #160). Source: `build/maincpu.disasm.txt`.
- Genesis-translated parallel trace verified at: `0x3B0EA / 0x3B104` (Genesis WRAM rebase to `0xFF0000` — translation-patched), `0x3B27E … 0x3B296` (main loop, byte-perfect translated-flow equivalent: `bsrw` offset `0xef14` preserved, target relocated by `+0x200`), `0x3A1A8` (watchdog test wrapper, byte-identical).
- Genesis kick sites verified at: `0x3A5D4`, `0x3AC88`, `0x3ACF2` (= arcade + `0x200`). Source: `build/genesis_postpatch.disasm.txt`.

Outcome: B — first-kick reachability is the open RUNTIME question.
- No causally-meaningful static divergence identified on the reset-to-kick path.
- WRAM rebase `0x10C000` → `0xFF0000` preserves the `%a5@(44)` invariant (KF-001 confirms live counter at `0xFF002C`).
- Main loop is watchdog-test-only; kicks live inside state-machine handlers dispatched by Level-5 VBlank (KF-011).
- Reachability of any kick is determined at runtime by VBlank vector configuration and state-machine dispatch.

## [Andy — Immediate Predecessor Chain to runtime_genesis_pc 0x0003AC88]

* files changed: `docs/design/Andy_predecessor_chain_0x0003AC88.md` (new), `AGENTS_LOG.md` (this single new append; no existing entries modified)
* build produced: NO
* ROM path: N/A

* fix implemented: N/A
* no unrelated changes: YES

Phase 0 classification: EXTENDING KF-003. Priors statement produced in doc §0. Phase 0 STOP: not triggered. No CONFIRMED/STRONG contradiction.

Backward trace (9-node chain, kick → tie-in), all verified against `build/maincpu.disasm.txt` (arcade) and `build/genesis_postpatch.disasm.txt` (Genesis):
- `0x3ac88` (kick, `movew #208,%a5@(44)`) ← in-routine ← `0x3ac54` (handler entry, falls through to kick) — STATICALLY_PROVEN
- ← inner jmp `0x3ac3a` via inner table `0x3ac3c` (word[1]=`0x18`→`0x3ac54`); selector `%a5@(4)==1` — EXPECTED_BUT_RUNTIME_DEPENDENT
- ← outer jmp `0x3ac1e` via outer table `0x3ac20` (word[0]=`0x06`→inner dispatch `0x3ac26`); selector `%a5@(2)==0` — EXPECTED_BUT_RUNTIME_DEPENDENT
- ← title dispatcher `0x3abfe`, gated on `%a5@(44)==0` (decrement-and-return otherwise) — STATICALLY_PROVEN structure
- ← master jmp `0x3a26a` via master table `0x3a26c` (word[0]=`0x992`→`0x3abfe`); selector `%a5@(0)==0` — EXPECTED_BUT_RUNTIME_DEPENDENT
- ← master dispatch read `0x3a256` ← VBlank handler entry `0x3a208`
- ← `jmp 0x3a208` at `0x70100` (sole referencer; grep-confirmed) ← Genesis Level-6 service `0x700c2` ← Level-6 autovector (vector offset `0x78`=`0x000700c2`)

Caller-uniqueness confirmed: `0x3a208` referenced only by `0x70100`; `0x3abfe`/`0x3ac54` reached only via computed dispatch (no symbolic callers).

Genesis comparison: every chain node translated-flow equivalent. Only byte-level difference = VBlank entry hardware-write substitution: arcade `clrw 0x350008` + `movew %d0,0x3c0000` (12 bytes) → 6× `4e71` NOP on Genesis. Neither write feeds any branch on the kick path; reachability-neutral hardware-elision shim, NOT a divergence (translated-flow-equivalence test applied). Disclosed as observation; not patch scaffolding.

New durable observation: `%a5@(44)` is dual-use — both the KF-001 watchdog expiry counter AND the title dispatcher's (`0x3abfe`) per-frame delay timer; both decrement the same cell.

Outcome: B — Runtime dependency. Chain statically equivalent to a point gated entirely by runtime WRAM state selectors (`%a5@(0)/%a5@(2)/%a5@(4)/%a5@(44)`). Stopping condition: chain ties into validated Level-6 VBlank territory but only through runtime gates (so NOT the special-case full-static-connection STOP).

Timing evaluation: chain duration ~low-thousands of cycles per VBlank pass vs ~21.6M-cycle (~3.6s) watchdog window. Timing starvation plausible: NO (gating is by state-selector values / counter race, not chain duration). Counter-race nuance flagged as KF-001-scope, not pursued.

KNOWN_FINDINGS impact: Option C — proposed KF-003 Use-as-prior refinement (concrete 9-node chain + `%a5@(44)` dual-use). Additive; existing wording preserved; STRONG unchanged; last-verified bump 2026-06-11 proposed. Cody applies after Tighe ack.

Secondary follow-up: `0x0003ABFE`. Evidence-capture handoff, not implementation (no Outcome-A divergence exists).

## [Andy — Master Dispatch Selector Audit: 0x0003A256 → 0x0003ABFE]

* files changed: `docs/design/Andy_master_dispatch_selector_audit.md` (new), `AGENTS_LOG.md` (this single new append; no existing entries modified)
* build produced: NO
* ROM path: N/A
* root cause confirmed: N/A (static audit; runtime evidence still needed)
* fix implemented: N/A
* no unrelated changes: YES

Phase 0 classification: EXTENDING (touches KF-003). Phase 0 STOP: not triggered. No CONFIRMED/STRONG contradiction.

Bounded question: why does reaching `0x0003A256` not visibly lead to `0x0003ABFE`? Audit limited to master selector `%a5@(0)` + master dispatch table.

Master dispatch table targets (Genesis; `target = 0x3a26c + word`; STATICALLY_PROVEN, byte-identical to arcade `0x3a06c`):
- `%a5@(0)==0` → word `0x0992` → `0x3abfe` (title dispatcher — the BM-006 target)
- `%a5@(0)==1` → word `0x0840` → `0x3aaac` (counter-gated sub-dispatcher)
- `%a5@(0)==2` → word `0x00ee` → `0x3a35a` (counter-gated sub-dispatcher)
- `%a5@(0)==3` → word `0x0b02` → `0x3ad6e` → `braw 0x3a180` (KF-001 watchdog routine)

Only state 0 reaches `0x3abfe`. State 3 branches into the watchdog whose `0x3a192/0x3a196/0x3a19c` loop dominates the BM-006 window.

`%a5@(0)` writer audit: 7 direct writers, ALL translated-flow equivalent (identical encodings at arcade = Genesis − 0x200):
- `0x3a4ea` clrw→0; `0x3a650` clrw→0; `0x3a65a` #1; `0x3abde` #2; `0x3ad48` #2; `0x3add6` #3; `0x3ae7a` #1.
- Load-bearing: writer #6 `0x3add6` (`movew #3,%a5@(0)`) lives in VBlank-prologue routine `0x3ad7c`, called at `0x3a23e` BEFORE the dispatch read at `0x3a256`. Gate to reach the state-3 write: `%a5@(0)!=3` AND `btst #2,0xff60ff == 0`. Arcade gate is `btst #2,0x390007` (TC0040IOC input port, KF-022 active-low); Genesis redirects to WRAM input-mirror `0xff60ff`. Standard hardware-input-redirect translation; gate branch structure preserved. If mirror bit 2 == 1 (arcade idle) → state-3 skipped → `%a5@(0)` stays 0 → reaches `0x3abfe`. If bit 2 == 0 → `%a5@(0)`=3 → routes to watchdog → never reaches `0x3abfe`.

Initialization: `%a5@(0)`=`0xFF0000` is BSS-cleared to 0 by boot zero-fill at `0x3b0ea` (STATICALLY_PROVEN); no boot-path writer sets it non-zero before main loop. First-frame value is 0 unless the same-frame prologue `0x3ad7c` sets it to 3 (EXPECTED_BUT_RUNTIME_DEPENDENT).

Observation cross-reference (NOT proof): BM-006 register samples during the dominant watchdog loop (frames 128/132/140) show A0=`0x0003AD6E` = exactly the state-3 dispatch residue (`0x3a26c + 0x0b02`). Consistent with `%a5@(0)==3` routing to the watchdog.

Outcome: B — Static path translated-flow equivalent; runtime evidence needed. Whole master-state machinery (read + table + 7 writers) is translated-flow equivalent; only differences are standard hardware-address redirects, load-bearing one being the state-3 gate source (`0x390007`→`0xff60ff`). Whether `%a5@(0)` is 0 vs 3 at dispatch hinges on the runtime value of mirror `0xff60ff` bit 2, populated by a shim outside this bounded scope. Smallest runtime evidence: `%a5@(0)` at `0x0003A256` (expected 3 if cross-reference holds). Instrument NOT designed (per directive).

KNOWN_FINDINGS impact: Option A — no update. Outcome B; per directive no KF update unless Outcome A concrete durable mechanism. Master-table target map documented in design doc for a future KF-003 refinement once runtime value confirmed (not canonized now — runtime-dependent).

Recommended next task: BM-007 parked-helper bookmark on Genesis runtime PC `0x0003ADD6` (state-3 writer) — reuses existing instrument, decisive. Reached → state-3 transition fires → routes to watchdog → explains non-reach of `0x3abfe`; next task then audits the `0xff60ff` input-shim vs arcade `0x390007` (KF-022). Not reached → gate taken, `%a5@(0)` stays 0, non-reach needs re-examination of VBlank-handler completion to `0x3a256`. Alternative: direct `%a5@(0)` state-capture at `0x0003A256`.

Floor:
- Documentation only; no source/spec/tool/Makefile/ROM modifications: YES
- No bookmark cycle initiated; no instrument designed; no runtime probing: YES
- Scope held to `%a5@(0)` + master table (did NOT extend to `%a5@(2)/%a5@(4)/%a5@(44)`; did NOT chase `0x0003AF40`; did NOT relitigate KF-003 reset-to-main-loop equivalence): YES
- §0.1 evidence labels applied throughout: YES
- No KF/OPEN/CLOSED file modifications: YES
- STOP triggered: NO

## [Andy — 0xff60ff Input-Shim Audit vs Arcade 0x390007 (TC0040IOC Bit 2)]

* files changed: `docs/design/Andy_0xff60ff_input_shim_audit.md` (new), `AGENTS_LOG.md` (this single new append; no existing entries modified)
* build produced: NO
* ROM path: N/A
* root cause confirmed: localized (static defect; fix + post-fix reachability check pending)
* fix implemented: N/A
* no unrelated changes: YES

Phase 0 classification: EXTENDING (touches KF-003). Phase 0 STOP: not triggered. No CONFIRMED/STRONG contradiction.

Bounded question: what writes `0xff60ff`, and does it correctly mirror arcade TC0040IOC `0x390007` per KF-022 active-low?

Findings:
- `0xff60ff` writers identified: 1 — `0x711ac` (`moveb %d5,0xff60ff`), inside controller-poll shim at `0x710ca`. STATICALLY_PROVEN sole writer (full-file grep).
- `0xff60ff` readers identified: 7 — `0x3a690`,`0x3a9b8`,`0x3ab1a`,`0x3ad96` (the state-3 gate, `btst #2`),`0x3ae04`,`0x3ae94`,`0x3aefe`. Mirror of arcade `0x390007`.
- `0x390007` arcade reads: 11 (bits 0,1,2,3,5,6 read across game); software writes: NONE (read-only TC0040IOC hardware port).
- Shim bit-2 logic: `%d5 = moveq #-1`; only bits 3,4,5 conditionally cleared; **bit 2 never cleared** → shim writes `0xff60ff` bit 2 = 1 = active-low idle = CORRECT value IF it ran. STATICALLY_PROVEN.
- Caller analysis: shim entry `0x710ca` is **unreferenced** in Build 0077 — no jsr/bsr/jmp immediate, no pointer-table `0007 10ca`, not fall-through (preceded by rts). STATICALLY_PROVEN. → shim does not execute (INFERRED; can't statically exclude indirect call, but none evident; corroborated by BM-007 gate fall-through).
- Initialization: `0xff60ff` is Genesis BSS domain (>0xff4000, KF-008); NOT covered by arcade WRAM zero-fill (`0xFF0000..0xFF3FFF`); in boot-clear GAP `0xff60fa..0xff6103` (boot clears stop at `0xff60f8`, resume at `0xff6104`). So never initialized. STATICALLY_PROVEN.

Translated-flow-equivalence at bit 2: NO — shim computes the correct value but is unreferenced AND the byte is uninitialized, so the idle value is never delivered; gate reads uninitialized bit 2 = 0 (per BM-007) → fall-through → `%a5@(0)=3` → master dispatch → `0x3ad6e→0x3a180` watchdog. Complete explanation of BM-007 Outcome A.

Outcome: A — Static defect found. Mechanism: Genesis input mirror `0xff60ff` (translating TC0040IOC `0x390007`) populated only by the unreferenced shim at `0x710ca`, and the byte is in the boot-clear gap, so it is never written; the state-3 gate at `0x0003AD96` reads the uninitialized bit 2 = 0 and routes the master state machine to the watchdog instead of the title dispatcher.

Recommended Cody fix SCOPE (recommendation only; not drafted): establish/restore invocation of the input-poll shim `0x710ca` on the Genesis per-frame input-servicing path BEFORE the arcade VBlank handler consumes the mirror; first determine whether a call site was dropped (regression) vs never wired. Note: BSS-initializing `0xff60ff` alone would unblock this gate but leave the input mirror stale/non-functional — not the real fix. Targets OPEN-004; likely unblocks OPEN-001 downstream.

KNOWN_FINDINGS impact: Option B — proposed NEW KF entry (KF-028 candidate): "Genesis input mirror `0xff60fc..0xff6100` is unpopulated in Build 0077" (sole writer `0x711ac` in unreferenced shim `0x710ca`; boot-clear gap `0xff60fa..0xff6103`; shim would write bit 2 = 1 active-low). Confidence CONFIRMED (static facts) / STRONG (causal link). BUILD_SPECIFIC, HIGH hazard. Cross-ref KF-003, KF-022. Cody applies after Tighe ack.

Recommended next task: Cody implementation — wire input-poll shim `0x710ca` into the per-frame input path ahead of the arcade VBlank handler (determine dropped-vs-never-wired first); after fix, re-run reachability check on `0x0003ABFE` to confirm master state stays 0 and the title path is reached.

Floor:
- Documentation only; no source/spec/tool/Makefile/ROM modifications: YES
- No bookmark cycle initiated; no instrument designed; no implementation code drafted; no runtime probing: YES
- Scope held to `0xff60ff` bit 2 / `0x390007` bit 2 (no other ports, no other bits): YES
- Did NOT relitigate KF-022 active-low semantics: YES
- §0.1 evidence labels applied throughout: YES
- No KF/OPEN/CLOSED file modifications (KF-028 is a proposal): YES
- STOP triggered: NO

## [Andy — Locate Intended Call Site for Input-Poll Shim 0x710ca]

* files changed: `docs/design/Andy_0x710ca_call_site_investigation.md` (new), `AGENTS_LOG.md` (this single new append; no existing entries modified)
* build produced: NO
* ROM path: N/A
* root cause confirmed: fix site localized (implementation + verification pending)
* fix implemented: N/A
* no unrelated changes: YES

Phase 0 classification: EXTENDING KF-028. Phase 0 STOP: not triggered. No CONFIRMED/STRONG contradiction.

Bounded question: where should `0x710ca` be invoked, and is its absence a dropped call (regression) or a never-wired gap (incomplete port)?

Symbol resolution (STATICALLY_PROVEN, `apps/rastan-direct/out/symbol.txt`):
- `0x710ca` = `rastan_direct_update_inputs` (`tilemap_hooks.s:1598`), `.global`-exported.
- `0xff60ff` = `genesistan_shadow_input_390007` (and `0xff60fc/fd/fe` = shadow_input_390001/390003/390005). Confirms shadow↔port mapping; gate `0x3ad96` reads `genesistan_shadow_input_390007`.

Phase 1 — Arcade has NO dedicated controller-poll routine; TC0040IOC port reads are scattered inline at consumer sites (0x124,0x3a6,...,0x3a490,0x3ab96,0x3ac04,...). STATICALLY_PROVEN. ⇒ the Genesis shim is a Genesis-introduced consolidation with NO arcade-counterpart call to translate. INFERRED.

Phase 2 — Caller search: `rastan_direct_update_inputs` referenced in source only at the `.global` (line 17) and label (line 1598); NO jsr/bsr/jmp to it in any source file. STATICALLY_PROVEN. Per-frame service is `_vblank_service` (`vdp_comm.s:156`, Level-6 vector `boot.s:92`, runtime `0x700c2`): movem save → display off → commit tiles/bg/fg/sprites/palette/scroll → display on → movem restore → `jmp 0x3a208` (arcade VBlank handler, the shadow consumer). No input call; no NOP/padding/stub gap. STATICALLY_PROVEN. Shim clobbers regs but doesn't save them; `_vblank_service`'s movem bracket covers a call placed inside it (register-safe).

Phase 3 — Dropped-vs-never-wired: NEVER-WIRED (INFERRED, strongly supported). No structural drop indicator (clean complete service, no hole); `.global` export with zero callers in any unit; no arcade counterpart call. Caveat: git history not consulted (out of static scope); `git blame`/log on vdp_comm.s/tilemap_hooks.s would fully confirm.

Recommended call site: `bsr rastan_direct_update_inputs` at the TOP of `_vblank_service` (`vdp_comm.s`, immediately after the `movem.l` save at line 157; runtime within `0x700c2`, before the `0x70100 jmp 0x3a208`). Any point before line 181 is functionally sufficient; top is cleanest and register-safe.
Architectural justification: `_vblank_service` runs every VBlank before handing to the arcade handler that consumes the shadows (gate `0x3ad96` + state-handler reads); refreshing shadows first guarantees valid input each frame, consistent with KF-011 servicing-only model. Evidence category: INFERRED (architectural; consumption path).

Recommended Cody fix SCOPE (recommendation only; not drafted): add one `bsr rastan_direct_update_inputs` at the top of `_vblank_service` in `vdp_comm.s` (no change to the shim itself). After fix, re-run reachability on `0x0003ABFE` to confirm master state stays 0 and the title path is reached. Targets OPEN-004; likely unblocks OPEN-001 downstream.

Completes KF-028's existing "until the shim is wired in" pointer; no new mechanism. Cody applies after Tighe ack.

## [Andy — KF-028 Patched ROM Crash Triage: Address Error Exception Screen]

* files changed: `docs/design/Andy_kf028_patched_rom_address_error_triage.md` (new), `AGENTS_LOG.md` (this single new append; no existing entries modified)
* build produced: NO
* ROM path: N/A (analysis of existing `dist/rastan-direct/fixes/build_0077_kf028_input_shim_wiring/patched_rom.bin`)
* root cause confirmed: failure mode changed + crash localized; full attribution pending one audit
* fix implemented: N/A
* no unrelated changes: YES

Question: why did the KF-028 patched ROM crash to the exception screen, and what does the screen tell us with this build's instruction sequence?

Crash-handler decode (from `crash_handler.s`, group-0 frame, type 3 = ADDRESS ERROR; vector offset `0x0C`=`0x334`=`_crash_stub_address_error` confirmed):
- FAULT PC `0x00000116` = stacked PC. Disasm `2032 3032` = cartridge-header ASCII (" 202…") executed as code. Data, not real code.
- FAULT ADDR `0x00000196` = access address. Header padding (`2020…` ASCII spaces). Data.
- SR `0x013C` = stacked SR (S=0 user mode, IPM=1) — plausibly real, not necessarily artifact.
- On-screen VECTOR `C8` = transcription artifact (handler renders hex8(type)=`03`; if truly 0xC8 the name would decode "OTHER", not "ADDRESS ERROR"). On-screen register block is a regular arithmetic progression inconsistent with the live panel — unreliable transcription.
- `0x518`=`.Lcrash_halt` (stop #0x2700 loop); `0xA16`=`crash_set_cursor` VDP_CTRL write (crash renderer). Both are the handler, not the fault origin.

Conclusion: the CPU was executing the cartridge header (`0x100-0x1FF`, ASCII data) as code after a corrupted control transfer — classic wild-jump/bad-return crash.

Patched-vs-baseline: the +4 byte growth from the inserted `bsr` is at `0x700c6`; `_vblank_service` stayed at `0x700c2`, shim moved `0x710ca→0x710ce`. ALL crash-involved addresses (`0x116/0x196/0x334/0x514/0xA16`) are below `0x700c6` → byte-identical patched-vs-baseline. Disproves "+4 moved the crash code"; does NOT exclude a stale absolute reference to a shifted (≥`0x700c6`) helper as the causal bad-jump.

Path: pre-crash cadence = main loop (`0x3b284/28c/292`) + Level-6 VBlank service helpers (`0x700ae`, `0x719f0`, `0x71a1c/1e`, IPM 6) — NOT the pre-fix watchdog-delay-loop cadence. Stack dump return addresses: `0x3a27e` (arcade VBlank handler tail), `0x3accc` (title sub-state handler — unreachable pre-fix per BM-006), `0x3b292` (main loop). Execution progressed into the title state machine the fix enabled, then jumped into the header and faulted.

Outcome: B — Fix-revealed downstream crash (primary). The shim wiring is architecturally working: cadence changed and stack evidence places execution in the newly-reached title sub-state machine; the run then crashed downstream with an address error to the header. Residual Outcome-A NOT fully excluded (stale absolute reference to a +4-shifted helper could be causal — disproven for crash-code location, not for causation). Outcome-C caveat: VECTOR/register-block transcriptions unreliable, but exception name + group-0 fault decode + stack dump are dependable; decoding is sufficient to classify.

KNOWN_FINDINGS impact: Option A — no update now. Hold the KF-028 refinement until Outcome A is excluded. Once confirmed B, refine KF-028 to note the wiring changed the failure mode (watchdog-state-3 → main-loop/VBlank/title) and exposed a downstream address-error blocker. Do not canonize yet.

Recommended next task: patched-vs-baseline absolute-reference integrity audit — verify the +4 Genesis-native shift propagated to all absolute references into shifted code (94 opcode_replace targets; absolute jsr/jmp to helpers e.g. boot `0x71c7c`/`0x71b72`; level vectors). All valid → exclude A, confirm B, then localize the downstream bad-jump origin in the title path. Any stale → Outcome A (re-relocate that reference). Secondary: re-capture crash screen at higher resolution to resolve the VECTOR/register transcription.

Floor:

- No fix designed/applied; no bookmark cycle; no runtime probing: YES
- Did NOT assume watchdog fixed / title confirmed reached (held as stack evidence with transcription caveat): YES
- Did NOT assume `0x116` is early boot, nor that +4 caused the crash — decoded handler + disassembled patched build + reasoned the shift boundary: YES
- Used this build's actual instruction sequence; did not ignore stack breadcrumbs: YES
- No KF/OPEN/CLOSED file modifications: YES

## [Andy — CORRECTION to KF-028 Crash Triage after Full-Resolution Image Review]

* files changed: `docs/design/Andy_kf028_patched_rom_address_error_triage.md` (revised in place — outcome reclassified B→C), `AGENTS_LOG.md` (this correction append; the prior triage entry above is left intact per log rules)
* build produced: NO
* root cause confirmed: crash-screen numerics proven to be display artifacts; real fault location still unknown
* no unrelated changes: YES

Correction trigger: Tighe supplied the full-resolution crash image. The prior entry (immediately above) classified the crash as Outcome B and treated `FAULT PC=0x116` / `FAULT ADDR=0x196` as the real fault location, and called `VECTOR: C8` a transcription artifact. That was WRONG.

What the full-res image proves: EVERY numeric field on the crash screen equals `row*128 + col*2` (the VRAM cursor offset of that field's own screen position), not the intended value. Root cause is a `crash_handler.s` display bug: `crash_set_cursor` leaves `row*128+col*2` in `%d2` and clobbers it, and every `crash_put_hexN_at` wrapper calls `crash_set_cursor` AFTER the caller loaded the value into `%d2`, so it prints the cursor offset. Verified field-by-field:
- VECTOR r1c36 = 0xC8 ✓ (matches screen `C8` — NOT a transcription error; it is the literal buggy render)
- FAULT PC r2c11 = 0x116 ✓; FAULT ADDR r3c11 = 0x196 ✓; SR r2c30 = 0x13C ✓
- D0 r5c3 = 0x286 ✓; D1 r5c15 = 0x29E ✓; … all D/A/DEST/DIRTY/PAL_D/TILE_D/FRAME fields match `row*128+col*2`.

Reliable fields only: the exception NAME ("ADDRESS ERROR", string-rendered) and the STACK DUMP (loaded via `(a2)+` into `%d2` AFTER the cursor set, then printed inline). The fault PC/addr ARE correctly stored in WRAM (`0xFF6806`, `0xFF6854`) by `_crash_common` but are never displayed.

Reclassified Outcome: C — Crash-report decoding issue. The reported FAULT PC/ADDR do not identify the failing instruction. The earlier "executed the cartridge header at 0x116" narrative is WITHDRAWN (it rested on the bogus 0x116).

What still holds (from reliable evidence, unchanged): exception is a genuine ADDRESS ERROR; the fix changed the failure mode; the stack dump (clean arcade addresses at 2-byte alignment: `0x3A274` [VBlank master-dispatch return], `0x3A27E`, `0x3ACCC` [title sub-state handler], `0x3BD68`, `0x3B292`) plus the live PC run segments place execution in the arcade VBlank master-dispatch / title sub-state machine — newly reached vs pre-fix. A-vs-B is now SUSPENDED pending recovery of the real fault PC.

The crash-handler display bug is PRE-EXISTING (baseline `crash_handler.s`, below the +4 insertion point), not introduced by the KF-028 fix.

KNOWN_FINDINGS impact: Option A — no update (was Option A before; unchanged). The display bug is better tracked as a code defect to fix than a KF entry.

Recommended next task (revised): (1) Cody fix the `crash_handler.s` `%d2`-clobber display bug (preserve `%d2` across `crash_set_cursor`, or load the value after the cursor set), OR dump WRAM crash record `0xFF6804/0x6806/0x6854/0x6816..0x684E` at the halt to read the REAL fault PC/addr/registers; (2) re-triage A-vs-B with the genuine fault PC.

Issue opened:
- `OPEN-015 — crash_handler.s numeric renderer prints cursor offsets instead of saved crash values`

Rationale:
- Andy's corrected KF-028 patched-ROM crash triage identified a project-wide diagnostic defect in `crash_handler.s`: `crash_put_hexN_at` loads a value into `%d2`, then calls `crash_set_cursor`, which clobbers `%d2` with `row*128 + col*2`, causing on-screen numeric fields to print cursor offsets instead of saved crash values.
- Reliable crash-screen fields are the exception name and stack dump only; numeric fields such as `VECTOR`, `FAULT PC`, `FAULT ADDR`, `SR`, registers, and DEST/DIRTY/FRAME are not trustworthy until the renderer is fixed.
- Workaround verified on 2026-06-17: reading the WRAM crash record directly recovered the real KF-028 patched-ROM crash values, including fault PC `0x0003BD68` and fault address `0x50205741`, after the on-screen render showed cursor-offset artifacts.

## [Andy — KF-028 Re-triage with Real Crash Record: PC 0x0003BD68 / Fault Addr 0x50205741]

* files changed: `docs/design/Andy_kf028_real_fault_triage.md` (new), `AGENTS_LOG.md` (this single new append; no existing entries modified)
* build produced: NO
* root cause confirmed: fault locus + mechanism localized (B); exact upstream id/descriptor source pending one caller trace
* fix implemented: N/A
* no unrelated changes: YES

Real crash record read from WRAM `0xFF6800` (only name + stack dump + WRAM record trusted; on-screen numerics remain bogus per prior triage):
- Exception ADDRESS ERROR (type 3); Stacked SR `0x2700` (supervisor, IPM7 → inside VBlank ISR); Stacked PC `0x0003BD68`; Fault addr `0x50205741` (odd → addr error); IR `0x32C2`; SSW `0x000D` (WRITE, supervisor data).
- Register-record caveat (2nd crash-handler bug): `_crash_common` overwrites d1-d5/a0 with frame values and `lea .Lhandler_pc_marker,%a1` before saving, so saved D0=type(3), D1=SR, D2=PC, D3=IR, D4=faultaddr, D5=SSW, A0=SP, A1=0x442(marker) are NOT at-fault regs; only D6/D7/A2-A6 are real (A2=0xC01C18, A3=0x050082, A5=0xFF0000). Real faulting a1 is lost; fault addr from frame is reliable.

Disassembly (0x3bd48 routine, arcade-translated, BELOW 0x700c6 → unshifted, byte-identical to baseline): glyph/string renderer. `andiw #127,%d0; lslw #2,%d0` → idx; `lea 0x3bd7c,%a0; addaw %d0; moveal (a0),%a0` → a0=table[idx] (128 ROM descriptor ptrs); `moveal (a0)+,%a1` → a1=descriptor[0]=dest ptr; `movew %d2,(a1)+` at 0x3bd66 = faulting write (IR 0x32C2 matches); 0x3bd68 (movew %d0,(a1)+) = imprecise "next-instr" stacked PC.

Faulting op + source: word WRITE through a1 at 0x3bd66; a1 = descriptor[0] = `0x50205741` = ASCII "P WA" = text/glyph data dereferenced as a destination pointer. table(0x3bd7c)+descriptors(0x3bcxx) are unshifted ROM, so the bad value is produced by the path being DRIVEN with an input that resolves table[idx] to text — input arrives from the title text-producer path the KF-028 fix newly enabled.

Stack/call-context: consistent. main loop → Level-6 VBlank → master dispatch (pea 0x3a274) → title sub-state handler (0x3accc) → glyph renderer (0x3bd68) → addr-error write; SR=0x2700 confirms VBlank ISR. Matches KF-013.

Outcome: B — Fix-revealed downstream issue. Wiring correct; execution advanced into newly-reached title text path; crash is a downstream glyph-renderer bug (text-as-pointer deref). Outcome A not supported (faulting routine/table/descriptors unshifted; no stale reference evidence) — speculative +4 audit not warranted.

Recommended next task: trace the caller of glyph renderer `0x3bd48` from the title sub-state path (0x3accc) — what char/string-id (d0) it passes and the WRAM/ROM state computing it; read ROM table 0x3bd7c[idx] + the descriptor to confirm bad-index vs malformed-descriptor source of 0x50205741. Bounded to that. Secondary: Cody fix BOTH crash-handler defects (display %d2-clobber + register-save clobber) so future crash reports are trustworthy.

KNOWN_FINDINGS impact: Option C — proposed KF-028 refinement (A-vs-B now classified as B): wiring changed failure mode to main-loop/VBlank/title; downstream ADDRESS ERROR write at `0x0003BD66` in glyph renderer `0x3bd48` (dest ptr = text `0x50205741`); not a layout-shift (unshifted ROM machinery); exact upstream id/descriptor source pending caller trace. STRONG (locus/write/text-as-pointer/unshifted) / WORKING_HYPOTHESIS (exact upstream root). Cross-ref KF-013. Cody applies after Tighe ack.

## [Andy — KF-028 Caller Trace: Source of Bad Glyph-Renderer Input at 0x3BD48]

* files changed: `docs/design/Andy_kf028_glyph_renderer_caller_trace.md` (new), `AGENTS_LOG.md` (this single new append; no existing entries modified)
* build produced: NO
* root cause confirmed: full fault chain proven; U3-vs-U2 provenance pending one check
* fix implemented: N/A
* no unrelated changes: YES

Closed the gap from the real-fault triage by identifying the active caller, d0 source, and table/descriptor path (all in unshifted arcade-translated ROM, byte-identical to baseline).

Phase 1 — `0x3BD48` reached only by direct `bsrw` (~48 sites); no indirect/table route. Each caller sets d0 via a preceding `moveq #N`.

Phase 2 — active caller (renderer is a leaf; top return address above the group-0 frame is the caller's): **`0x0003ACC8 bsrw 0x3bd48`**, return addr `0x3ACCC` = the prior reliable stack breadcrumb. STATICALLY_PROVEN. In the title sub-state handler (`0x3ACAE`/`0x3ACCC`; SR 0x2700 = VBlank ISR; KF-013).

Phase 3 — d0 source: hardcoded `moveq #65,%d0` at `0x3ACC6` (static immediate, arcade-intended; part of the run 17,63,64,65,66,67,68,69,70). NOT WRAM/computed/uninitialized → rules out U1.

Phase 4 — `idx = 65`; `table` base `0x3BD7C`; `table[65]` @ `0x3BE80` = `0x0003C246`; descriptor[0] @ `0x3C246` = bytes `50 20 57 41` = `0x50205741` = EXACTLY the recorded fault address ("P WA", odd → addr-error write at `0x3BD66`). Region `0x3C2xx` is ASCII text ("SELECT","WARP","1P","2P",spaces) → table[65] points into text data, not a descriptor header. Sampled other entries also lack valid Genesis dest pointers (table[0]→0x0B1E1E0C, table[63]→0x00003150, table[64]→0x8C200000). STATICALLY_PROVEN.

Phase 5 — Upstream cause: **U3 — missing/incomplete translation** (primary): the title text-render descriptor table/data is arcade-origin verbatim and carries no valid Genesis destination pointers in descriptor[0]; renderer writes (attr,glyph) tilemap pairs to descriptor[0] as dest → garbage/odd → crash. Alternative **U2** (table mis-targets text / malformed descriptors). NOT U1, NOT layout shift (unshifted ROM). Consistent with prior Outcome B.

Phase 6 — Recommended next task: determine descriptor/table provenance for the title-string indices — (1) how the `0x3BD7C` table + `0x3BCxx-0x3C2xx` descriptors are produced and how descriptor[0] dest is meant to be set/translated for Genesis; (2) cross-check arcade reference (arcade `0x3bb48` = Genesis `0x3bd48-0x200`) for how descriptor[0] resolves there. Pins U3-vs-U2 and the exact fix locus. Bounded; no +4 audit; no title-path survey. (Separately: Cody fix the two crash-handler defects.)

KNOWN_FINDINGS impact: Option C — proposed KF-028 refinement (caller trace landed; full chain known): title sub-state handler `0x3ACC8` (d0=65) → table[65]=`0x3C246` (text) → descriptor[0]=`0x50205741` → odd-addr write at `0x3BD66`; descriptor table/data is unshifted arcade ROM with no valid Genesis dest pointers (U3; possibly U2). Full chain STATICALLY_PROVEN; U3-vs-U2 root WORKING_HYPOTHESIS pending provenance check. Cross-ref KF-013. Cody applies after Tighe ack.

## [Andy — KF-028 Title Text Descriptor Provenance: U3 vs U2]

* files changed: `docs/design/Andy_kf028_title_text_descriptor_provenance.md` (new), `AGENTS_LOG.md` (this single new append; no existing entries modified)
* build produced: NO
* root cause confirmed: YES — U3, exact mechanism + fix locus identified
* fix implemented: N/A
* no unrelated changes: YES

Question: is the title-text table/descriptor data untranslated arcade-origin (U3) or malformed/mis-targeted (U2)?

Findings (Genesis = arcade + 0x200, KF-006):
- Descriptors WERE relocated correctly: Genesis `0x3BE98` (=arcade 0x3BC98) = `00C08F4C | 0000 | "BEST…"`; Genesis `0x3C446` (=arcade 0x3C246) = `00C0914C | 0000 | "OTHERW…"`. Both well-formed `[long dest=0x00C0xxxx][word attr][text]`. STATICALLY_PROVEN.
- The descriptor-pointer TABLE at `0x3BD7C` was NOT relocated: Genesis entries are IDENTICAL to arcade (`table[0]=0x0003BC98`, `table[65]=0x0003C246` in BOTH). So table[65] points 0x200 below the real relocated descriptor (0x3C446), into text `…"P WA"…` (0x50205741, odd) → fault. STATICALLY_PROVEN.
- Arcade side is valid: arcade table[65]=0x3C246 → arcade descriptor[0]=0x00C0914C (valid even dest). The arcade data is correct; only the Genesis table pointers are stale.
- Tooling gap: `tools/translation/postpatch_lenient.py` relocates absolute targets in INSTRUCTION operands (jsr/jmp; `absolute_rom_target_relocation`) but NOT absolute pointers embedded in DATA tables. The 0x3BD7C table is data → left at pre-relocation arcade values. The renderer reaches the table via PC-relative `lea %pc@(0x3bd7c)` (auto-relocated), so only the table's absolute contents are stale.

Descriptor dest (0x00C0xxxx) is a hardware-region address, correctly NOT +0x200-relocated (outside ROM window), identical arcade↔Genesis. Only the table's ROM-range pointers (0x3Bxxxx–0x3C2xx) needed +0x200.

Classification: **U3 — missing/incomplete Genesis translation** (un-relocated absolute descriptor-pointer table). NOT U2 (data valid, descriptors well-formed & correctly relocated), NOT layout shift, NOT U1.

Exact fix locus: relocate the absolute descriptor-pointer table at Genesis ROM offset `0x3BD7C` by +0x200 per entry (128 longwords, ~0x3BD7C..0x3BF7C) so table[idx] → relocated descriptors (table[65]→0x3C446, dest 0x00C0914C, even). 

Recommended next task (Cody, investigate→implement; not designed here): (1) relocate the 0x3BD7C table +0x200 via rastan_direct_remap.json/postpatch (confirm exact table length first); (2) SURVEY for other absolute data-pointer tables in the relocated blob with the same gap — postpatch only relocates instruction operands, so other embedded pointer tables are latent crashes. Separately: Cody fix the two crash-handler defects.

KNOWN_FINDINGS impact: Option C — proposed KF-028 refinement with the U3 root (un-relocated 0x3BD7C table; descriptors correctly relocated; postpatch relocates instruction operands not data-pointer tables). STRONG/CONFIRMED. Flagged a possible GLOBAL KF for the general gap pending the table survey. Cody applies after Tighe ack.

Floor:
- Files changed: only the design doc + this AGENTS_LOG append: YES
- No source/spec/tool/Makefile/ROM modifications; no bookmark; no runtime probing; no +4 audit: YES
- Trusted only exception name + stack dump + WRAM record (no on-screen numerics): YES
- U3-vs-U2 classified before recommending fix: YES
- Did NOT broaden into a general title-system survey (bounded to 0x3BD7C table + descriptor region + arcade ref): YES
- KNOWN_FINDINGS.md NOT modified; no issues opened/closed: YES
- STOP triggered: NO

## [Andy — Title Render VBlank/VDP Ownership Audit (after OPEN-016 Part 1)]

* files changed: `docs/design/Andy_title_render_vblank_vdp_ownership_audit.md` (new), `AGENTS_LOG.md` (this single new append; no existing entries modified)
* build produced: NO
* root cause confirmed: YES — R1, destination-translation gap localized
* fix implemented: N/A
* no unrelated changes: YES

Question: after OPEN-016 Part 1, when the title renderer writes to descriptor dest `0x00C0914C`, where do those writes go and what VBlank/VDP path commits them?

Findings:
- `0x00C0914C` is PC080SN FG-tilemap hardware (AGENTS.md: 0xC00000-0xC0FFFF = PC080SN; tilemap_hooks CWINDOW_BASE_FG=0xC08000; 0xC0914C in FG C-window). On Genesis 0xC0xxxx IS the VDP I/O region → the renderer's `movew %d2,(a1)+` writes hit VDP ports directly with no control-port address setup. STATICALLY_PROVEN/DOCUMENTED + INFERRED (Genesis HW).
- The port translates PC080SN access via FUNCTION-level hooks (remap spec): number renderer `0x3C2E2`, text writers `0x3C4D2/550/586/636/6dc/75c/7a4/830/950` — routed to `staged_fg_buffer`, "prevent direct C-window FG writes". The glyph renderer `0x3BD48` is NOT hooked; the only OPEN-016 remap entry on it (line 726) relocates its descriptor table, not its writes. STATICALLY_PROVEN/DOCUMENTED.
- `0x3BD48` is reached DIRECTLY from the title sub-state handler (`0x3ACC8 bsrw 0x3bd48`) and ~48 other direct sites, none routed through the hooked 0x3c2e2-0x3c950 functions → its writes hit PC080SN raw.
- VBlank commit inventory (`_vblank_service`): vdp_commit_tiles/bg_strips/fg_strips/sprites/palette/scroll; all visible-plane commits read Genesis WRAM staging (`staged_bg_buffer`/`staged_fg_buffer`) → VRAM Plane B(0xC000)/A(0xE000). The title renderer populates NO staging buffer and sets NO dirty flag → orphaned from the commit pipeline.

Ownership analysis: not a two-routine stomp / not a dirty-flag race — the renderer is an ORPHANED WRITER. Committed planes come only from staging (which it never fills), while its raw 0xC0xxxx writes poke the VDP directly.

Garbled-output explanation: raw address-less VDP-port writes scatter (attr,glyph) words → sparse top-of-screen garbage; coherent FG text never forms (never staged); input advances title sub-state → different d0/glyphs → different garbage; no crash (dests even). Fully explained without the old 0x50205741 crash (fixed) or the Start→C→A crash (out of scope).

Classification: **R1 — destination translation missing.** Renderer `0x3BD48` writes PC080SN FG-tilemap dests (0xC08000-0xC0FFFF) not translated to Genesis VDP staging/commit; the hooked text writers (0x3c2e2-0x3c950) handle other text but not this renderer path. Not R2/R3 (commit path exists; renderer just doesn't feed it). R4 secondary at most.

Bounded recommendation: extend the FG text-writer hook pattern to cover `0x3BD48` — redirect its PC080SN FG C-window writes into `staged_fg_buffer` + set `fg_row_dirty`, mirroring `genesistan_hook_text_writer_3c4d2`/…; decide renderer-level hook (covers all ~48 callers) vs title-caller-only (renderer-level is higher leverage); verify FG-staging offset math (CWINDOW_BASE_FG subtraction). Scope to the title glyph-render write path; no graphics rewrite; not the Start→C→A crash or crash-handler defects.

KNOWN_FINDINGS impact: Option C — proposed KF-028 refinement (renderer reads valid descriptors post-OPEN-016 but writes PC080SN dests directly to VDP; unhooked unlike 0x3c2e2-0x3c950; R1 staging-bypass garble). STRONG/CONFIRMED. Cross-ref KF-010/KF-013/KF-028, OPEN-001. Assess-only; KNOWN_FINDINGS.md not modified.

OPEN issue impact: OPEN-016 continues (Part 1 relocated table; next part = translate/hook the renderer's writes) — NOT closed. OPEN-001 relevant context (staging-bypass is one instance), no status change. New issues: NONE. Closed: NONE.

Phase 1.4 renderer semantics proof:
- Descriptor layout: `[long PC080SN dest][word attr][zero-terminated bytes]`
- Normal mode: one byte emits attr word then glyph word
- Negative low-byte mode: one byte emits attr word then `0x0020` space word
- Termination: zero byte consumed before `RTS`
- Input contract: `d0` renderer ID / signed-byte mode source
- Output contract: original clobbers `d0/d1/d2/a0/a1`; final `d0=0`, `a0` after terminator, `a1` advanced four bytes per emitted character; callers do not depend on output registers before resetting state
- Unresolved control bytes/sentinels: NONE for caller-reached descriptors
- Acceptance: PASS

- Hook name: `genesistan_hook_glyph_renderer_3bd48`
- Remap entry: arcade source `0x03BB48` -> runtime Genesis `0x03BD48`

- Staging path: hook replays original index/header/string loop, calls existing `.Ltw_store_from_components_at_a2` with `a2 = a1 + 2`, and advances original `a1 += 4` per emitted character
- Static staging verification: generated disassembly shows `0x70800` subtracts `0x00C08000`, `0x70794` writes `staged_fg_buffer`, and `0x7079E` sets `fg_row_dirty`

- Operational assessment: likely Build 0091 / OPEN-016 Part 2 regression, but exact mechanism remains unresolved from video alone because hook PCs were not sampled and real crash values require WRAM verification.

## [Andy — Analysis, Build 0091 Helper Crash Triage]

* files changed: `docs/design/Andy_build_0091_helper_crash_triage.md` (new), `AGENTS_LOG.md` (this single new append; no existing entries modified)
* build produced: NO
* root cause confirmed: YES (classification A)
* fix designed: NO (bounded recommendation only)
* no unrelated changes: YES
* architecture compliance: CONFIRMED (hook = arcade-called helper, RTS, hardware-dest translation; fix is production-intent register setup, no scaffolding/lifecycle)

Phase 0: EXTENDING (refines KF-028/OPEN-016). Priors: KF-028, KF-013, KF-010, KF-004/006. No CONFIRMED/STRONG contradiction. Used WRAM crash record (on-screen fields are OPEN-015 artifacts).

Faulting instruction: `runtime_genesis_pc 0x00070794: 3d81 2000 movew %d1,%fp@(0,%d2:w)` (= `move.w %d1,(%a6+%d2.w)`). IR=0x3D81 MATCHES exactly; stacked PC 0x00070796 = the `2000` extension word (m68k group-0 imprecise PC). STATICALLY_PROVEN.

Operation producing fault addr 0x00000F41: staging write `staged_fg_buffer[row*128+col*2]`, dest = `%a6 + %d2.w`. `%a6` MUST be `staged_fg_buffer` (WRAM 0x00FF501A); fault addr 0x00000F41 is low/odd → `%a6` was NOT loaded with the staging base (held a stale value). Chain: hook 0x70BC8 → 0x707BC → 0x707E4 → 0x70816 → 0x7078A → 0x70794; chain also needs `%a3` (tile LUT) and `%a5` (attr LUT).

Hook-vs-text-writer convention: every `genesistan_hook_text_writer_*` (e.g. 3c550 `tilemap_hooks.s:1090`, 3c586 `:1122`) does `lea genesistan_pc080sn_tile_vram_lut,%a3` / `lea genesistan_pc080sn_attr_lut,%a5` / `lea staged_fg_buffer,%a6` before calling the shared store. `genesistan_hook_glyph_renderer_3bd48.Lgr_store_cell` (`:1078`) calls the SAME shared helper `.Ltw_store_from_components_at_a2` (=0x707BC) but sets NONE of `%a3/%a5/%a6` → `%a6` stale → write to 0x00000F41 (odd) → address error. STATICALLY_PROVEN/DOCUMENTED.

Crash classification: A — Hook behavior bug. The glyph hook omits the base-register setup the shared FG-staging store helper requires (immediate fault: missing `%a6=staged_fg_buffer`; also missing `%a3`/`%a5` LUT bases). Helper is correct (works for text writers). Not C (not a structural mismatch), not B, not D.

Bounded recommendation: single fix in the hook — add `lea genesistan_pc080sn_tile_vram_lut,%a3` / `lea genesistan_pc080sn_attr_lut,%a5` / `lea staged_fg_buffer,%a6` before `bsr .Ltw_store_from_components_at_a2` (at hook entry, loop-invariant, or inside `.Lgr_store_cell` after its movem save), mirroring text-writer hooks 3c550/3c586. `%a6` fixes the crash; `%a3`/`%a5` needed for correct output. Do not modify the shared helper.

KNOWN_FINDINGS impact: Option C — proposed KF-028 refinement (Build 0091 crash = glyph hook missing `%a6=staged_fg_buffer` + `%a3`/`%a5` setup the text-writer hooks satisfy; hook register-setup bug, not staging-data fault). STRONG/CONFIRMED. NOT applied (assess-only; Cody applies after Tighe ack).

Floor:
- Files changed: only the design doc + this AGENTS_LOG append: YES
- No source/spec/tool/Makefile/ROM modifications; no fix applied; no bookmark; no runtime probing: YES
- Used WRAM record only (no on-screen numeric fields per OPEN-015); IR-confirmed faulting instruction (did not assert 0x70794 without IR match): YES
- A/B/C/D classified before recommendation; single fix locus; no broadening: YES
- KNOWN_FINDINGS.md not modified; OPEN-016 not closed: YES
- STOP triggered: NO

## [Andy — Investigation Design, Build 0092 VDP/VBlank Refresh Frequency]

* files changed: `docs/design/Andy_build_0092_vdp_vblank_refresh_frequency_investigation.md` (new), `AGENTS_LOG.md` (this single new append; no existing entries modified)
* build produced: NO
* root cause confirmed: NO (investigation design only)
* fix designed: NO
* no unrelated changes: YES
* architecture compliance: CONFIRMED (violation criterion = Genesis-visible VDP churn, not arcade-VBlank execution)

Phase 0: EXTENDING (downstream of OPEN-001/OPEN-016 graphics failure). Priors KF-028/013/011/010/004/006/001. No CONTRADICTION. KF-001 watchdog referenced (does not write VDP), not rediscovered.

VDP write-path map: VDP_DATA=0xC00000, VDP_CTRL=0xC00004; VDP decodes 0xC00000-0xDFFFFF so PC080SN(0xC0xxxx)/scroll(0xC2/0xC4)/sprites(0xD0xxxx) alias VDP. Direct data-port writes (15 sites) almost all in `_vblank_service` commit helpers (0x70122/0x70160/0x701ae/0x701e4/0x701fa/0x70204/0x70218/0x70222) + boot/crash; control-port writes (22) = boot/crash reg sets + per-frame MODE2 toggle. Arcade PC080SN/scroll/sprite writes are REGISTER-INDIRECT (pointer=0xC0xxxx) → not in absolute greps → not statically enumerable. ~102 opcode_replace hooks redirect KNOWN writer functions to WRAM staging (staged_bg/fg_buffer, staged_sprite_sat, staged_scroll_*); any writer NOT in that set writes raw to VDP.

Canonical contract (STATICALLY_PROVEN): `_vblank_service` (0x700C2, Level-6 vector) runs once/VBlank: MODE2 off → commit tiles/bg/fg/sprites/(palette if dirty)/scroll → MODE2 on → jmp 0x3A208. vdp_commit_*/vdp_set_reg called only from vdp_comm.s (no external callers). Display toggled once/frame; plane bases/mode set once at boot.

Suspect paths: S1 (primary) unhooked register-indirect writers to 0xC0/C2/C4/D0 → raw VDP per frame (not statically enumerable; the deferred unhooked-writer survey); S2 commit re-entry/extra cycles; S3 extra display-enable toggles; S4 (FAIL-alt) bad staged data (descriptor/LUT/%a3/%a5/plane-base/palette/patterns). Watchdog 0x3A180 referenced (no VDP writes), not a suspect.

Measurement spec for Cody (runtime, read-only emulator observer; no scaffolding): M1 watchpoints on VDP ports 0xC00000-0xC00007 (+mirrors 0xC08000/0xC20000/0xC40000/0xD00000), log (frame,PC,port,value) over ≥10 steady-state no-input frames; M2 classify writer PC canonical vs non-canonical; M3 per-frame counters (total writes / distinct PCs / non-canonical / reg-1 toggles / full commit cycles); M4 top-N non-canonical writers; M5 dump Plane A/B nametable + CRAM + staged_fg/bg_buffer at VBlank exit to distinguish churn (S1-S3) from bad-data (S4).

Pass/fail: PASS if >1 commit cycle/frame, repeated mid-frame nametable/CRAM/display churn, non-canonical (esp. arcade-region) VDP-port writes, repeated display toggles, or racing producers. FAIL if exactly one commit cycle/frame, no non-canonical VDP writes, stable display/bases → S4 staged-data investigation.

Static evidence sufficient to rule out hypothesis: NO — canonical path proven one-commit/VBlank, but unhooked register-indirect VDP-aliased writers are not statically enumerable → Cody runtime measurement required.

KNOWN_FINDINGS impact: Option A — no update (design task; no new durable mechanism proven; refinement waits on measurement). KNOWN_FINDINGS.md not modified.

Recommended next task: Cody runtime VDP-write profiling (M1-M5) over a no-input steady-state window; result selects next step (PASS → hook the offending unhooked writer; FAIL → S4 staged-data-correctness investigation).

Floor:
- Files changed: only the design doc + this AGENTS_LOG append: YES
- No source/spec/tool/Makefile/ROM modifications; no build; no runtime probing; no bookmark; no fix: YES
- Did NOT re-inspect the Build 0092 video (read Cody's evidence doc as documentation): YES
- Did NOT treat arcade-VBlank execution as the violation (criterion = VDP-visible churn): YES
- Did NOT rediscover KF-001 watchdog (referenced as non-VDP); did not broaden to graphics rewrite; did not investigate Start→C→A; did not close OPEN-016/OPEN-001: YES
- KNOWN_FINDINGS.md not modified: YES

## [Andy — Static Audit, Single VBlank Lifecycle Placement]

* files changed: `docs/design/Andy_build_0092_vblank_lifecycle_placement_audit.md` (new), `AGENTS_LOG.md` (this single new append; no existing entries modified)
* build produced: NO
* root cause confirmed: NO (audit/classification only)
* fix designed: NO (bounded recommendation only)
* no unrelated changes: YES
* architecture compliance: CONFIRMED (single arcade-controlled VBlank lifecycle; classified evidentially)

Phase 0: EXTENDING (tests validity of Cody's 0x70100 Layer-1 sample point). Priors KF-028/013/011/010/004/006/001. No contradiction.

Single VBlank lifecycle map (STATICALLY_PROVEN): HW Level-6 (vector 0x78 → 0x700C2) → `_vblank_service`: [0x700C2 movem save] → [0x700C6 bsr 0x710CE = input-mirror update] → [DISPLAY OFF] → commits tiles(0x7010A)/bg(0x70134)/fg(0x70182)/sprites(0x719B4)/palette(if dirty, clr 0xff4000)/scroll(0x701F0) → [DISPLAY ON] → [0x70100 movem restore] → [0x70104 jmp 0x3A208 = arcade VBlank body] → master dispatch %a5@(0) at 0x3A256 → title sub-state handler → title PRODUCERS (glyph renderer 0x3BD48 + hooks) write staged_fg/bg_buffer → rte (0x3A27E).

Two decisive facts:
1. Within one lifecycle, COMMITS run BEFORE the arcade producers (deferred-commit pattern: commit prior frame's staging at VBlank start under display-off, then produce this frame's staging for next commit). Input update correctly precedes the producer body.
2. Commits do NOT clear staging — vdp_commit_bg/fg_strips read staged_bg/fg_buffer into VDP_DATA and clear only the dirty flag (bclr → bg_row_dirty/fg_row_dirty); tiles clears tiles_dirty only. So staged content PERSISTS across frames until a producer overwrites it.

Location of 0x70100: verified = `moveml %sp@+` restore immediately before `jmp 0x3A208`. After all commits + display-on + dirty-clears; before this lifecycle's producers.

Should staged content exist at 0x70100? YES in steady state — producers run in the prior lifecycle (step 13), output persists through the commit (commit doesn't clear staging), so the prior frame's title cells should be present and equal to this frame's expected output. Empty across the steady window ⟹ producers genuinely not populating staging (dirty-clear at 0x70100 is expected post-commit state, not independent evidence).

Classification: C — current placement semantically correct; empty-buffer evidence implies real producer failure. A ruled out (commit does NOT clear staging → no silent wipe at the sample point; deferred commit is legitimate, not wrongly front-loaded). B not indicated (input already before producers; commit/dirty-clear after prior producers). D not needed (placement fully determinable statically).

Cody's Layer 1 conclusion validity: SUPPORTED. "Title producers do not populate staged_fg/bg_buffer" stands — the sample point captures persisted output and the commit does not clear staging.

Recommended next task: producer trace (bounded) of the title producer→staging path in the arcade VBlank body — (1) is the title sub-state producer path reached each lifecycle (master %a5@(0)==0 → 0x3ABFE → handler → renderer)? (2) when the glyph renderer hook / FG-BG producers run, do they write staged_fg/bg_buffer — verify the Build 0092 OPEN-016 Part-2 fix actually set %a6=staged_fg_buffer + %a3/%a5 LUTs so the staging write lands (continuation of Andy_build_0091_helper_crash_triage; "no crash" ≠ "writes staging"); (3) do producer dests fall in the hooked C-window ranges (BG 0xC00000-0xC03FFF, FG 0xC08000-0xC0BFFF)? Preserves the single-lifecycle deferred-commit architecture; no rewrite.

KNOWN_FINDINGS impact: Option C — proposed refinement of KF-011/KF-013 recording the explicit single-lifecycle ordering + the staging-persistence fact (commit clears only dirty flags, never the staged buffers; a post-commit/pre-producer sample validly reflects persisted producer output). STRONG/CONFIRMED. NOT applied (assess-only; Cody applies after Tighe ack).

Floor:
- Files changed: only the design doc + this AGENTS_LOG append: YES
- No source/spec/tool/Makefile/ROM modifications; no build; no runtime probing; no bookmark; no fix: YES
- Single-lifecycle framing (no "Genesis VBlank vs arcade VBlank" as two lifecycles): YES
- Did NOT pre-judge `_vblank_service` as scaffolding/wrong; classified evidentially (deferred commit is legitimate): YES
- Did NOT drift into producer-tracing before classifying placement; did not broaden to graphics rewrite: YES
- KNOWN_FINDINGS.md not modified; OPEN-001/OPEN-016 not closed: YES
- STOP triggered: NO

## [Andy — Analysis, Build 0092 Title Producer to Staging Trace]

* files changed: `docs/design/Andy_build_0092_title_producer_staging_trace.md` (new), `AGENTS_LOG.md` (this single new append; no existing entries modified)
* build produced: NO
* ROM path: N/A
* root cause confirmed: NO (P5 — static evidence insufficient; bounded runtime spec provided)
* fix implemented: NO (fix locus only)
* no unrelated changes: YES
* architecture compliance: CONFIRMED (glyph renderer = arcade-called hook, RTS return; no Genesis control-flow ownership proposed)

Disasm confirmed = Build 0092 (same mtime as ROM; hook fix `lea 0xff501a,%fp` at 0x70bd8 present).

Producer chain (all links STATICALLY_PROVEN correct):
- Steady state (Cody WRAM): %a5@(0)=0, %a5@(2)=1, %a5@(4)=2 → dispatch 0x3A256→0x3ABFE→0x3AC90→(%a5@(4)=2)→0x3AD00→jsr 0x712B4 (genesistan_palette_hook_03ab00 — PALETTE only; does NOT clear tilemap staging).
- Title FG glyph producer = 0x3ACAE (runs only at %a5@(4)==1, one-shot); unconditional renders moveq #17/63/64/65/66/67/68/69/70 → bsrw 0x3BD48.
- 0x3BD48 = `jsr 0x70B8E` (opcode-replaced → glyph hook). Dispatch CONFIRMED.
- Phase 3: hook .Lgr_store_cell (0x70BC8) sets %a3=tile_vram_lut, %a5=attr_lut, %a6=staged_fg_buffer (0xff501a) before the store — Build 0091 fix present. Writes staging when run.
- Phase 4: FG-range gate 0x707E4 accepts dest 0xC0914C (in [0xC08000,0xC0C000)). cwindow_clear (0x710D6, zeros both staging buffers) called only from 0x563B6 (game loop), NOT the steady title handler.
- Phase 5: store 0x70794 (movew %d1,(%a6+%d2)) lands in-bounds in the 2048-word staged_fg_buffer.
- Phase 6: Part-1 relocation intact — table[65]@0x3BE80=0x0003C446; descriptor 0x3C446 = dest 0x00C0914C, attr 0, glyph "OTHERW..." (non-empty).

Classification: P5 — static evidence insufficient. Every static link is correct, and nothing clears staging in the steady title state, so IF 0x3ACAE executed its renders, staged_fg_buffer would be non-empty AND persist (commits clear only dirty flags). It is empty. Unresolvable statically because it turns on runtime execution history: (a) %a5@(4)=2 has FIVE writers (0x3a406/0x3a96a/0x3a9e6/0x3aa58/0x3acf8) — the steady (0,1,2) state does NOT prove 0x3ACAE ran; (b) Cody sampled only the steady window (~frame 600), far after the one-shot title-entry where the producer runs, so the producer execution is unobserved.

Decisive evidence: the d0=17 render at 0x3ACB6 → 0x3BD48 → 0x70B8E → store 0x70794 should place the first nonzero word in staged_fg_buffer; statically it would; why it doesn't appear cannot be determined without observing the producer at title entry.

P5 bounded runtime measurement (single MAME task; decides P1/P2/P3/P4): from reset through title-entry (frames ~0-120, not only 600): BP 0x3ACAE/0x3ACB6 (count + log %a5@(0/2/4)); BP 0x70794 (count/frame + log %a6/%d2/%d1); write-watchpoint WRAM 0xFF501A..0xFF601A (FG) and 0xFF401A..0xFF501A (BG) logging (frame,PC,offset,value). Decision: producer never runs → P1; runs but 0x70794 never hit → P2/P3 (range-gate/descriptor logs distinguish); 0x70794 writes then later filled with clear-cell → report clearing PC (fix locus = that clear); writes+persist but frame-600 read empty → re-examine the read.

Fix locus (pending measurement): the title FG producer EXECUTION at entry (which %a5@(4)=2 writer fires; whether 0x3ACAE's render handler runs) — NOT the glyph hook (verified correct), NOT commit/clear placement (settled Classification C). Did NOT pre-blame the hook.

KNOWN_FINDINGS impact: Option A — No new finding to index. Verifies existing structure; lands at P5; no durable new fact until the runtime measurement resolves P1/P2/P3/P4.

Required evidence results:
- `0x3ACAE` producer entry executed once at frame 212 with `%a5@(0/2/4)=0/1/1`.
- `0x3ACB6` first render executed once at frame 212 with `d0=0x11`.
- `0x707E4` FG range gate hit 258 times and `0x70816` accept path hit 258 times; no range rejection observed.
- `0x70794` FG staging store hit 258 times with `%a6=0x00FF501A` and in-buffer offsets, but every store had `%d1=0x0000`.
- FG staging watchpoint saw 2306 writes and 0 nonzero writes.
- BG staging was observe-only: 9944 writes and 0 nonzero writes; no BG producer investigation performed.
- Only `%a5@(4)=2` writer site observed was `0x3ACF8`; the alternate writer sites did not fire.

Resolved class: P2 — producer emits no nonzero staged cells (zero-cell output). This is not P1 (producer non-reach), not P3 (range rejection), and not P4 (later clear), because the producer runs, the accepted store lands in `staged_fg_buffer`, and no nonzero cell is ever produced.

Component probe finding: tile LUT results become nonzero after `0x707D2` (example `d7_tile=0x001F`), but by `0x707E0` the final composed cell is `d1_cell=0x0000`; the zero value is then written at `0x70794`. Evidence points to cell-value composition before the FG staging write.

## [Andy — Analysis, Build 0092 FG Cell-Composition Diagnosis]

* files changed: `docs/design/Andy_build_0092_fg_cell_composition_diagnosis.md` (new), `AGENTS_LOG.md` (this single new append; no existing entries modified)
* build produced: NO
* ROM path: N/A
* root cause confirmed: YES (F4 — proven from disasm + register lifecycle)
* fix implemented: NO (fix locus + shape only)
* no unrelated changes: YES
* architecture compliance: CONFIRMED (register-preservation correctness fix in an arcade-called helper; no Genesis control-flow ownership)

Phase 0: EXTENDING (KF-028/OPEN-016). No CONFIRMED/STRONG KF contradiction ("text writers reported working" is a task premise, not a KF — refuted statically, as the prompt invited).

Fix-locus classification: F4 — attr lookup clobbers the tile register.

Loss instruction: runtime_genesis_pc 0x707DE `movew %d7,%d1` (= tilemap_hooks.s:645 `move.w %d7,%d1`). It reads %d7 after %d7 was clobbered by `.Ltw_translate_attr`.

Mechanism (STATICALLY_PROVEN, disasm + source):
- `.Ltw_compose_d1_from_d0_d2` (0x707C6 / tilemap_hooks.s:634): 0x707CE (line 638) loads tile into %d7 = tile_vram_lut[d0]; 0x707DA (line 643) bsr `.Ltw_translate_attr`; 0x707DE (line 645) movew %d7,%d1; 0x707E0 (line 646) or.w %d2,%d1.
- `.Ltw_translate_attr` (0x70752 / tilemap_hooks.s:578-605) uses %d7 as scratch 3× (move.w %d2,%d7 at lines 582/589/596), NO save/restore (no movem; exit %d7=((d2>>13)&1)<<4). So tile (loaded at line 638) is destroyed before line 645 reads it.
- Stored cell d1 = clobbered-%d7 | attr_lut[%d2]; tile-index bits absent → 0x0000 when attr_lut value is 0. Runtime corroborated (d7 0x001F→0x00).

Helper register contract (.Ltw_store_from_components_at_a2): %d0=tile/glyph index, %d2=raw attr word, %a3=tile_vram_lut, %a5=attr_lut, %a6=staged_fg_buffer, %a2=dest C-window addr. Tile carried internally in %d7, contract ASSUMES %d7 survives `.Ltw_translate_attr` — it does not (the defect).

Glyph-hook vs text-writer comparison: BOTH enter the SAME label `.Ltw_store_from_components_at_a2` → `.Ltw_compose_d1_from_d0_d2` → `.Ltw_translate_attr`. Glyph hook `.Lgr_store_cell:1088`; text writers `_3c550:1116`/`_3c586:1209/1223` → `.Ltw_write_pair_same:672` → same helper. So the known-good hooks would NOT survive the glyph hook's path — they take it identically. The "text writers reported working" premise is REVISED: per static analysis they share the d7-clobber (may only appear to render where attr_lut≠0, producing wrong-tile cells). USER MUST VERIFY text-writer visual output.

Ruled out: F1 (glyph-hook contract mismatch — its a3/a5/a6/d0/d2 setup matches text writers), F2 (wrong entry label — both use the same label), F5 (compose reads the correct register — d1 is right; the problem is d7 is clobbered before the read). Not F6 (mechanism proven statically).

Fix shape (no implementation): locus `.Ltw_compose_d1_from_d0_d2`/`.Ltw_translate_attr`. Option A (byte-neutral, PREFERRED): change `.Ltw_translate_attr` scratch from %d7 to a free reg (e.g. %d3), leaving the tile in %d7 — pure register rename, 0-byte delta, no invariant shift; follow-on MUST confirm the chosen reg is free across all `.Ltw_store_from_components_at_a2` callers. Option B (fallback): preserve %d7 across the call (`move.w %d7,-(%sp)` before line 643; `move.w (%sp)+,%d1` at line 645) — +2/+4 bytes → shifts subsequent 0x70xxx addresses + total_genesis_bytes_covered (0x17CAF0) + absolute refs (OPEN-016-class re-relocation), needs invariant pre-authorization. Do NOT touch store/range/offset code (verified correct).

KNOWN_FINDINGS impact: Option C — proposed refinement to KF-028 recording the proven zero-cell root cause (shared FG compose clobbers tile %d7 via `.Ltw_translate_attr`; affects glyph + text-writer hooks; fix = preserve the tile register). STRONG. NOT applied (assess-only; Tighe/Chad Sr. approve).

Floor:
- Files changed: only the design doc + this AGENTS_LOG append: YES
- No source/spec/tool/Makefile/ROM modifications; no build; no runtime probing; no fix written: YES
- Did the MANDATORY glyph-hook-vs-text-writer comparison BEFORE assigning the F-class; did not assume the attr-clobber — proved it from disasm+source: YES
- Did NOT reopen producer reachability / range acceptance / staging base / commit placement; single compose path + caller only: YES
- Address spaces labeled; KNOWN_FINDINGS.md / OPEN_ISSUES.md not modified; no issues closed: YES
- STOP triggered: NO

## [Cody — Analysis, FG Cell-Composition Fix Liveness Audit — STOP, Option B Proposed]

* files changed: `docs/design/Cody_build_0092_fg_cell_composition_fix.md` (new), `AGENTS_LOG.md` (this append)
* build produced: NO
* ROM path: N/A
* root cause confirmed: YES (F4, pre-proven)
* fix implemented: NO (NOT-SAFE; Option B proposed, awaiting explicit approval)
* no unrelated changes: YES

Phase 0 classification: EXTENDING (KF-028 / OPEN-016). Relevant priors loaded: KF-004, KF-006, KF-010, KF-011, KF-013, KF-028. Open issues touched: OPEN-016, OPEN-001, OPEN-015. Contradiction detected: NO. Architecture compliance: CONFIRMED.

Phase A liveness verdict: NOT-SAFE. No byte-neutral replacement scratch register for `.Ltw_translate_attr` is proven safe across the complete caller set.

Caller inventory:
- Direct `.Ltw_translate_attr` callers: `genesistan_hook_text_writer_3c4d2` at `tilemap_hooks.s:515`, `549`, `563`; shared compose at `tilemap_hooks.s:643`.
- `.Ltw_compose_d1_from_d0_d2` caller: `.Ltw_store_from_components_at_a2` at `tilemap_hooks.s:630`.
- `.Ltw_store_from_components_at_a2` callers include `.Ltw_write_pair_same`, `genesistan_hook_glyph_renderer_3bd48.Lgr_store_cell`, `genesistan_hook_text_writer_3c950`, `genesistan_hook_number_renderer_3c2e2`, and text writer paths `_3c586`, `_3c636`, `_3c6dc`, `_3c75c`, `_3c830`.
- `.Ltw_write_pair_same` callers include text writers `_3c550`, `_3c586`, `_3c636`, `_3c6dc`, `_3c75c`, `_3c7a4`, `_3c830`.

Candidate table:
- `%d0`: NOT-SAFE — direct `3c4d2` callers need `%d0` after attr translation as tile/LUT result.
- `%d1`: NOT-SAFE — attr-index accumulator inside `.Ltw_translate_attr`.
- `%d2`: NOT-SAFE — raw attr input and attr-LUT output.
- `%d3`: NOT-SAFE — direct `3c4d2` slow path tests `%d3` after attr call (`cmpi.b #0x50,%d3`), and shared callers use it as loop/state.
- `%d4`: NOT-SAFE — direct `3c4d2` slow path uses `%d4` after attr calls (`cmpi.w #4,%d4`, `addq.w #1,%d4`, `cmpi.w #5,%d4`), and shared callers use it as offset/counter.
- `%d5`: NOT-SAFE — row state for `.Ltw_store_cell`.
- `%d6`: NOT-SAFE — column state for `.Ltw_store_cell`.
- `%d7`: NOT-SAFE — tile-carrying register; current clobber is the defect.

Inner/outer safety summary: `%d3/%d4` do not appear inside runtime range `0x70752..0x7081E`, but the helper does not save them. Direct `genesistan_hook_text_writer_3c4d2` slow-path callers and several shared-helper caller loops keep `%d3/%d4` live across the call, so a pure register rename would leak the clobber to callers.

Option A implementation: NOT performed. Source/spec/tool/Makefile/ROM/invariant files were not modified. No build was run.

Option B proposed shape (separate approval required): preserve `%d7` across the attr call in `.Ltw_compose_d1_from_d0_d2` by inserting `move.w %d7,-(%sp)` before `bsr .Ltw_translate_attr`, and replacing `move.w %d7,%d1` with `move.w (%sp)+,%d1`. This preserves the tile value without requiring a globally free scratch register.

Projected Option B invariant impact:
- Expected byte delta: `+2` bytes.
- Current checked-in gate values: opcode_replace count `95`, total_genesis_bytes_covered `0x17CB58`.
- Projected current-source-of-truth coverage after Option B: `0x17CB5A`; opcode_replace count remains `95`.
- Prompt's `0x17CAF0` value is stale relative to current gates; same `+2` delta would be `0x17CAF2` only if using that stale textual baseline.
- `address_map.json` would regenerate; helper addresses after the insertion point in the `0x70xxx` region would shift by `+2`, so absolute references into shifted helpers require re-relocation verification.

KNOWN_FINDINGS impact: Option A — No new finding to index. F4 is already proven; this task produced a liveness-gate STOP and proposed Option B only.

Open/Closed Issues Impact:
- Open issues touched: OPEN-016 (active; byte-neutral F4 fix blocked, Option B proposed), OPEN-001 (active graphics context), OPEN-015 (context only)
- Closed issues touched: NONE
- New issues opened: NONE
- Issues closed: NONE
- Issues intentionally deferred: Start/C/A crash, OPEN-015 crash-handler defects, BG producer path, `0x3ACEA`, sprites/palette/scroll/general graphics, broader unhooked-writer survey, Option B implementation

Gate-metric definition: `total_genesis_bytes_covered` is the sum of finalized address-map segment sizes and must equal final ROM length; in `rastan-direct`, the native helper area is represented as one `genesis_only` wrapper segment from `wrapper_start` to `len(rom_bytes)`, not as per-symbol helper spans (`postpatch_startup_rom.py:643-683`, `1958-1974`, `1979-2006`). Therefore native-helper instruction growth only affects this metric if final ROM length changes.

Constant correction:
- `CANONICAL_TOTAL_GENESIS_BYTES_COVERED` reverted from `0x17CB5A` to `0x17CB58` in both canonical gate files.
- `CANONICAL_OPCODE_REPLACE_COUNT` stayed `95`.

Build command run exactly once:
`source tools/setup_env.sh && make -C apps/rastan-direct release`

- invariant result: `total_genesis_bytes_covered=0x17CB58`, `opcode_replace patched_site count=95`

Phase 0 classification: EXTENDING. Relevant priors loaded: KF-028, KF-013, KF-011, KF-010, KF-004, KF-006, and KF-001 as context. Open issues touched: OPEN-001 active, OPEN-016 context, OPEN-015 do-not-touch. Contradiction detected: NO. Architecture compliance: CONFIRMED.

Runtime evidence:
- Trace directory: `states/traces/build_0094_attract_text_clear_lifecycle_20260622_231814/`
- MAME/debugger workflow used as read-only runtime observation; no ROM instrumentation and no build.
- Input script pulsed P1 A at frames 520-522 and held P1 Start at frames 640-700.
- `0x0003ACAE` title text producer executed once at frame 211.
- `0x0003ACB6` first render call executed once at frame 211 with `d0=0x11`.
- `0x00070794` FG staging store executed 401 times, 313 with nonzero cell values, and wrote through `%a6=0x00FF501A` (`staged_fg_buffer`).
- VBlank FG commit executed each frame and committed 26 dirty FG rows; commit clears dirty flags only and does not clear staging.
- `0x000563B6` clear caller, `0x000710D8` cwindow clear entry, and `0x00071130` clear done were not observed.
- The trace reached crash-handler halt at frame 699; gameplay exception cause was not investigated.

VDP-vs-WRAM clear finding:
- Static source/disassembly proves `genesistan_hook_cwindow_clear` clears both WRAM staging buffers (`staged_bg_buffer` and `staged_fg_buffer`) and marks dirty rows when reached.
- The known clear helper is not a VDP-only clear and not wrong-range for BG/FG staging.
- In the observed title/attract transition, no clear helper ran before text was produced and committed into persistent staging.

Classification: E - no arcade clear exists at the title/attract text transition; the correct fix target is a translation-layer WRAM staging clear at the semantic transition point. A is partially true for the observed path, but E is the tighter classification because the missing clear is a Genesis staging lifecycle obligation with no direct arcade staging-buffer equivalent. B/C are not supported for the known clear helper; D is not proven because no clear was observed before later producer/staging replay in this run.

Two-transition comparison:
- Title/attract entry (`0x3ACAE -> 0x3ACB6`): producer/render observed, FG staging stores observed, no clear observed.
- Input-driven game-start attempt: A/Start input sent; trace halted before `0x563B6`/`0x710D8`; no clear observed before halt. Tighe's user-visual game-start clear/redraw source remains unidentified by this run. Crash analysis intentionally deferred.

Recommended next fix target: prove the exact title/attract semantic boundary where prior text should stop existing, then add a production translation-layer WRAM tilemap staging clear and dirty-row marking there. Candidate boundary starts with the observed one-shot title/attract producer entry path at `runtime_genesis_pc 0x0003ACAE`; follow-on attract producer `0x0003AD08` needs separate observation before inclusion. Do not clear per frame, do not bypass producers, and do not modify gameplay exception handling.

BG relevance: possible, because the clear helper clears both BG and FG staging when reached; BG/logo/artwork producer correctness was not investigated in this task.

- Attempt 2 timing: no P1 A and no P1 Start, long no-input attract/title window interrupted after 2042 VBlank entries. Result: `0x3AD08`, `0x563B6`, `0x710D8`, and `0x71130` all 0 hits; crash halt 0 hits.
- Attempt 3 timing: P1 A frames 520-525; P1 Start frames 1800-1880. Result: `0x3AD08`, `0x563B6`, `0x710D8`, and `0x71130` all 0 hits; crash halt 1 hit.
- Common observed boundary: `0x0003ACAE` and first render `0x0003ACB6` each executed once; `0x0003ACF8` advanced `%a5@(4)` from 1 to 2.

Boundary observations:
- Follow-on attract producer `runtime_genesis_pc 0x0003AD08` was not observed in a long no-input window with 2042 VBlank entries.
- Game-start clear path `runtime_genesis_pc 0x000563B6 -> 0x000710D8 -> 0x00071130` was not observed under the tuned game-start timings tried.
- Post-clear WRAM staging contents were not observed because no clear event occurred.
- The B-vs-D-vs-already-cleared discriminator for the user-visual game-start clear/redraw could not be measured because no post-clear runtime interval was captured.

Classification: NOT-REPRODUCED for the game-start redraw. The user-visual observation remains labeled user-visual only: near game start, the screen visibly clears, then stale attract/title text redraws for 1-2 frames before exception. This task did not reproduce that clear path as runtime events and therefore does not classify it as SAME-E or SEPARATE-D.

Proven boundary set: `runtime_genesis_pc 0x0003ACAE` only. `0x0003AD08` and the game-start clear path are not proven implementation boundaries from this evidence.

Recommended implementation target: if proceeding to implementation, target only the proven title/attract producer boundary at `runtime_genesis_pc 0x0003ACAE` for a production translation-layer WRAM tilemap staging clear plus dirty-row marking. Do not include `0x3AD08` or a separate game-start boundary without further runtime proof. Do not clear per frame, bypass arcade producers, or use the gameplay exception path as placement evidence.
